Title: Analyst, Measurement & Attribution
Description:
Description & Requirements

Electronic Arts creates next-level entertainment experiences that inspire players and fans around the world. Here, everyone is part of the story. Part of a community that connects across the globe. A place where creativity thrives, new perspectives are invited, and ideas matter. A team where everyone makes play happen.

Analyst, Measurement & Attribution

Workplace Type: Hybrid (Vancouver, BC or Austin, TX)

Reporting To: Director, Franchise Analytics

Studio / Department

The Office of the CXO drives effectiveness across the EA Experiences organization with a focus on excellence in business operations, fan intelligence, and technology solutions. We connect teams with the data, insights, and tools needed to accelerate outcomes.

Impact

You will support marketing investment decisions by translating modeling outputs into clear insights. You will connect data across channels to inform budget allocation and improve performance.

Responsibilities


Run marketing mix modeling analyses to support budget decisions
Deliver insights and validate outputs with business teams
Combine modeling outputs with first-party and channel data
Partner with media teams to support ROI target setting
Support experimentation roadmap to improve model accuracy
Contribute to insight storytelling for marketing strategy


Required Qualifications


Bachelor’s degree in STEM, economics, or related field
2+ years experience in analytics or marketing measurement
Experience querying large datasets using SQL
Knowledge of statistics, hypothesis testing, and regression
Experience using R or Python for analysis


Core Skills And Experience


Exposure to marketing mix modeling or attribution
Experience working with campaign or media data
Data visualization and reporting
Experience supporting cross-functional analytics work
Familiarity with experimentation methods


Pay Transparency - North America

Compensation And Benefits

The ranges listed below are what EA in good faith expects to pay applicants for this role in these locations at the time of this posting. If you reside in a different location, a recruiter will advise on the applicable range and benefits. Pay offered will be determined based on a number of relevant business and candidate factors (e.g. education, qualifications, certifications, experience, skills, geographic location, or business needs).

PAY RANGES


British Columbia (depending on location e.g. Vancouver vs. Victoria) *$77,700 - $107,900 CAD


Pay is just one part of the overall compensation at EA.

For Canada, we offer a package of benefits including vacation (3 weeks per year to start), 10 days per year of sick time, paid top-up to EI/QPIP benefits up to 100% of base salary when you welcome a new child (12 weeks for maternity, and 4 weeks for parental/adoption leave), extended health/dental/vision coverage, life insurance, disability insurance, retirement plan to regular full-time employees. Certain roles may also be eligible for bonus and equity.

About Electronic Arts

We’re proud to have an extensive portfolio of games and experiences, locations around the world, and opportunities across EA. We value adaptability, resilience, creativity, and curiosity. From leadership that brings out your potential, to creating space for learning and experimenting, we empower you to do great work and pursue opportunities for growth.

We adopt a holistic approach to our benefits programs, emphasizing physical, emotional, financial, career, and community wellness to support a balanced life. Our packages are tailored to meet local needs and may include healthcare coverage, mental well-being support, retirement savings, paid time off, family leaves, complimentary games, and more. We nurture environments where our teams can always bring their best to what they do.

Electronic Arts is an equal opportunity employer. All employment decisions are made without regard to race, color, national origin, ancestry, sex, gender, gender identity or expression, sexual orientation, age, genetic information, religion, disability, medical condition, pregnancy, marital status, family status, veteran status, or any other characteristic protected by law. We will also consider employment qualified applicants with criminal records in accordance with applicable law. EA also makes workplace accommodations for qualified individuals with disabilities as required by applicable law.

Title: Data & Automation Analyst
Description:
Data & Automation Analyst (on-site, Calgary)




POSITION SUMMARY:

The Data & Automation Analyst plays a key role in delivering data-driven solutions by combining automation, data engineering, analytics, and emerging AI capabilities. This position focuses on translating business needs into scalable data and automation solutions while supporting the growth of a high-performing analytics function.




KEY RESPONSIBILITIES:




1. Automation & Digital Solutions

• Design, develop, and implement automated workflows to improve operational efficiency

• Leverage modern automation tools and low-code platforms to streamline business processes

• Support AI and advanced analytics initiatives, including use case development and implementation

• Identify and deliver opportunities for process optimization and digital transformation




2. Data Engineering & Management

• Extract, transform, and load (ETL) data from multiple structured and unstructured sources

• Ensure data quality, integrity, and consistency across systems

• Develop and maintain scalable data models to support analytics and reporting

• Collaborate on data architecture and integration across enterprise platforms




3. Analytics & Business Intelligence

• Design and develop interactive dashboards and reporting solutions using modern BI tools

• Translate business requirements into actionable insights and analytical frameworks

• Deliver data-driven recommendations that support operational and strategic decision-making

• Connect analytics outputs to measurable business outcomes




4. Solution Design & Delivery

• Conduct requirements gathering and analysis with stakeholders

• Design end-to-end data, reporting, and automation solutions

• Translate complex and ambiguous business problems into structured, scalable data solutions




5. Team Support & Mentorship

• Provide guidance and mentorship to junior analysts

• Review deliverables to ensure quality and consistency

• Contribute to the development of analytics, automation, and AI capabilities within the team




6. Stakeholder Engagement

• Partner with cross-functional teams to deliver data and automation solutions

• Communicate technical concepts and insights effectively to non-technical audiences

• Act as a trusted advisor for data-driven initiatives




7. Training & Enablement

• Deliver knowledge sharing sessions and training on analytics tools and best practices

• Support adoption of data, automation, and reporting solutions across the organization




ROLE REQUIREMENTS (Technical Emphasis)

• Strong experience with data analytics, automation, and business intelligence solutions

• Hands-on expertise with Microsoft Power Platform, including Power BI and Power Automate

• Strong proficiency in SQL for data extraction, transformation, and analysis

• Experience with data modeling, ETL processes, and data integration across multiple systems

• Familiarity with Microsoft 365 ecosystem and integration with data and automation tools

• Exposure to Azure data services or cloud-based data platforms is considered an asset

• Experience building interactive dashboards and reporting solutions

• Working knowledge of automation tools and low-code/no-code platforms

• Exposure to AI and machine learning concepts, including practical application in analytics workflows

• Strong understanding of data governance, quality, and reporting best practices

• Ability to translate business requirements into technical solutions and analytical outputs

• Strong problem-solving, critical thinking, and analytical skills

• Excellent communication skills with the ability to engage both technical and business stakeholders

• Demonstrated ability to work in fast-paced, collaborative environments




REQUIRED EXPERIENCE / EDUCATION

• Post-secondary education in data analytics, computer science, information systems, or a related field, or equivalent experience

• 2+ years of experience in data analytics, business intelligence, or data engineering roles

• Experience mentoring or supporting junior team members is considered an asset




COMPENSATION

• Salary range: $75,000 – $85,000

Title: Demand Planning Analyst
Place: St John’s, Newfoundland and Labrador, Canada
Description:
Location: Canada (Remote or In-Person)

About The Role

We are seeking a highly analytical and organized Demand Planning Analyst to lead our forecasting plans, improve supply confidence, and drive cross-functional alignment across brands and sales channels.

This role is responsible for developing and maintaining demand forecasts using historical sales data, market trends, promotional activity, and strategic business insights. The Demand Planner will partner closely with Sales, Marketing, Operations, Finance, Product Development, and Procurement to support business planning, budgeting, new product launches, and sourcing initiatives. This individual will proactively monitor forecast performance, inventory health, and production schedules, communicate risks and opportunities to leadership, and continuously improve planning tools, reporting, and ERP data integrity.

Responsibilities


Develop and maintain accurate demand forecasts across brands and sales channels using historical sales data, market trends, promotional activity, and strategic insights.
Partner cross-functionally with sales, marketing, and operations to align forecasts with business plans and customer demand.
Work closely with Finance to support the Planning and Budgeting rollup.
Assist Product Development team during new product launches by determining opening order forecasts and sales channel allocation.
Monitor forecast accuracy, inventory health, and production schedules to identify stockout risks proactively.
Steward forecast performance to leadership by highlighting trends, insights, and risks and translate into action plans to improve operations.
Support Procurement initiatives by providing analyses for ad-hoc sourcing proposals, awards, and commitments.
Maintain data integrity in ERP and planning systems, improve forecasting tools, reporting, and processes through ongoing continuous improvement.


What We’re Looking For


Minimum 3 years’ experience in supply/demand planning and forecasting, ideally in a fast-moving CPG industry.
Direct experience with developing and stewarding forecasting models that manage more than $1M of monthly sales
Bachelor’s degree in Business, Economics, Data Science, or a related field.
Strong analytical and quantitative skills, with experience in data visualization.
Advanced knowledge in Excel (nested formulas, fuzzy lookups, PowerPivot/Query)
Experience using Python, R, Power BI, or SQL is preferred, but not required.
Strong communication skills, both verbally and written.
Previous experience supporting multiple stakeholders.
Familiarity with ERP systems.


Who Thrives Here


People who take initiative, spot problems early, and bring solutions before being asked.
Detail-oriented, highly organized, and comfortable managing multiple priorities simultaneously.
Thrive in a test-and-learn environment where experimentation and iteration are core to success.
People who are data-driven and are confident in their thinking but can adapt and adjust their thinking in the presence of new information.


What We Offer

Location: Canada (Remote or In-Person)

About The Role

We are seeking a highly analytical and organized Demand Planning Analyst to lead our forecasting plans, improve supply confidence, and drive cross-functional alignment across brands and sales channels.

This role is responsible for developing and maintaining demand forecasts using historical sales data, market trends, promotional activity, and strategic business insights. The Demand Planner will partner closely with Sales, Marketing, Operations, Finance, Product Development, and Procurement to support business planning, budgeting, new product launches, and sourcing initiatives. This individual will proactively monitor forecast performance, inventory health, and production schedules, communicate risks and opportunities to leadership, and continuously improve planning tools, reporting, and ERP data integrity.

Responsibilities

  • Develop and maintain accurate demand forecasts across brands and sales channels using historical sales data, market trends, promotional activity, and strategic insights.
  • Partner cross-functionally with sales, marketing, and operations to align forecasts with business plans and customer demand.
  • Work closely with Finance to support the Planning and Budgeting rollup.
  • Assist Product Development team during new product launches by determining opening order forecasts and sales channel allocation.
  • Monitor forecast accuracy, inventory health, and production schedules to identify stockout risks proactively.
  • Steward forecast performance to leadership by highlighting trends, insights, and risks and translate into action plans to improve operations.
  • Support Procurement initiatives by providing analyses for ad-hoc sourcing proposals, awards, and commitments.
  • Maintain data integrity in ERP and planning systems, improve forecasting tools, reporting, and processes through ongoing continuous improvement.

What We’re Looking For

  • Minimum 3 years’ experience in supply/demand planning and forecasting, ideally in a fast-moving CPG industry.
  • Direct experience with developing and stewarding forecasting models that manage more than $1M of monthly sales
  • Bachelor’s degree in Business, Economics, Data Science, or a related field.
  • Strong analytical and quantitative skills, with experience in data visualization.
  • Advanced knowledge in Excel (nested formulas, fuzzy lookups, PowerPivot/Query)
  • Experience using Python, R, Power BI, or SQL is preferred, but not required.
  • Strong communication skills, both verbally and written.
  • Previous experience supporting multiple stakeholders.
  • Familiarity with ERP systems.

Who Thrives Here

  • People who take initiative, spot problems early, and bring solutions before being asked.
  • Detail-oriented, highly organized, and comfortable managing multiple priorities simultaneously.
  • Thrive in a test-and-learn environment where experimentation and iteration are core to success.
  • People who are data-driven and are confident in their thinking but can adapt and adjust their thinking in the presence of new information.

Title: Billing Analyst II
Description:
Description

Join our team

TELUS Health Care Centre (Care Centres) is an occupational health clinic. We are Canada’s leading provider of corporate health services, delivering integrated health management programs nationwide. With a team of highly skilled professionals, Care Centres provides clinical and remote medical services across Canada. Offering a full range of occupational health services from pre-employment screenings, to rehabilitative services for the ill or injured worker, to complete on-site clinic management in some of the most remote and harsh areas of the country, Care Centres is the chosen partner for proactive health management programs.

Here's the impact you’ll make and what we’ll accomplish together

The Billing Analyst II is a key part of the TELUS Health Occupational Health team as they are responsible for a variety of duties that generate billing processes and client reporting.

Here’s How

Duties & responsibilities include (but not limited to):



Interpret data, analyze results using effective and efficient techniques and provide ongoing reports
Acquire data from primary or secondary data sources and maintain databases/data systems
Identify, analyze, and interpret trends or patterns in complex data sets
Filter and “clean” data by reviewing reports, and performance indicators to locate and correct problems
Work with management to prioritize business and information needs
Locate and define new process improvement opportunities



Qualifications

You're the missing piece of the puzzle



Proven working experience as a Data Analyst or Business Data Analyst
Technical expertise regarding data models, database design development, and data mining techniques
Understanding of data confidentiality protocols (Personal Health Information, Personal Information , Privacy Health Information Act) and experience handling high-stakes, sensitive information
Strong knowledge of and experience with reporting packages and databases
Knowledge of and experience analyzing datasets (Excel, XML, etc)
Strong analytical skills with the ability to collect, organize, analyze, and disseminate significant amounts of information with attention to detail and accuracy
Adept at queries, formulas, macros, report writing and presenting findings
Experience working with Cority is an asset
Experience working with billing and finance teams is an asset



Who is TELUS Health Care Centres?

We are a national network of renowned medical professionals dedicated to identifying health risks, inspiring positive lifestyle choices and improving patient outcomes. We have one of the largest networks of Care Centres in Canada



Our commitment: Better healthcare options for everyone. We are leveraging the power of technology to provide Canadians with better health outcomes. Connecting health teams, streamlining workflows, and empowering patients to manage their health are just some of the ways our digital solutions promote collaboration and efficiency
We are looking for enthusiastic, customer service minded individuals who want to work in a collaborative, friendly and supportive environment. Join our team and help us shape the future of how Canadians engage with the healthcare ecosystem



Accessibility



THCC is proud to foster an inclusive culture that embraces diversity. We are committed to fair employment practices and all qualified applicants will receive consideration for employment. We offer accommodation for applicants with disabilities, as required, during the recruitment process. Please let the recruitment team know of any accommodation requirement at the beginning of the process
Please note that we are exclusively recruiting people who can work legally in Canada

Title: Systems Analyst II - Data Analysis and Migration
Description:
Description

Join our team

Do you thrive in a fast-paced, team centric environment? Are you a detailed-oriented individual who loves solving problems by leveraging creative solutions? Do you want to be in a space where your knowledge and experience are used and fostered? Read on as this is the job for you!

As a key member of the TELUS Health team, you will be joining a growing team of Analysts in transforming and converting health data for Canadian physicians on a TELUS EMR (Electronic Medical Record) platform. This exciting opportunity will allow you to leverage your experience and skillset within an innovative and collaborative environment while performing meaningful work that is crucial to the success of our TELUS Health EMR platforms.

Here’s How

As part of the team, you’ll be responsible for:



Extracting data from various source systems and data stores by performing light business coding (ex. Java, Python, C#, etc.)
Connecting with our customers to conduct requirement gathering and performing data validations
Mapping and transforming data using various techniques (ex. XSLT, MapForce, scripting, etc.) based on customer input and requirements
Performing ad-hoc queries (ex. SQL, LINQ, etc.) and reports to analyse customer data before and after migration
Planning and performing go-live transformation and validation to ensure no data is lost or corrupted
Executing production data migrations (requires occasional evening/weekend work)
Collaboration with various teams across the business to identify roadblocks and resolve issues, to ensure timely completion for each project
Contributing to on-going development and refinement of data migration tools and processes
Tracking issues, communicating project status, and managing project risk



What You Bring

Required Experience



Graduation from a post-secondary institution with a Diploma specializing in related technologies
1 year minimum experience working on data transformation, development, or school related projects



Technical Knowledge



SQL/TSQL
Data Transformation Scripting and Mapping (ex. XML, XSL/XPath)
Familiarity with databases such as MSSQL, Oracle, MySQL, etc.
Scripting (ex. Python/Groovy) or development (ex. Java/C#)
Linux/Unix and Groovy an asset
MapForce an asset



Functional Skills



Detail oriented
Avid problem solver
Ability to communicate both verbally and written in English, French is an asset
Ability to multi-task and manage numerous concurrent projects
Strong analysis and problem solving skills with the ability to identify root cause
Ability to communicate in an effective and respectful manner with customers and fellow team members
Self-directed in completing project-based work



Great to Have



Agile & Project Management skills
Understanding of Network Infrastructure
Electronic Medical Records (EMR), Medical or clinical workflow experience
Experience with HL7, CoPD and CDS/CMS XML standards
Experience with the Atlassian tool suite (ex. Jira, Bitbucket, Confluence)

Title: Analytics Data Engineer
Description:
✅ Analytics Data Engineer

📍 Waterloo, ON (4 days onsite)

📅 12‑month contract (extendable)

📌 T4 employment - $60 - 75/HR

Overview

This analytics data team is responsible for transforming large‑scale operational and telemetry‑style data into trusted, actionable insights that directly influence engineering decisions, product strategy, customer experience, and executive direction.

This role sits at the intersection of analytics, data engineering, and business strategy . You will own the end‑to‑end lifecycle of data - from validating and understanding raw data sources, to structuring and analyzing datasets, to delivering insights that stakeholders can confidently act on.

You will work closely with engineering, product, and business teams to ensure data accurately reflects real‑world behavior and supports meaningful decision‑making. Success in this role comes from the ability to work through ambiguity, validate data rigorously, and translate technical findings into clear business outcomes .

This is not a purely pipeline‑driven role. The team is looking for someone who understands what the data means and how it drives decisions , not just how it is built.

Key Responsibilities

🔹 Analytics, Data Quality & Insight Delivery (~60%)


Analyze large, complex datasets to uncover trends, patterns, and key performance drivers
Validate and ensure data quality and integrity across the full analytical lifecycle
Translate loosely defined business problems into structured analytical approaches
Apply analytical and statistical techniques (e.g., trend analysis, variation analysis, basic hypothesis testing)
Deliver insights through clear visualizations, dashboards, and stakeholder-ready narratives
Partner with stakeholders to ensure data reflects real-world business and product context



🔹 Data Engineering & Pipeline Development (~30%)


Design, build, and maintain ETL/ELT pipelines across structured and semi-structured data sources
Work with APIs and large-scale data ingestion processes
Transform raw data into clean, analytics-ready datasets
Contribute to cloud-based data workflows (GCP preferred; AWS/Azure experience transferable)
Support scalable and maintainable data architecture for long-term analytics



🔹 Collaboration & Data Enablement (~10%)


Partner with cross-functional teams to gather requirements and deliver solutions
Maintain shared data models, documentation, and analytics frameworks
Ensure data solutions are reliable, reusable, and scalable
Communicate findings effectively to both technical and non-technical audiences



Required Qualifications


5+ years of experience working with SQL and Python for data analysis and transformation
Strong experience working with large and complex datasets, including data validation and troubleshooting
Proven ability to translate business questions into analytical solutions and insights
Experience building or supporting data pipelines / ETL workflows
Strong understanding of data quality, reconciliation, and root cause analysis
Experience working directly with stakeholders and delivering insights
Exposure to cloud data platforms (GCP preferred; AWS/Azure also acceptable)
Experience with data visualization tools (Power BI, Tableau, Looker, etc.)



Nice to Have


Experience with statistical modeling, experimentation, or predictive analysis
Experience working with high-volume or event-based datasets (e.g., telemetry, IoT)
Exposure to GCP tools such as BigQuery, Dataflow, or dbt



Work Environment


Hybrid model: 4 days onsite in Waterloo
Fast-paced, highly collaborative team working closely with business stakeholders



✅ What Success Looks Like


You understand not just how data is built, but what it represents and how it drives decisions
You ensure data is accurate, trusted, and validated before use
You can take ambiguous problems and turn them into clear analytical outcomes
You communicate complex findings in a way that enables real business action



Job Type & Location

This is a Contract position based out of Waterloo, ON.

Pay And Benefits

The pay range for this position is $70.00 - $80.00/hr.

Workplace Type

This is a hybrid position in Waterloo,ON.

Title: Intermediate Mining Data Analyst
Description:
Be part of something incredible. Metals are necessary for our world, but mining historically faced issues with waste, sustainability, and inefficiencies. MineSense is changing that. Our solutions reduce mining waste and environmental impacts while increasing profits and efficiencies. Backed by the best in venture capital and government funds, and recognized by tech industry and sustainability programs, MineSense is doing what many said could not be done: improving mining for people and planet.







We are looking to add an Intermediate Mining Data Analyst to our GeoScience team. The role will report to the Manager, GeoScience and be based out of the Vancouver office. The ideal candidate will have a strong interest in detailed data analysis and statistics within the mining industry. You will work with clients globally, to incorporate MineSense solutions into their ore control processes and show them the value of using our systems. If you enjoy working with data, collaborating with multidisciplinary groups, and have a mining, geology, or geophysics background, we want to hear from you!













Job Responsibilities:




Helping new and existing clients leverage MineSense technology in their grade control engineering processes, which includes developing client facing reports and presentations.
Collaborating with internal teams to develop prediction models by correlating mine grade measurements or estimates with our sensor results to identify patterns, trends or other data features that relate to grade, texture, mineralogy, or lithology.
Working with the Field Service and Product Development team to further enhance our products and services through system data analysis and data quality assurance/control.
Determining and tracking data model accuracy and precision through comparison with sampled assay grades or grade estimates.
Reporting and presenting data results/trends to internal teams, as well as external clients.

Building and maintaining good working relationships with our clients.
Promotes a positive company culture through sharing of information and supporting the learning and development of all colleagues.
Additional duties as occasionally required by your manager.




Qualifications:

Degree in Earth Sciences (Geology, Geophysics), Geostatistics, Data Analytics, Mining Engineering or related field.
Minimum of 2-4 years of relevant working experience, where experience in mining is preferred.
Experience performing statistical analysis or data science in a work setting is preferred.
Mining experience with in-grade control or working with ore control models is preferred.
Experience with data analysis tools such as python or other statistical packages.
Experience with SQL is preferred.
Experience and passion for solving analytical problems involving diverse data sets using quantitative approaches to generate insights.
Attention to detail and an interest in problem solving.
Oral and written proficiency in English is required.
Additional oral and written proficiency in Spanish is strongly preferred.
The ability to travel and support global offices is required (10-30%).
Legally able to work in Canada.




The salary offered to the successful candidate will be based on their experience, knowledge and skills they bring to MineSense, market data based on their location as well as internal equity. Keeping this mind, the salary offered can be outside of the range posted.




Our total rewards package offers market-researched benefits, promotes internal equity, and supports team members at all levels of our organization, and includes the following:




Our total rewards package includes:

· A competitive salary and bonus.

· Matching RRSP or 401K program (for Canadian and US employees)

· A comprehensive benefit program including an employee assistance program.

· Learning and career development programs and resources (live sessions and on-demand)

· Transit Subsidy (for roles based in Vancouver, BC)

When you join MineSense, a trailblazing Canadian mining technology company, you become a part of our purpose to maximize global metals and minimize global impacts. Title: Data Analyst
Description:
At CI, we see a great place to work as one that is a safe place for everyone to have a voice, where people are empowered to take ownership over meaningful work, where there is an opportunity to grow through stretching themselves, where they can work on innovative products and projects, and where employees are supported and engaged in doing so.

We are seeking experienced Data Analysts to support a large-scale data migration and integration initiative. You will be responsible for investigating existing data structures, analyzing business logic hidden in queries and reports, mapping source data to target systems, and collaborating with business stakeholders to ensure accuracy and completeness.

This role is highly investigative and requires a blend of technical analysis and business communication skills. You should be comfortable navigating undocumented environments, reverse-engineering logic, and documenting findings clearly for both technical and business audiences.

Key Responsibilities


Source System Analysis
Investigate source systems and databases to understand data structures, relationships, and logic.
Analyze existing SQL queries, reports, and stored procedures to reverse engineer business rules.
Data Mapping & Transformation
Map source fields to target model requirements.
Identify gaps, data quality issues, and transformation needs.
Work closely with data engineers to validate ETL/transformation rules.
Stakeholder Collaboration
Partner with business SMEs and stakeholders to clarify definitions, logic, and requirements.
Translate technical findings into clear documentation for non-technical stakeholders.
Documentation & Knowledge Transfer
Produce detailed data dictionaries, mapping documents, and gap analysis reports.
Capture undocumented business logic and processes uncovered during investigation.
Validation & Testing Support
Support data validation and reconciliation efforts during development and UAT.
Assist in resolving discrepancies between source and target systems.

Required Skills & Experience


Strong SQL skills, with ability to read, analyze, and write complex queries.
Proven experience in data migration, data warehousing, or system integration projects.
Background in analyzing large datasets.
Strong data profiling, mapping, and reconciliation skills.
Excellent analytical and problem-solving abilities in ambiguous environments.
Strong communication skills to work with both technical teams and business stakeholders.
Experience creating data dictionaries, mapping documents, and gap analysis reports.
Experience with dashboard tools such as Sigma or equivalent
Experience with code repository systems – Bitbucket or Git


Nice to Have


Experience with Snowflake or other modern cloud data platforms.
Familiarity with ETL/ELT processes and tools.
Exposure to financial services or other regulated industries


This opportunity is for an existing vacancy with the company. The anticipated base salary range for this position is $55,000 - $95,000. Exact salary depends on several factors such as experience, skills, education, and budget. Salary range may vary based on geographic location. In addition to base salary, this position is eligible for participation in a bonus program. In addition, The Company offers a variety of benefits to eligible employees, including health insurance coverage, wellness programs, life and disability insurance, retirement savings plans, paid leave programs, education-related programs, paid holidays and vacation time, and many others. Many of these benefits are subsidized or fully paid for by the company.

This role follows our in‑office work model and requires employees to be onsite four days per week to support collaboration and business needs.

CI Financial is an independent company offering global wealth management and asset management advisory services through diverse financial services firms. Since 1965, we have consistently anticipated and responded to the changing needs of investors. We are driven by a commitment to provide individuals and institutions with the highest-quality investments and advice. Our commitment to the highest levels of performance means that whatever their position, CI employees must be comfortable in a fast-paced environment that will stretch them to tap into their highest potential. Employees with a healthy dose of ambition, a desire to commit to a curious mindset for continuous learning, and a willingness to go the extra mile thrive at CI.

A Supportive Environment for Success

We offer an in-office environment, competitive benefits, and a supportive workplace to help our employees thrive both personally and professionally.

What We Offer


Modern HQ location within walking distance from Union Station
Training Reimbursement
Paid Professional Designations
Employee Savings Plan (ESP)
Corporate Discount Program
Enhanced group benefits
Parental Leave Top–up program
Paid time off for Volunteering


We are focused on building a diverse and inclusive workforce. If you are excited about this role and are not confident you meet all the qualification requirements, we encourage you to apply to investigate the opportunity further.

Please submit your resume in confidence by clicking “Apply”. Only qualified candidates selected for an interview will be contacted. CI Financial Corp. and all of our affiliates (“CI”) are committed to fair and accessible employment practices and provide reasonable accommodations for persons with disabilities. If you require accommodations in order to apply for any job opportunities, require this posting in an additional format, or require accommodation at any stage of the recruitment process please contact us at accessible.recruitment@ci.com, or call 416-364-1145 ext. 4747.

Title: People Data Analyst
Description:
Slalom is a fiercely human business and technology consulting company that leads with outcomes to bring more value, in all ways, always. From strategy through delivery, our agile teams across 52 offices in 12 countries partner with clients to co-create powerful customer experiences, modern ways of working, and meaningful impact.




What sets us apart? We believe work should be challenging and fulfilling, not perfect, but possible. That’s why we prioritize purpose, flexibility, connection, and recognition, so our people can thrive and love what they do, most days.


Job Title:




People Data Analyst


So, what will I do?
Serve as the primary analytics partner for Talent Acquisition while contributing to Slalom's broader People Analytics strategy through direct collaboration with the Global People Team and People Health product organization.
Partner with Talent Acquisition leaders, People Business Partners, Talent and People Experience, Total Rewards, Staffing, Workforce Planning, HR Product teams, and business stakeholders to define reporting requirements, establish key performance indicators, and deliver actionable insights that support strategic workforce decisions.
Support the People Health product hub by translating Talent Acquisition and broader people data into integrated views that enable leaders to understand workforce health, talent effectiveness, organizational trends, and employee outcomes across the talent lifecycle.
Develop executive-level dashboards and reporting solutions that combine recruiting, workforce, and people data to provide a comprehensive view of organizational performance and talent health.
Analyze recruiting funnel performance, sourcing effectiveness, workforce trends, talent movement, retention indicators, and people metrics to identify opportunities for optimization and continuous improvement.
Lead data domain integration efforts by partnering across People systems, data platforms, and business functions to improve data consistency, reporting alignment, and enterprise-wide visibility into people data.
Collaborate with cross-functional teams to integrate, transform, and model data from Avature, HR systems, workforce planning tools, data warehouses, and business intelligence platforms to support scalable analytics solutions.
Establish and promote reporting governance, data quality standards, and analytics best practices to improve trust, consistency, and usability of people data across the organization.
Translate complex datasets into compelling visualizations, narratives, and recommendations that enable leaders to make informed, data-driven decisions regarding talent strategy, workforce planning, and organizational effectiveness.

And, what will I bring?
Skills
5+ years of experience in People Analytics, Talent Acquisition Analytics, Workforce Analytics, Business Intelligence, or a related analytical discipline.
Advanced experience building reporting and dashboard solutions within Avature and leveraging business intelligence tools such as Power BI, Tableau, or similar platforms.
Experience integrating and analyzing data across multiple HR, recruiting, workforce planning, and business systems to deliver enterprise-level insights.
Strong understanding of recruiting, talent, workforce, and people metrics, including the ability to connect data across multiple domains to support strategic decision-making.
Experience supporting data governance, data quality, reporting standardization, and analytics modernization initiatives.

Competencies
Strategic thinker who can connect Talent Acquisition metrics to broader workforce and organizational outcomes.
Strong relationship builder who effectively partners with stakeholders across Talent Acquisition, People Business Partners, Talent and People Experience, Total Rewards, Staffing, Workforce Planning, HR Product, and business leadership teams.
Exceptional analytical and problem-solving skills with the ability to identify patterns, opportunities, and risks within complex datasets.
Effective communicator and storyteller who can translate technical findings into actionable business recommendations.
Continuous improvement mindset with a passion for leveraging data to improve workforce experiences, talent outcomes, and organizational effectiveness.


Hybrid/In Office

We are hybrid, but there are expectations that if your team leader or business requirements require you onsite, you are able to meet those expectations and requirements.




Slalom is committed to fair and equitable compensation practices. For this position, the expected base salary pay range is $110,000 to $140,000. In addition, there is potential for a discretionary bonus based on individual and company performance. Actual compensation will depend upon an individual's skills, experience, qualifications, and other relevant factors.


Slalom Employee Perks
Culture & fun!
Lunch & learns
Health & wellness activities
Holiday parties
Employee community groups
Social clubs & activities
Vacation (20 days prorated based on start date & wellness/sick days)

Tangible Perks
Competitive compensation package
$100 cell phone monthly reimbursement
Registered Retirement Program (RRSP) & Employer Matching
Tax-Free Savings Account (TFSA)
Annual Thrive Well-Being Gift Card
Family Planning/Fertility Benefit
Maternity/Parental Leave Top-Up
Medical & Dental Benefits
Life and AD&D Insurance
Access to Supplementary Life and AD&D Insurance
Access to the Employee Assistance Program (EAP) and Inkblot Therapy
And more!



All benefits are subject to eligibility requirements.

Slalom is an inclusive, equal opportunity employer dedicated to building a diverse workforce. We encourage applications from all qualified candidates and will work to reasonably accommodate applicants' needs throughout all stages of the recruitment and selection process. Please advise the Talent Acquisition team if you require accommodations during the interview process.

Please note that if you are hired at Slalom, you will be required to complete a background check.

Title: Data Analyst, HR
Description:
Who We Are




For more than 130 years, The Salvation Army has served people in need in communities across Canada and Bermuda. Building on our roots as a world-wide Christian church, each year we help more than 2 million people, providing necessities such as food, clothing and shelter. In addition, we support people experiencing unemployment, addiction and family challenges. We continually adapt and innovate to meet emerging needs and live out our mission, vision and values of hope, service, dignity and stewardship. As a faith- and values-based organization, we hire and serve people of all backgrounds and walks of life - there is a place for everyone to belong here.




Mission Statement




The Salvation Army exists to share the love of Jesus Christ, meet human needs and be a transforming influence in the communities of our world.




Job Description:




Position Purpose:




The Data Analyst, HR exists to strengthen HR technology, analytics, and data integrity so that HR systems effectively support organizational goals. The role contributes by improving HR processes, ensuring accurate and reliable data through analysis, integration, and migration, and enabling robust reporting and analytics capabilities. By training and upskilling team members in Workday Reporting and partnering with stakeholders to define business needs, the role helps translate data into actionable insights. Ultimately, it empowers HR leaders to make informed, strategic decisions that enhance workforce planning and people outcomes across the organization.




This role is a 12 - month, temporary, fixed-term contract.




Key Accountabilities:




Elicit business, financial, and operational requirements across headquarters, divisions, ministry units, and frontline teams to ensure HR systems meet organizational needs.
Develop HR analytics capability by training team members on Workday reporting and creating high-quality, custom reports that support decision-making.
Assess HR system integrity through configuration reviews, interface evaluations, and data quality checks to improve reliability and consistency.
Collaborate cross‑functionally to define and recommend system enhancements, integrations, workflows, and release impacts that support digital transformation.
Establish reporting governance standards for Workday, including design, access, data integrity, and compliance, to ensure accurate and consistent reporting.
Consult on system releases and projects by providing insight on downstream impacts, risks, and requirements, and by escalating and resolving system issues as needed.
Prepare and execute testing and release management activities, including test plans, source-to-target data mapping, migration audits, and reconciliation reporting.
Oversee HR data assurance activities such as gap analysis, data extraction and conversion, validation controls, and integration mapping to ensure end‑to‑end data integrity.




Education and Experience Qualifications:




A university degree in computer science or an approved equivalent combination of education and experience.
7–10 years of experience leading and supporting data analysis and migration initiatives for cloud-based/SaaS HR system implementations.
3–5 years of hands-on experience in HR Reporting and Analytics, with a strong emphasis on statistical analysis and programming using R and/or Python.
Delivers advanced analytics and reporting solutions through strong expertise in Workday Reporting and Power BI.
Builds team capability by designing and delivering effective training, knowledge transfer, and practical guidance.
Navigates complex, multi-system and multi-entity environments while contributing to high-priority, high-visibility initiatives.
Collaborates effectively within Agile delivery teams on large-scale projects from analysis through implementation.
Utilizes advanced Microsoft Excel skills, with solid proficiency in Word and PowerPoint to support analysis and communication.
Demonstrates strong analytical, problem-solving, communication, adaptability, and continuous learning capabilities in fast-paced, changing environments.







Compensation:




The target hiring range for this position is $85,321.60 to $106,641.60 with a maximum of $127,982.40. Placement in the salary range will be based on factors such as market conditions, internal equity, and candidate experience, skills, and qualifications relevant to the role.

Who We Are


For more than 130 years, The Salvation Army has served people in need in communities across Canada and Bermuda. Building on our roots as a world-wide Christian church, each year we help more than 2 million people, providing necessities such as food, clothing and shelter. In addition, we support people experiencing unemployment, addiction and family challenges. We continually adapt and innovate to meet emerging needs and live out our mission, vision and values of hope, service, dignity and stewardship. As a faith- and values-based organization, we hire and serve people of all backgrounds and walks of life - there is a place for everyone to belong here.


Mission Statement


The Salvation Army exists to share the love of Jesus Christ, meet human needs and be a transforming influence in the communities of our world.


Job Description:


Position Purpose:


The Data Analyst, HR exists to strengthen HR technology, analytics, and data integrity so that HR systems effectively support organizational goals. The role contributes by improving HR processes, ensuring accurate and reliable data through analysis, integration, and migration, and enabling robust reporting and analytics capabilities. By training and upskilling team members in Workday Reporting and partnering with stakeholders to define business needs, the role helps translate data into actionable insights. Ultimately, it empowers HR leaders to make informed, strategic decisions that enhance workforce planning and people outcomes across the organization.


This role is a 12 - month, temporary, fixed-term contract.


Key Accountabilities:


  • Elicit business, financial, and operational requirements across headquarters, divisions, ministry units, and frontline teams to ensure HR systems meet organizational needs.
  • Develop HR analytics capability by training team members on Workday reporting and creating high-quality, custom reports that support decision-making.
  • Assess HR system integrity through configuration reviews, interface evaluations, and data quality checks to improve reliability and consistency.
  • Collaborate cross‑functionally to define and recommend system enhancements, integrations, workflows, and release impacts that support digital transformation.
  • Establish reporting governance standards for Workday, including design, access, data integrity, and compliance, to ensure accurate and consistent reporting.
  • Consult on system releases and projects by providing insight on downstream impacts, risks, and requirements, and by escalating and resolving system issues as needed.
  • Prepare and execute testing and release management activities, including test plans, source-to-target data mapping, migration audits, and reconciliation reporting.
  • Oversee HR data assurance activities such as gap analysis, data extraction and conversion, validation controls, and integration mapping to ensure end‑to‑end data integrity.


Education and Experience Qualifications:


  • A university degree in computer science or an approved equivalent combination of education and experience.
  • 7–10 years of experience leading and supporting data analysis and migration initiatives for cloud-based/SaaS HR system implementations.
  • 3–5 years of hands-on experience in HR Reporting and Analytics, with a strong emphasis on statistical analysis and programming using R and/or Python.
  • Delivers advanced analytics and reporting solutions through strong expertise in Workday Reporting and Power BI.
  • Builds team capability by designing and delivering effective training, knowledge transfer, and practical guidance.
  • Navigates complex, multi-system and multi-entity environments while contributing to high-priority, high-visibility initiatives.
  • Collaborates effectively within Agile delivery teams on large-scale projects from analysis through implementation.
  • Utilizes advanced Microsoft Excel skills, with solid proficiency in Word and PowerPoint to support analysis and communication.
  • Demonstrates strong analytical, problem-solving, communication, adaptability, and continuous learning capabilities in fast-paced, changing environments.



Compensation:


The target hiring range for this position is $85,321.60 to $106,641.60 with a maximum of $127,982.40. Placement in the salary range will be based on factors such as market conditions, internal equity, and candidate experience, skills, and qualifications relevant to the role.


The Salvation Army will provide reasonable accommodation upon request. Please email Recruitment.Accommodations@salvationarmy.ca if you have a need for any accommodation and we will be pleased to discuss this with you.

Title: Analytics Engineer
Description:
About Dialpad

Dialpad is the AI-native business communications platform. We unify calling, messaging, meetings, and contact center on a single platform - powered by AI that understands every conversation in real time.

More than 70,000 companies around the globe, including WeWork, Asana, NASDAQ, AAA Insurance, COMPASS Realty, Uber, Randstad, and Tractor Supply, rely on Dialpad to build stronger customer connections using real-time, AI-driven insights.

We’re now leading the shift to Agentic AI: intelligent agents that don’t just analyze conversations but take action by automating workflows, resolving customer issues, and accelerating revenue in real time. Our DAART initiative (Dialpad Agentic AI in Real Time) is redefining what a communications platform can do.

Visit dialpad.com to learn more.

Being a Dialer

At Dialpad, AI isn’t just a feature; it’s how our teams do their best work every day. We put powerful AI tools in every employee’s hands so they can move faster, think bigger, and achieve more.

We believe every conversation matters. And we’ve built the platform that turns those conversations into insight and action, for our customers and ourselves.

We look for people who are intensely curious and hold themselves to a high bar. Our ambition is significant, and achieving it requires a team that operates at the highest level. We seek individuals who embody our core traits: Scrappy, Curious, Optimistic, Persistent, and Empathetic.

Your role

As an Analytics Engineer, you will collaborate with business stakeholders across various departments to effectively leverage big data and provide accurate, actionable insights that address key business questions. This role is based in and reports to the Sr Manager, Analytics Engineering in Argentina. The candidate must be available to work during California overlapping hours (9AM to 5PM PST).

Dialpad’s data team acts as a centralized hub, supporting the analytics and business intelligence needs of all global departments such as Finance, Product, Engineering, Customer Support, Marketing, Legal, HR, and Sales. The team is responsible for data ingestion, ensuring data quality, managing data engineering, and delivering business analytics services, including report automation and descriptive and predictive analytics. Dialpad’s data team operates from four global hubs located in the US, Canada, Argentina and India.

What You’ll Do


Work closely with the AI Transformation team and data analysts supporting different departments to build datasets and to deliver analyses that enable AI engineers to develop internal AI products and workflows.
Partner with cross-functional teams to analyze data from multiple sources and generate insights that inform strategic decision-making.
Perform exploratory data analysis, statistical modeling, and hypothesis testing to uncover trends and opportunities.
Develop and deploy predictive machine learning models to enhance product experiences, forecast business outcomes, and support growth initiatives.
Design and maintain a suite of automated, accurate, and reliable dashboards to enable self-service analytics.
Define data model requirements and document business logic to support the Data Engineering team in creating curated datasets for reporting and analysis.
Collaborate with internal and external stakeholders to improve data quality, accessibility, and usability.
Monitor business performance using automated dashboards and ad hoc analysis, delivering actionable insights to leadership.
Work with analysts and engineers across the organization to establish best practices and improve data processes and utilization.
Identify and drive opportunities for process improvement, efficiency gains, and cost optimization.


Skills You’ll Bring


5+ years of advanced data analytics experience using big data.
Experience working directly with business stakeholders or AI Analysts to define requirements, drive analytics, and deliver effective insights.
Experience collaborating with Data Engineers, Business Analysts, and Team Leads, with a demonstrated track record of successfully working with offshore teams and delivering complex projects on time.
Advanced SQL development skills for data exploration and analysis.
Strong analytical and problem-solving skills.
Experience building effective visualizations and dashboard automations in an advanced BI tool like Tableau (preferred), PowerBI, DOMO or Looker.
Bachelor’s degree in a technical or business field.
Expertise in product and financial data analysis.
Experience working in a Saas based technology company.
Experience in building and training machine learning models for business analysis use cases.
Familiarity with data modeling best practices and experience working with tools such as dbt.
Proficiency in Python for data exploration and analysis.
Hands-on experience with Agile Software Development methodologies and tools like Jira.
Working experience in cloud-based data platforms like GCP, AWS or Azure.


For exceptional talent based in Ontario, Canada the target base salary range for this position is posted below. Our salary ranges are determined by role, level, and location. The range displayed on each job posting reflects the target range for new hire salaries for the position. Within the range, individual pay is determined by work location and additional factors, including job-related skills, experience, and relevant education or training. Your recruiter can share more about the specific salary range for your preferred location during the hiring process. Please note that the compensation details listed in Ontario role postings reflect the base salary only, and do not include bonus, equity, or benefits.

Ontario Pay Transparency Range

$86,000—$108,500 CAD

Why Join Dialpad


Work at the center of the AI transformation in business communications
Build and ship agentic AI products that are redefining how companies operate
Join a team where AI amplifies every employee’s impact
Competitive salary, comprehensive benefits, and real opportunities for growth


We believe in investing in our people. Dialpad offers competitive benefits and perks, cutting-edge AI tools, and a robust training program that help you reach your full potential. We have designed our offices to be inclusive, offering a vibrant environment to cultivate collaboration and connection. Our exceptional culture, repeatedly recognized as a Great Place to Work, ensures that every employee feels valued and empowered to contribute to our collective success.

Don’t meet every single requirement? If you’re excited about this role and possess the fundamental traits, drive, and strong ambition we seek, but your experience doesn’t meet every qualification, we encourage you to apply.

Dialpad is an equal-opportunity employer. We are dedicated to creating a community of inclusion and an environment free from discrimination or harassment.

Title: Data Consultant
Description:
Join our Global Data and Compliance team!

How Will You Contribute

Collaborating with the Director of Metrics and Analytics Shared Service;

You will be a part of a team passionate about achieving results, championing the team’s goals and embracing the enterprise’s values and objectives.

This role contributes to the development and maturation of metric automation and data analytic efforts for Global Compliance; influence how data is captured, visualized and analyzed for teams all across Global Compliance. This role will have multi-functional responsibilities that include interacting with a range of collaborators, from fellow team members to business unit partners to leaders both within the function and in other functions such as Law, Government Relations, Finance, Technology, Strategy and Risk.

This is an exciting opportunity to join an innovative, rapidly maturing team with a growth mindset.

This is a hybrid position with three days in office per week, preferably at the Waterloo, ON, Canada location.

Position Responsibilities


Develop, maintain and update relevant dashboards and other data analytic tools and technologies that support partner efficiency, reporting, analysis and risk informed decision-making.
Proactively enhance data analytic and general technology and technological skills within the Compliance teams
Independently identify, analyze and define material, unmet business needs, issues, trends and opportunities within the Compliance function and recommend potential data analytic or other technology solutions.
Actively participate in projects and initiatives.
Partner, coordinate and collaborate with team members, partners and leaders within the LCG function and in other functions like Finance, Technology, Strategy and Risk.
Assist in identifying, planning and implementing change and/or continuous improvement opportunities and associated dependencies and/or roadblocks, with recommended options for addressing them.
Find opportunities to use AI in achieving function’s goals and objectives.


Required Qualifications


Bachelor’s degree in Statistics, Economics, Math, Computer Science, Engineering; or equivalent work experience.
Master’s degree in relevant field or 1-3 years of progressive analytics and technology architecture experience with expertise including cloud-based technologies (Azure, Synapse), big data, data lakes, master data management, advanced data analytics for data gathering, storing and retrieval methods on various data platforms, both structured and unstructured.
Familiarity with basic statistical tests, distributions, maximum likelihood estimators, statistical modelling techniques (regression, cluster / factor / principal components, decision tree, survival analysis, etc.)
Basic understanding of different techniques and knowledge of machine learning algorithms.
Knowledge of AI toolkits, how to create AI Agents.
Expert in data visualization tools such as Power BI.
Capability of applying a systematic, structured approach to explore / navigate / mine substantial amount of data, structured or un-structured, to uncover hidden patterns / insights.
Comfort working in a complex data environment with a wide variety of systems and numerous internal organizations.
Outstanding analytic and problem-solving skills, using data and information to highlight key themes and make recommendations for a senior executive audience


When You Join Our Team


We’ll empower you to learn and grow the career you want.
We’ll recognize and support you in a flexible environment where well-being and inclusion are more than just words.
As part of our distributed team, we’ll support you in shaping the future you want to see.


The role being advertised is an existing vacancy.

We strive to attract, develop and retain a workforce that is as diverse as the customers we serve and to foster an inclusive work environment that embraces the strength of cultures and individuals. We are committed to fair recruitment, retention, advancement and compensation, and we administer all of our practices and programs without discrimination on the basis of race, ancestry, place of origin, colour, ethnic origin, citizenship, religion or religious beliefs, creed, sex (including pregnancy and pregnancy-related conditions), sexual orientation, genetic characteristics, veteran status, gender identity, gender expression, age, marital status, family status, disability, or any other ground protected by applicable law.

Title: HR Data Analyst
Description:
Location: Kingston, Ontario, Hybrid work model with flexibility based on project requirements Language: English, strong written and verbal communication skills required

Duration: 18-month term contract

Background Check Requirement: Must be eligible to complete applicable background and reference checks




About the Opportunity

Our client is seeking a Data Analyst to join its Human Resources Systems & Analysis team during an exciting period of transformation and systems enhancement. This role will play a key part in supporting a major institutional budget tool implementation, helping ensure HR data is accurate, integrated, and translated into meaningful insights that support workforce planning and operational decision-making.

Working closely with HR, Finance, IT, and project stakeholders, you’ll contribute to enterprise-wide initiatives focused on reporting, forecasting, data validation, and systems integration. This is an excellent opportunity for someone who enjoys solving complex data challenges, collaborating across departments, and contributing to projects that have a direct impact on the employee experience and organizational planning.




What’s In It for You

• Join a collaborative and people-focused team that values curiosity, professionalism, and continuous improvement

• Gain exposure to enterprise-wide projects and transformational systems initiatives within a complex organization

• Work alongside experienced HR systems and business analysis professionals in a highly collaborative setting

• Enjoy a flexible hybrid work model that supports balance while maintaining strong team connection

• Contribute to meaningful work that supports long-term planning, operational excellence, and data-driven decision making




Your Responsibilities

• You’ll collect, clean, validate, and analyze HR data to support operational and project-based reporting requirements

• You’ll support a major HR-to-budget tool integration initiative, ensuring accurate workforce and compensation data alignment

• In this role, you’ll develop dashboards, reports, and visualizations using business intelligence and reporting tools

• You’ll collaborate with HR, Finance, IT, and external vendors to support integrations, troubleshooting, and project deliverables

• You’ll translate complex data and reporting requirements into clear, actionable insights for stakeholders across the organization

• You’ll contribute to process improvement initiatives focused on data quality, governance, and operational efficiency




Skills and Qualifications

• 3+ years of experience in a Data Analyst, HRIS Analyst, Reporting Analyst, or related analytics-focused role

• Experience working with HR systems, ERP platforms, or enterprise data environments, exposure to PeopleSoft is considered an asset

• Strong technical skills using Power BI, SQL, Excel, Tableau, Python, R, or similar analytics and reporting tools

• Demonstrated experience with data validation, reporting, dashboard development, and process analysis

• Experience supporting projects, systems implementations, integrations, or transformation initiatives

• Strong attention to detail, organizational skills, and ability to manage competing priorities within a deadline-driven environment

• Excellent communication and collaboration skills with the ability to work effectively across technical and non-technical stakeholder groups

• Experience working within large, complex, or highly regulated organizations is considered an asset




Why Partner with Altis

If you’ve never worked with a staffing agency before, we make it easy. We work with top employers across Canada who have great jobs to fill, each vetted and verified by our team. When you apply for a job with Altis, we get to know you as a candidate and learn what your strengths are. Then, if you’re a solid match, we handle all the logistics, advocating for you as a candidate for the role, providing access to coaching and connecting you directly with the hiring manager. And rest assured, all our services are free of cost for candidates.

• Strong technical skills using Power BI, SQL, Excel, Tableau, Python, R, or similar analytics and reporting tools

• Demonstrated experience with data validation, reporting, dashboard development, and process analysis

• Experience supporting projects, systems implementations, integrations, or transformation initiatives

• Strong attention to detail, organizational skills, and ability to manage competing priorities within a deadline-driven environment

• Excellent communication and collaboration skills with the ability to work effectively across technical and non-technical stakeholder groups

Title: Data Analyst I
Description:
At Acorns, our mission is to look after the financial best interests of the up-and-coming, beginning with the empowering, proud step of micro-investing. How do we accomplish our mission? Our values.

Lead With Heart - With compassion, integrity, and tenacity, inspire yourself and others to move past all previous thresholds of possibility

Make Bold Decisions - We are creating a new world. Be optimistic, adventurous, and courageous. Leap fearlessly into the future

Always Build Trust - Say what you mean, mean what you say, and do what you say you’re going to do. We are all owners. We are one team

Never Stop Growing - Change IS the constant. Stay hungry and curious. Be relentless in the pursuit of progress

Find a way - Never settle, no excuses, nothing is impossible. Just make it happen

Our values guide us, and our mission drives us. Come join us and help deliver financial wellness for the whole family, putting the tools of wealth-making into everyone’s hands.

Data Analyst I | Acorns

Acorns is seeking a Data Analyst I to serve as a key partner for our Core Experience team, specifically focused on our Investing and Banking product lines. In this role, you will leverage data to optimize the customer journey, working at the intersection of Product, Finance, and Operations to ensure our core features deliver maximum value to our customers. You will play a vital role in shaping the product roadmap by transforming raw data into actionable insights that drive retention and enhance the overall customer experience.

Acorns is a remote-first organization, offering the flexibility to work remotely while providing optional access to office space in Irvine, CA.

What You Will Do At Acorns


Collaborate with the Core Experience team to drive the ideation and rigorous analysis of product experiments.
Partner with Product, Finance, and Operations to build and maintain robust reports and dashboards specifically for our Core Experience product lines.
Write and optimize complex SQL queries to extract data from various sources for deep-dive analysis.
Utilize Databricks and DBT to transform raw data into clean, accessible datasets for the broader team.
Design and manage intuitive Tableau dashboards that track key performance indicators (KPIs) for Investing and Banking features.
Monitor customer behavior and product performance using Amplitude to identify opportunities for friction reduction and growth.
Work with managers and cross-functional teams to establish priorities, including new infrastructure and tool building.


What You Will Bring To Acorns


3-5 years of Analytics experience, preferably supporting product development or financial services.
Strong SQL skills for data extraction and experience with data visualization tools like Tableau.
A solid understanding of statistical concepts and hands-on experience of A/B testing methodologies and product experimentation.
Familiarity with modern data stack tools such as Databricks, DBT, and product analytics platforms like Amplitude.
The ability to translate complex numerical findings into clear, persuasive recommendations for non-technical stakeholders in Finance and Operations.
Hunger to deliver game-changing products.
Exceptional drive and precision in delivery.
A belief that your work is tied to your life's mission.
Optimistic about the potential of societal change.


You are not expected to have experience with all listed requirements. If you feel passionate about Acorns' mission, vision, and values, please apply.

What We Offer


Competitive salary and stock options
A comprehensive benefits package for you and your family
Flexible work location, hours, and paid time off
401(k) matching
Monthly Acorns account contribution & GoHenry account for your family
Mindfulness and Financial Wellness resources, Headspace and Addition Wealth
Acorns Career Development Program (Ongoing training sessions, development plans, development check-ins, Cornerstone’s online training platform)
Roots Leadership Program for Emerging Leaders
Community week onsite gatherings and various virtual events
Talented and motivated team members who care deeply about one another, our mission, and our customers.
The rare opportunity to create a new world. We inspire one another every day to do meaningful work that solves big societal challenges.


About Acorns

Acorns is a financial wellness app that helps everyday people and families save and invest money for the long term. Since 2014, Acorns has grown into a global company with multiple life-stage products serving the needs of kids, teens, adults, and parents. Named one of Time’s “World’s Best Brands of 2024,” Acorns has helped over 14 million people save & invest over $25 billion dollars, much of it from spare change and small amounts.

Compensation Information

The pay range for this position at commencement of employment is expected to be between $121,000 and $140,000/year; however, base pay offered may vary depending on multiple individualized factors, including market location, job-related knowledge, skills, and experience. The total compensation package for this position may also include other elements, including equity grants, and discretionary cash bonus awards in addition to a full range of medical, financial, and/or other benefits (including 401(k) eligibility and various paid time off benefits, such as vacation, sick time, and parental leave), dependent on the position offered. Details of participation in these benefit plans will be provided if an employee receives an offer of employment.

If hired, employee will be in an “at-will position” and the Company reserves the right to modify base salary (as well as any other discretionary payment or compensation program) at any time, including for reasons related to individual performance, Company or individual department/team performance, and market factors.

Work Authorization

Acorns participates in E-Verify to confirm the employment eligibility of all newly hired employees. For more information about E-Verify, please visit www.e-verify.gov

Title: Data Analyst
Description:
Job Description

What is the opportunity?

The Data Analyst is responsible for hands on data analysis, profiling, validation, and quality assurance across the CDR and broader Client Data ecosystem. This role is the engine room of the team, ensuring that client and counterparty data is accurate, complete, consistent, and fit for purpose across regulatory, risk, and business use cases.

You will work across multiple source systems and datasets, performing detailed investigations into data issues, supporting onboarding and migration initiatives, executing reconciliations, and contributing to the team's data quality frameworks. The ideal candidate is analytically rigorous, detail oriented, and comfortable navigating messy, multi source data environments. You take ownership of your workstreams and deliver reliable, well documented analysis with minimal oversight.

What will you do?

Key Responsibilities

Data Analysis & Profiling

Perform data profiling, exploratory analysis, and validation across large, multi source datasets to assess completeness, accuracy, and consistency. Execute end to end analytical work in support of data onboarding, migration, reconciliation, and quality remediation initiatives. Investigate data anomalies and discrepancies, tracing issues across source systems, transformation logic, and target platforms. Deliver structured, well documented analysis that is clear enough for stakeholders to review and for developers to act on.

Data Quality & Issue Resolution

Execute data quality checks against defined rules, thresholds, and business expectations. Perform root cause analysis on data breaks and exceptions, identifying whether issues are systemic or isolated. Recommend and track remediation actions, pushing toward structural fixes rather than recurring manual corrections. Contribute to the definition and refinement of data quality rules and exception handling processes.

Reconciliation & Validation

Execute reconciliation between source systems and target platforms, documenting discrepancies with clear supporting evidence. Validate data transformations by comparing expected versus actual outputs, flagging mismatches and working with technology teams to resolve them. Ensure reconciliation results are reproducible, well documented, and traceable.

Data Mapping & Lineage Support

Contribute to the documentation and validation of data mapping and transformation logic across source and target systems. Support the team in maintaining data lineage documentation to ensure traceability and auditability. Verify that implemented data flows match business specifications, identifying gaps and escalating where needed.

Documentation & Process

Maintain clear, structured documentation including analysis logs, reconciliation results, data dictionaries, and mapping specifications. Ensure all work products are reproducible and well organized for handoff or review. Contribute to the development and improvement of team standards for analysis, documentation, and quality assurance.

Collaboration & Stakeholder Support

Work closely with Business Analysts and Senior Analysts to support requirements validation through data samples, profiling results, and feasibility assessments. Participate in working sessions with stakeholders and technology teams, contributing data level insights. Respond to ad hoc data requests from stakeholders, delivering accurate and timely outputs. Communicate findings clearly, escalating issues with context and recommended next steps.

What do you need to succeed?

Experience

Required Experience & Skills

3 to 5 years in a Data Analyst or similar data focused role, with experience working across multi source datasets in a complex environment. Demonstrated ability to own and deliver analytical workstreams independently within larger programs. Experience with data quality, reconciliation, or migration initiatives is strongly preferred.

Technical Skills


Strong SQL skills with experience writing complex queries against large relational databases (SQL Server preferred). Comfortable with joins, window functions, CTEs, and subqueries.
Strong proficiency in Excel including advanced functions, pivot tables, lookups, and Power Query.
Experience with data profiling, validation, reconciliation, and standardization techniques.
Exposure to Python, R, or VBA for data manipulation is an asset.
Comfort working across messy, incomplete, and inconsistent datasets, and applying structured approaches to make sense of them.


What’s in it for you?

We thrive on the challenge to be our best, progressive thinking to keep growing, and working together to deliver trusted advice to help our clients thrive and communities prosper. We care about each other, reaching our potential, making a difference to our communities, and achieving success that is mutual.


A comprehensive Total Rewards Program including bonuses and flexible benefits, competitive compensation, commissions, and stock where applicable
Work in a dynamic, collaborative, progressive, and high-performing team
Opportunities to do challenging work
Opportunities to build close relationships with clients
Access to a variety of job opportunities across business and geographies


Job Skills

Critical Thinking, Data Administration, Data Mining, Data Modeling, Data Movement, Detail-Oriented, Group Problem Solving, Quantitative Research, Research Documents

Additional Job Details

RBC CENTRE, 155 WELLINGTON ST W:TORONTO

City:

Toronto

Canada

Employment Type:

Full time

CAPITAL MARKETS

Regular
