Title: Supply Chain Analyst
Description:
Overview

The Surface Supply Chain organization is responsible for delivering the right products to customers at the right time, cost, and quality supporting a Global, highly complex hardware portfolio. We partner closely across Product, Finance, and Sales & Marketing to plan, build, and deliver Surface devices through demand planning, supply execution, materials management, and logistics.

The Supply Chain Analyst is responsible for analyzing, optimizing, and supporting end-to-end supply chain operations through data-driven insights, operational reporting, and cross-functional collaboration. This role operates at the intersection of operations execution and data analytics — transforming raw supply chain data into actionable intelligence that drives better decision-making across demand planning, supply allocation, materials management, logistics, and inventory operations.

The Supply Chain Analyst partners closely with supply chain leads, materials, and customer operations teams to identify inefficiencies, surface risks, build scalable reporting frameworks, and support scenario-based planning that enables resilient and efficient supply chain performance.

Microsoft’s mission is to empower every person and every organization on the planet to achieve more. As employees we come together with a growth mindset, innovate to empower others, and collaborate to realize our shared goals. Each day we build on our values of respect, integrity, and accountability to create a culture of inclusion where everyone can thrive at work and beyond.

Responsibilities


Develop and maintain operational dashboards, KPI scorecards, and analytical models to monitor supply chain health across demand, supply, inventory, and fulfillment.
Analyze demand-supply gaps, allocation shortfalls, and materials constraints to provide timely, insight-driven recommendations to planning and operations teams.
Support scenario modeling and what-if analysis for planning cycles, including CFO targets, demand/supply plan of records, material supportability reconciliation.
Partner with cross-functional stakeholders (planning, sourcing, materials, finance) to translate business questions into structured analytical frameworks.
Identify patterns, trends, and anomalies in supply chain data; proactively surface risks and opportunities before they impact operations.
Automate recurring reports and data pipelines to reduce manual effort and improve data accuracy and timeliness.
Support the design and adoption of advanced analytics capabilities, including predictive modeling, AI-assisted forecasting, and optimization tools within supply chain workflows.
Contribute to process improvement initiatives by quantifying operational inefficiencies and recommending data-backed solutions.
Ensure data integrity and consistency across supply chain systems, serving as a steward for key operational datasets.


Qualifications

Required Qualifications:


Bachelor's Degree in Engineering, Industrial Engineering, Supply Chain Management, Operations Management, Supply Chain Analytics, or related field AND 3+ years supply planning, operations, supply chain, indirect supply planning, technology-related supply chain, sourcing and manufacturing, Sales & Operations Planning, material requirement planning systems and tools or related experience.
OR Master's Degree in Engineering, Industrial Engineering, Supply Chain Management, Business, Operations Management, Supply Chain Analytics, or related field AND 1+ year(s) supply planning, operations, supply chain, indirect supply planning, technology-related supply chain, sourcing and manufacturing, Sales & Operations Planning, material requirement planning systems and tools or related experience.
OR equivalent experience.

Preferred Qualifications


2+ years of experience in supply chain, data analytics, or a related field.
Solid proficiency in data analysis tools (SQL, Python/R) and visualization platforms (Power BI, Tableau).
Knowledge of supply chain fundamentals, and proven experience designing or transforming processes, systems, and data architectures at scale.
Experience leveraging AI/ML tools to analyze complex datasets and translate ambiguous business problems into structured insights, clear recommendations, and scalable solutions.
Solid analytical and systems thinking, with the ability to connect strategy, process, data, and technology to drive measurable business impact.


#W+DJOBS

Supply Planning IC4 - The typical base pay range for this role across the U.S. is USD $97,600.00 - $188,400.00 per year. There is a different range applicable to specific work locations, within the San Francisco Bay area and New York City metropolitan area, and the base pay range for this role in those locations is USD $125,000.00 - $206,400.00 per year.

Title: Senior Data Engineer
Description:
Who We Are

Verkada is transforming how organizations protect their people and places with an integrated, privacy-sensitive AI-powered platform that includes solutions for video security, access control, air quality sensors, alarms, intercoms, and visitor management.

We’ve got serious momentum in the market: more than 30,000 customers (including 100+ of the Fortune 500), a $5.8B valuation, more than $1 billion in annualized bookings, and backing from CapitalG, Sequoia Capital, General Catalyst, Felicis Ventures, Next47 and more. Physical AI is one of the most consequential technology shifts of our time, and Verkada is at the center of it.

You can look at all kinds of communities to see our platform’s impact in the world. It's the retailer that uses our agentic AI to deter theft before it happens. The warehouse that uses AI-powered alerts to make sure its team is protected on the floor with proper PPE. The school that’s alerted to a threat in real-time and triggers a lockdown in seconds, not minutes. We’re rapidly scaling this impact: today, more than 2 million Verkada devices are deployed across 170+ countries.

About The Role

As a member of our Data Platforms and Analytics Team and reporting directly to the Head of Data, you will be responsible for developing the core enterprise data warehouse infrastructure, data models, and pipelines at Verkada. We aim to provide a single reporting source of truth for enterprise data with clear business data definitions to empower internal Finance, Sales, Marketing, Product and HR teams to make informed data driven decisions. Our strategy emphasizes automation, scalable architecture, and accuracy, while providing iterative improvements over time.

We are committed to a thriving in-office culture. This role requires you to be onsite at our HQ in San Mateo, CA.

What You'll Do


Engineer and maintain efficient, scalable warehouse infrastructure that facilitates high-quality, accurate insights and reporting.
Design, implement and manage automated data pipelines from various data sources including databases, API endpoints, business systems, and data lakes.
Collaborate across departments to develop bronze, silver, and gold data models, enforcing business alignment and data governance.
Partner with Finance, Sales, Marketing, Product, and HR stakeholders to define data pipeline sources, data modeling requirements, and data quality standards.
Oversee the entire project lifecycle, moving initiatives from initial design through to production leveraging development standards such as Github PR reviews and Jira sprint board management.
Create and deploy strategies to maintain data security, integrity, and regulatory compliance.
Provide leadership and guidance to grow and mentor future members of the data engineering team.


What You Bring


Bachelor's or Master's degree in Computer Science or a related technical field.
Minimum of 5 years of professional data engineering experience.
Advanced skill in Python and SQL.
Expertise with cloud warehouses such as BigQuery, Snowflake, or Databricks leveraging DBT as a data modeling framework.
Expertise in managing data lakes with open source file formats such as Apache Iceberg, Delta Lake or Apache Hudi
Proven track record in constructing automated pipelines using Airflow, Dagster, Fivetran, and / or Airbyte from various operational databases, API endpoints, business systems, and data lakes.
Expert-level proficiency in SQL and Python is required.
Experience building / managing data observability and data quality platforms such as BigEye, Monte Carlo and Great Expectations is a plus
Familiarity or experience building vector databases for Generative AI use cases is a plus.
Experience building Gen AI agents to optimize development workflows within data engineering is a plus.
Experience building Gen AI agents for providing support for business intelligence related inquiries is a plus
Must be willing and able to work onsite five days per week.


Employee Benefits

Verkada is committed to fostering a workplace environment that prioritizes the holistic health and wellbeing of our employees and their families by offering comprehensive wellness perks, benefits, and resources. Our benefits and perks programs include, but are not limited to:


Healthcare programs that can be tailored to meet the personal health and financial well-being needs - Premiums are 100% covered for the employee under at least one plan and 80% for family premiums under all plans
Nationwide medical, vision and dental coverage
Health Saving Account (HSA) with annual employer contributions and Flexible Spending Account (FSA) with tax saving options
Expanded mental health support
Paid parental leave policy & fertility benefits
Time off to relax and recharge through our paid holidays, firmwide extended holidays, flexible PTO and personal sick time
Professional development stipend
Wellness/fitness benefits
Healthy lunches provided daily
Commuter benefits


Additional Information


You must be independently authorized to work in the U.S. We are unable to sponsor or take over sponsorship of an employment visa for this role, at this time.


Annual Pay Range

At Verkada, we want to attract and retain the best employees, and compensate them in a way that appropriately and fairly values their individual contribution to the company. With that in mind, we carefully consider a number of factors to determine the appropriate starting pay for an employee, including their primary work location and an assessment of a candidate's skills and experience, as well as market demands and internal parity. A Verkada employee may be eligible for additional forms of compensation, depending on their role, including sales incentives, discretionary bonuses, and/or equity in the company in the form of restricted stock units (RSUs)

Below is the annual on-target earnings (OTE) range for full-time employees for this position, comprised of base compensation and commissions (if applicable).

Estimated Annual Pay Range

$140,000—$210,000 USD

Verkada Is An Equal Opportunity Employer

As an equal opportunity employer, Verkada is committed to providing employment opportunities to all individuals. All applicants for positions at Verkada will be treated without regard to race, color, ethnicity, religion, sex, gender, gender identity and expression, sexual orientation, national origin, disability, age, marital status, veteran status, pregnancy, or any other basis prohibited by applicable law.

Your application will be handled in accordance with our Candidate Privacy Policy.

Title: Remote IT Technician
Description:
Remote IT Technician – Role Description & Qualifications

We are seeking a dedicated and technically skilled Remote IT Technician to provide remote technical support, troubleshoot technology issues, and ensure the smooth operation of IT systems and services. The ideal candidate should possess strong problem-solving abilities, excellent customer service skills, and the capability to resolve technical issues efficiently in a remote environment.

The Remote IT Technician will be responsible for supporting end users with hardware, software, network, and system-related issues through remote support tools and communication platforms. This role requires strong technical knowledge, attention to detail, and the ability to manage multiple support requests while maintaining high service standards and user satisfaction.

Key Responsibilities
Provide remote technical support for desktops, laptops, mobile devices, and peripheral equipment.
Diagnose and resolve hardware, software, operating system, and network-related issues.
Install, configure, update, and maintain software applications and system components.
Manage user accounts, passwords, permissions, and access controls.
Support email systems, cloud-based services, VPN connectivity, and remote access solutions.
Monitor system performance and proactively identify technical issues.
Document support requests, troubleshooting activities, and resolutions within ticketing systems.
Perform routine maintenance, updates, backups, and security checks.
Assist with cybersecurity best practices and endpoint security management.
Collaborate with internal teams and external vendors to resolve complex technical problems.
Required Skills & Competencies
Strong knowledge of Windows, macOS, and common business software applications.
Understanding of networking fundamentals, including TCP/IP, DNS, DHCP, VPNs, and wireless networking.
Experience with remote support tools, help desk platforms, and ticket management systems.
Excellent troubleshooting and problem-solving abilities.
Strong verbal and written communication skills.
Ability to explain technical concepts clearly to non-technical users.
Strong organizational and time-management skills.
Ability to prioritize tasks and manage multiple support requests effectively.
Professionalism, patience, and a customer-service-oriented mindset.
Preferred Qualifications
Bachelor's degree or Diploma in Information Technology, Computer Science, Information Systems, Network Administration, or a related field.
Previous experience in IT support, technical support, help desk operations, system administration, or network support is an advantage.
Familiarity with cloud platforms, endpoint management solutions, cybersecurity practices, remote monitoring tools, and ticketing systems is beneficial.
Industry certifications such as CompTIA A+, Network+, Security+, Microsoft, Cisco, or equivalent certifications are considered an asset.
What We Offer
Competitive compensation and benefits package.
Opportunities for professional development and certification growth.
Flexible and collaborative remote work environment.
Exposure to diverse technologies and IT projects.
Opportunity to contribute to maintaining secure and reliable IT operations.
The ideal Remote IT Technician is dependable, technically proficient, and solution-oriented, capable of delivering high-quality remote support while maintaining secure, reliable, and efficient technology environments that enhance productivity and support organizational success.

Title: Data Center Technician
Description:

As a Senior Data Center Technician, you will lead operational support for Treasury’s 24x7 data center environment, ensuring the availability, reliability, and security of critical IT infrastructure. This role includes performing system health checks, conducting hardware maintenance, and resolving incidents in alignment with strict service-level agreements. You will also mentor junior technicians and contribute to the continuous improvement of operational processes.

Conduct daily system checks, monitor performance, and perform scheduled hardware maintenance.
Respond to and resolve incidents per defined SLAs, coordinating with other technical teams when necessary.

Maintain accurate maintenance logs and incident resolution records.
Participate in a 24x7 support rotation, including weekends and holidays.


Basic Qualifications (Required)


U.S. Citizenship and eligibility for High Public Trust clearance.
1–8+ years of relevant IT infrastructure or data center experience.
Proficiency in troubleshooting Windows OS, Microsoft Office Suite, and standard enterprise tools.
Experience with ITSM platforms such as ServiceNow.
Strong communication, organizational, and problem-solving skills.


Preferred Qualifications


CompTIA A+, Network+, or Security+ certification.
ServiceNow or other ITSM platform experience.
Federal IT or large-scale enterprise infrastructure support experience.
ITIL Foundation certification or equivalent training.

Title: Business Intelligence Engineer, Amazon Shipping BI
Description:
Description

Amazon’s global fulfillment network enables any merchant to ship items that are ordered on Amazon to any place on earth. There is a complex network of ways in which items move between vendor locations, Amazon warehouses, and customer locations as well as several intermediate locations through which packages travel before reaching the customer. With a scale of millions of packages, each with different attributes and delivery requirements, what results is a highly dense graph of nodes. We have built a highly respected BI and Data Engineering team which is focused on solving complex problems in worldwide transportation using workflows, optimization algorithms, and machine learning systems. These are large-scale distributed systems handling millions of packages being shipped through the Amazon logistics network. As a Business Intelligence Engineer, you will develop datasets, dashboards, reports, and visualizations using BI tools, and provide data-driven recommendations to support strategic planning within Amazon Shipping. Additionally, you will deliver across planned projects that will improve overall cost and profitability for the business.

Key job responsibilities

Data Management and Integration: Collect, process, and manage large datasets from various sources, ensuring data accuracy, consistency, and accessibility. Data Analysis: Analyze complex data sets to identify trends, patterns, and insights that can inform business decisions. Reporting and Visualization: Develop and maintain dashboards, reports, and visualizations to present data insights in an understandable and actionable format. Stakeholder Collaboration: Work closely with stakeholders to understand their data needs, provide data-driven recommendations, and support strategic planning. Tool and Technology Utilization: Utilize and stay updated with the latest BI tools and technologies to enhance data analysis and reporting capabilities.

Basic Qualifications


3+ years of analyzing and interpreting data with Redshift, Oracle, NoSQL etc. experience
1+ years of SQL, ETL or Oracle experience
1+ years of processing large, multi-dimensional datasets from multiple sources experience
1+ years of performing statistical analysis experience
1+ years of developing automated reporting experience
Experience with data visualization using Tableau, Quicksight, or similar tools
Experience with data modeling, warehousing and building ETL pipelines
Experience in Statistical Analysis packages such as R, SAS and Matlab
Experience using SQL to pull data from a database or data warehouse and scripting experience (Python) to process data for modeling


Preferred Qualifications


Experience with AWS solutions such as EC2, DynamoDB, S3, and Redshift
Experience in data mining, ETL, etc. and using databases in a business environment with large-scale, complex datasets


Amazon is an equal opportunity employer and does not discriminate on the basis of protected veteran status, disability, or other legally protected status.

Our inclusive culture empowers Amazonians to deliver the best results for our customers. If you have a disability and need a workplace accommodation or adjustment during the application and hiring process, including support for the interview or onboarding process, please visit https://amazon.jobs/content/en/how-we-hire/accommodations for more information. If the country/region you’re applying in isn’t listed, please contact your Recruiting Partner.

The base salary range for this position is listed below. Your Amazon package will include sign-on payments and restricted stock units (RSUs). Final compensation will be determined based on factors including experience, qualifications, and location. Amazon also offers comprehensive benefits including health insurance (medical, dental, vision, prescription, Basic Life & AD&D insurance and option for Supplemental life plans, EAP, Mental Health Support, Medical Advice Line, Flexible Spending Accounts, Adoption and Surrogacy Reimbursement coverage), 401(k) matching, paid time off, and parental leave. Learn more about our benefits at https://amazon.jobs/en/benefits.

Title: Data Analyst (Remote)
Description:
Job Title: Data Analyst (Remote)
Work Mode: Fully Remote
Schedule: Flexible (up to 40 hours per week, based on project availability)




Role Overview:

We are looking for software engineers across Latin America to join a paid remote AI training program. In this role, you will review and evaluate AI-generated code, rate AI responses for accuracy and quality, and write coding challenges to help improve AI model performance. This is a flexible, remote opportunity well-suited for software engineers seeking project-based, asynchronous work. No prior AI experience required.




Key Responsibilities

Review and evaluate code written by AI models for accuracy, efficiency, and best practices
Rate AI responses across key dimensions including correctness, instruction-following, and quality
Write original computer science questions and coding challenges to test AI capabilities
Identify bugs, errors, and inconsistencies in AI-generated code using structured guidelines
Provide clear, constructive, and structured written feedback to improve AI responses
Follow project guidelines, quality standards, and confidentiality requirements




Qualifications

Software engineering experience — junior, mid-level, or senior welcome
Proficiency in one or more of the following: Python, Java, JavaScript, TypeScript, C++, Swift, or Rust
Strong attention to detail, analytical thinking, and problem-solving skills
Background in full stack, backend, frontend, mobile, or any engineering discipline




Eligibility Requirements

Candidates must be legally authorised to work in one of the following locations:
Argentina, Bolivia, Brazil, Belize, Chile, Colombia, Costa Rica, Dominican Republic, Dominica, Ecuador, El Salvador, Guatemala, Honduras, Mexico, Nicaragua, Panama, Paraguay, Peru, Puerto Rico, Trinidad and Tobago, Uruguay, Venezuela




Compensation

Earn up to $34 USD/hr — core project rate with additional performance incentives averaging 7.5% on top
Payments issued weekly via supported payout platforms (e.g., PayPal or AirTM)




Work Arrangement

Fully remote and asynchronous
Flexible workload depending on project availability
Free access to leading AI tools worth $200+/month included




Equal Opportunity Statement Selection decisions are based solely on skills, qualifications, and project requirements. We are committed to inclusive and fair engagement practices and consider all qualified applicants without regard to legally protected characteristics.




Ready to put your coding skills to work on AI? Apply now — interviews begin within days of application.

Title: Data Engineer - Remote
Description:
About The Company

Inclusa, a values-based organization dedicated to providing person-centered and community-focused long-term care services, manages support for Family Care members across Wisconsin. As a community-oriented organization, Inclusa emphasizes building vibrant, inclusive communities through its trademarked Commonunity® model, which centers on the belief in everyone's potential and the pursuit of the common good. In 2022, Inclusa was acquired by Humana, a leading healthcare company committed to transforming healthcare delivery. This partnership aims to create innovative, industry-leading models of care that support members across the healthcare continuum, enhancing quality of life and health outcomes for diverse populations.

About The Role

The Lead Data Engineer will play a pivotal role in modernizing, optimizing, and stabilizing the Wisconsin Medicaid Market's data platform ecosystem. This individual contributor role demands a high level of autonomy, technical expertise, and leadership, including directing contract resources. The primary responsibility is to oversee the Data Warehouse and Operational Data Store (ODS), drive ETL/data movement strategies—including SSIS modernization—and enhance the reliability, observability, and security of critical Medicaid data pipelines. The Lead Data Engineer collaborates closely with the Market BI team to improve data access, establish pipeline patterns within Databricks, and support the evolving analytics environment. The role involves owning and evolving complex data stores, reducing technical debt, and ensuring data platform stability across dual health plan technology stacks. The successful candidate will be instrumental in guiding modernization efforts, establishing standards, and fostering a culture of continuous improvement within a fast-paced, complex environment.

Qualifications

Title: Data Engineer, Product Data Warehouse, Go-To-Market
Description:
This role may also be located in our Playa Vista, CA campus.

Applicants in the County of Los Angeles: Qualified applications with arrest or conviction records will be considered for employment in accordance with the Los Angeles County Fair Chance Ordinance for Employers and the California Fair Chance Act.

Applicants in San Francisco: Qualified applications with arrest or conviction records will be considered for employment in accordance with the San Francisco Fair Chance Ordinance for Employers and the California Fair Chance Act.

The application window will be open until at least May 29, 2026. This opportunity will remain online based on business needs which may be before or after the specified date.

Note: By applying to this position you will have an opportunity to share your preferred working location from the following: New York, NY, USA; Atlanta, GA, USA; Boulder, CO, USA; Chicago, IL, USA; Mountain View, CA, USA; Los Angeles, CA, USA; San Francisco, CA, USA.Minimum qualifications:


Bachelor's degree or equivalent practical experience.
5 years of experience designing data pipelines, and dimensional data modeling for synch and asynch system integration and implementation using internal (e.g., Flume, etc.) and external stacks (DataFlow, Spark, etc.).
5 years of experience coding in one or more programming languages.
5 years of experience working with data infrastructure and data models by performing exploratory queries and scripts.


Preferred qualifications:


5 years of experience with statistical methodology and data consumption tools such as business intelligence tools, collabs, jupyter notebooks, Tableau, Power BI, DataStudio, and business intelligence platforms.
3 years of experience partnering with stakeholders (e.g., users, partners, customer), and managing stakeholders/customers.
Experience in ad sales, ad support, finance, data science, or strategy and operations roles.
Familiarity with Google's Search and Measurement products.
Knowledge of business intelligence platforms, distributed databases, data analysis tools, and code review systems.


About The Job

The Product Data Warehouse (PDW) team within Go-to-Market tracks adoption of ads and measurement products and features. This is used to generate lead lists, analyze the value of various features, set priorities for sales teams, and to help influence the direction of Ads products. Our goal is to organize and produce data that's thoughtful, useful to sales teams, and widely accessible. You'll also find our work in our Ads CRM (Connect Sales), in sales leads' database scripts, in weekly leadership meetings, in incentive contracts for partners, and referenced in Ads annual plans.

In this role, you will lead the measurement and monitoring of the measurement, audience and data products by developing Key Performance Indicators (KPIs), data pipelines, dashboards, and doing analysis to derive insights and opportunities. You will work cross-functionally with Global Product Leads, Go-to-Market, Sales, etc. to make sure we have a clear understanding of Measurement, Audience and Data products and to find areas of opportunity.

The Global Business Organization (GBO) is the engine that powers Google’s full ecosystem of products and services to help customers and partners succeed and grow. GBO includes the commercial arms of Ads sellers, business development teams, and customer services and support, as well as overlay programs that enable coordinated engagement with Google's most complex and important customers and partners.

Individual pay is determined by factors including job-related skills, experience, and relevant education or training.

US: $156000 - $227000 (USD) + 15% bonus target + bonus + equity + benefits

Responsibilities

Learn more about benefits at Google .


Drive ads and search, shopping, and performance max feature adoption by developing scalable, shareable sales tools, dashboards, data, and programs using the F1 query engine, and other tools to support key resources.
Synthesize advertiser data and conduct analyses to deliver insights and actionable recommendations for product and sales leadership.
Define and prioritize KPIs to drive key global initiatives that will be used by regions, countries, and sales pods in all sales channels.
Be a strategic thought partner to commercial leaders of products like Performance Max.
Manage special projects as assigned.



Google is proud to be an equal opportunity workplace and is an affirmative action employer. We are committed to equal employment opportunity regardless of race, color, ancestry, religion, sex, national origin, sexual orientation, age, citizenship, marital status, disability, gender identity or Veteran status. We also consider qualified applicants regardless of criminal histories, consistent with legal requirements. See also Google's EEO Policy and EEO is the Law. If you have a disability or special need that requires accommodation, please let us know by completing our Accommodations for Applicants form .

Title: Business Analyst, Jr.
Description:
About The Company

Landen is an organic skincare brand founded in Copenhagen, Denmark, in 2020. Specializing in creating hair and body care products derived from botanical ingredients, Landen emphasizes gentle yet effective formulations complemented by minimalist design. The company's philosophy centers around promoting positive wellbeing habits through high-quality, natural products. Landen is committed to sustainability, transparency, and innovation, aiming to enhance the daily routines of its customers with products that are both effective and environmentally conscious.

About The Role

We are seeking a Junior Business Analyst to join our dynamic team in a remote capacity. This entry-level position offers an excellent opportunity for individuals passionate about bridging business needs and technological solutions. As a Business Analyst, you will play a key role in facilitating business process improvements, supporting product development, and fostering innovation. Your primary responsibilities will include investigating, analyzing, reviewing, and documenting both functional and non-functional business specifications. You will manage the requirements development process through elicitation, analysis, specification, and verification, ensuring alignment with business goals and technical feasibility.

This role involves coordinating and project managing the implementation or integration of new products or features, including user training and change management activities. You will work closely with cross-functional teams, including IT, QA, and business stakeholders, to ensure seamless delivery of solutions. The ideal candidate will possess strong communication skills, problem-solving abilities, and a keen interest in learning new technologies and methodologies. Experience working in an agile environment and advanced Microsoft Excel skills will be advantageous.

Qualifications


Bachelor’s degree in Business Administration, Information Technology, or a related field
Previous internship or entry-level experience in business analysis or related roles (preferred but not mandatory)
Proficiency in Microsoft Excel and other MS Office tools
Understanding of agile methodologies and project management principles
Excellent written and verbal communication skills
Strong analytical and problem-solving skills
Ability to manage multiple priorities and work collaboratively in a remote setting
Curiosity and eagerness to learn about new technologies and business processes


Responsibilities


Bridge business requirements with IT solutions to support organizational goals
Analyze the impact of technology changes on existing business processes and systems
Document detailed requirements and break down complex initiatives following agile and MVP principles
Coordinate solution delivery through planning, refining, developing, testing, and releasing sprints
Negotiate scope changes with Scrum teams and business stakeholders to ensure project alignment
Facilitate knowledge transfer sessions and support change management activities
Analyze system defects, verify fixes, and assist in validation processes
Collaborate with QA teams to develop and review test plans, and assist with testing activities
Engage with stakeholders to gather feedback and ensure requirements are accurately captured and implemented


Benefits


Competitive salary and remuneration packages
Flexible and hybrid working arrangements, including tailored hours and remote work options
Generous paid parental leave and leave purchase plans
Career development opportunities and ongoing learning support
Focus on diversity, inclusion, health, and wellbeing of employees


Equal Opportunity

Landen is an equal opportunity employer committed to fostering a diverse and inclusive work environment. We welcome applications from individuals of all backgrounds, genders, ages, ethnicities, abilities, sexual orientations, and gender identities. We believe that diverse perspectives and experiences enrich our organization and drive innovation. We are dedicated to providing equal employment opportunities and ensuring a respectful, supportive workplace for all employees.

Title: Data Entry Clerk
Description:
We are looking for a detail-oriented Data Entry Clerk to support daily information processing for a transport organization in Dallas, Texas. This Long-term Contract position is ideal for someone who is accurate, dependable, and comfortable managing high volumes of data in a structured office setting. The role follows a daytime schedule and requires strong focus, organization, and confidence working with computer-based records.

Responsibilities:
• Enter and update numeric and text-based information in company systems with a high level of speed and accuracy.
• Review documents and records for completeness, correcting inconsistencies and flagging missing details when needed.
• Maintain organized digital files so information can be retrieved quickly and shared with the appropriate teams.
• Perform routine quality checks to ensure entered data matches source materials and internal standards.
• Support day-to-day administrative data tasks that help keep transport-related records current and reliable.
• Communicate with team members to clarify unclear information and resolve data discrepancies efficiently.
• Manage assigned workloads within standard business hours while maintaining attention to detail and productivity.
• Assist with office-based coverage needs, including working independently on days with limited onsite staff.
• Proven ability to perform accurate data entry in a fast-paced environment.
• Strong typing skills with confidence entering both numeric and general computer-based data.
• High attention to detail and commitment to maintaining data accuracy.
• Comfort using office computer systems and standard digital tools for record management.
• Ability to stay organized, manage repetitive tasks, and meet daily productivity expectations.
• Strong dependability and the ability to work independently when needed.
• Clear communication skills for identifying and resolving incomplete or inconsistent information.

Title: Data Insights Analyst
Description:
At bet365, we're one of the world's leading online gambling companies, revolutionising the industry since 2000. Founded by Denise Coates CBE, we now employ over 9,000 people and serve over 100 million customers in 27 languages. Our focus on In-Play betting has solidified our market-leading position, offering an unmatched experience across 96 sports and 700,000 streaming events. With over 750 concurrent sporting fixtures at peak and more live sports streamed than anyone else in Europe, we handle over 6 billion HTTP requests daily and process more than 2 million bets per hour at peak.




We empower our employees to push boundaries and explore new ideas, cultivating a culture that celebrates and rewards creativity. This offers employees a wealth of opportunities for growth, giving them the opportunity to make a real impact in the world of online gambling. As a forward-thinking company, we’re breaking new ground in software innovation too, redefining what’s possible for our customers worldwide.




Job Description




We are seeking a highly analytical and commercially minded Gaming Insights Analyst to join our dynamic Insights team. In this role, you will be a crucial partner to the Casino Gaming and Marketing divisions, transforming raw data into the strategic intelligence that drives our business forward.




You will move beyond surface-level reporting to conduct deep, methodologically sound analyses of our Gaming products, marketing campaigns, and player behaviour.




Your primary mission is to challenge assumptions, uncover the "why" behind the numbers, and provide clear, actionable recommendations that optimise our investments, enhance player engagement, and grow our market share.




This is a high-impact role for a data-driven storyteller who is passionate about the Gaming industry and thrives on turning complex questions into profitable business decisions.




Qualifications




Proven analytical experience as a data analyst, marketing analyst, or product analyst role, preferably within the e-commerce, mobile, or online Gaming/Gambling industry.
Advanced SQL skills and extensive experience querying and manipulating large, complex datasets from data warehouses like BigQuery, Redshift, or Snowflake.
Strong, practical understanding of statistical analysis, experimental design, and the principles of A/B testing (e.g., control groups, statistical significance), to extract reliable insights from imperfect data.
Highly proficient with business intelligence tools such as Looker (or a similar tool like Tableau/Power BI) for data visualisation and building intuitive dashboards.
Ability to connect data insights to real-world business results and financial outcomes, acting as a business partner rather than just an analyst.
Excellent communicator who is able to clearly explain complex findings to both technical and non-technical audiences in a simple and concise way.
Passion for casino games and a curiosity about the online gaming world, with a keen interest in understanding what drives player behaviour.




Additional Information




Designing, executing, and delivering post-mortems for marketing and promotional campaigns, going beyond top-line results to measure true incremental lift, ROI, and profitability, accounting for confounding variables and establishing clear attribution.

Job Description


We are seeking a highly analytical and commercially minded Gaming Insights Analyst to join our dynamic Insights team. In this role, you will be a crucial partner to the Casino Gaming and Marketing divisions, transforming raw data into the strategic intelligence that drives our business forward.


You will move beyond surface-level reporting to conduct deep, methodologically sound analyses of our Gaming products, marketing campaigns, and player behaviour.


Your primary mission is to challenge assumptions, uncover the "why" behind the numbers, and provide clear, actionable recommendations that optimise our investments, enhance player engagement, and grow our market share.


This is a high-impact role for a data-driven storyteller who is passionate about the Gaming industry and thrives on turning complex questions into profitable business decisions.


Qualifications


  • Proven analytical experience as a data analyst, marketing analyst, or product analyst role, preferably within the e-commerce, mobile, or online Gaming/Gambling industry.
  • Advanced SQL skills and extensive experience querying and manipulating large, complex datasets from data warehouses like BigQuery, Redshift, or Snowflake.
  • Strong, practical understanding of statistical analysis, experimental design, and the principles of A/B testing (e.g., control groups, statistical significance), to extract reliable insights from imperfect data.
  • Highly proficient with business intelligence tools such as Looker (or a similar tool like Tableau/Power BI) for data visualisation and building intuitive dashboards.
  • Ability to connect data insights to real-world business results and financial outcomes, acting as a business partner rather than just an analyst.
  • Excellent communicator who is able to clearly explain complex findings to both technical and non-technical audiences in a simple and concise way.
  • Passion for casino games and a curiosity about the online gaming world, with a keen interest in understanding what drives player behaviour.


Additional Information


  • Designing, executing, and delivering post-mortems for marketing and promotional campaigns, going beyond top-line results to measure true incremental lift, ROI, and profitability, accounting for confounding variables and establishing clear attribution.
  • Applying a rigorous analytical framework to evaluate the performance of marketing activities and product changes (e.g., game positioning) while measuring the impact of these "tests," using statistical principles to assess their effectiveness and inform future strategy.
  • Analysing player behavior by segmenting our user base across various dimensions (e.g., New vs. Existing, Player Value Tiers, Game/Provider Preference) to better understand player loyalty and lifetime value.
  • Translating complex findings into compelling narratives and actionable recommendations for stakeholders in Gaming, Marketing, and Leadership; such insights will directly influence decisions on marketing spend, product strategy, and promotional calendars.
  • Developing and maintaining powerful, intuitive dashboards and reports in Looker to provide stakeholders with self-serve access to performance metrics.
  • Using LookML to define business logic, creating reusable data models, and ensuring data consistency and accuracy for all analytics built on top of our BigQuery data warehouse.

Title: Data Analyst
Description:
Description

Position at LeoVegas Group

About The Role

We are seeking a Data Analyst to support P&T initiatives by analyzing data, identifying trends, and providing actionable insights to drive decision-making.This role is a unique opportunity to venture into 'uncharted territories,' focusing on how our products work and analyzing the performance of new features to drive our commercial and product strategy forward.

As a Data Analyst, you will be a key player in investigating customer journeys and website search performance, providing data-driven recommendations that directly impact the user experience. Working in a fast-paced environment in a team based in Malta, Sweden, Poland, and Italy, you will have the chance to build projects from scratch, revamp dashboards, and find areas for improvement within our global gaming product.

YOU WILL BE RESPONSIBLE FOR:


Product Analysis: Analyze website search performance and player insights to identify "loopholes" and areas for product improvement.
Stakeholder Management: Build strong, mature relationships with product owners, management, and commercial teams to communicate data-driven recommendations.
Customer Journey Investigation: Use behavioral and product analytics to deep-dive into how users interact with our platforms.
Dashboard Development: Collaborate with the team to revamp existing dashboards and create new tracking projects centered on product analytics.
Proactive Reporting: Conduct proactive analysis of new feature performance and provide actionable insights to the wider data team.
Data Unification: Help unify previously separate analysis tasks following the merger of our behavioral and commercial functions.


OUR SUCCESSFUL CANDIDATE WILL HAVE THE FOLLOWING:

Essential Skills


Professional Experience: Minimum of 2+ years of experience in a Data Analyst role, preferably within a product-focused environment.
Advanced SQL: Strong proficiency in SQL for querying and managing complex data sets.
Analytics Expertise: Proven experience in behavioral and product analytics, specifically investigating customer journeys.
Statistical Knowledge: A solid foundation in statistics to ensure the accuracy and reliability of your findings.
Communication Skills: The ability to translate complex data into clear recommendations for non-technical stakeholders.
Proactive Attitude: A self-starter mindset with the maturity to take ownership of projects and build stakeholder trust.
Python Proficiency: Experience using Python for data manipulation or advanced analysis.


NICE TO HAVES


iGaming Experience: Previous experience within the iGaming industry is considered a significant asset.


Who We Are

At the core of LeoVegas Group is Team Leo. Our culture is our foundation and is what enables us to innovate, build, and lead as we trailblaze our way through the igaming industry.

We're a team of over 2000 innovators, initiators, and groundbreakers working in a fast-paced and agile environment across 19 offices worldwide.

Benefits


Hybrid work policy
4 weeks of Workation (T&C apply)
Well-being allowance to support your active lifestyle
Private health insurance
Discounts across a range of retailers, gyms, bars & restaurants
We offer an employee assistance program that can provide help and guidance during challenging moments.


JOIN US!

In our pride, we empower our teammates to find their roar and run with their wildest ideas. We don’t wait for things to happen; we pounce and make it happen!

Would you be a good fit for the Leo Pride - give us a roar!

**As our company working language is English, we’d like to see your CV in English, please**

Title: Data Analyst
Description:
About Sway Gaming

Sway is an iGaming company focused on building and scaling digital products for international markets. We work with small, highly capable teams and place strong emphasis on clarity, ownership, and execution. We operate in a fast-moving environment where decisions are data-informed, responsibility is clearly defined, and teams are expected to deliver with consistency and focus.




About the Role

We are seeking a skilled and analytical Data Analyst with strong SQL expertise and hands-on experience in building dashboards, analysing performance, and delivering actionable insights to stakeholders. This role is ideal for someone who is confident working autonomously, enjoys solving business problems with data, and has experience within the iGaming sector.

You will work closely with product, marketing, CRM, and operational teams, supporting data-driven decisions across the organisation.




Key Responsibilities

Build, maintain, and improve high-impact dashboards in tools such as Tableau, Power BI, or Sigma.
Analyse player behaviour, marketing performance, and operational data to identify trends and opportunities.
Write, optimise, and validate complex SQL queries for analysis and reporting.
Apply statistical methods (A/B testing, regression analysis) to support business decisions.
Support data modelling and data warehousing initiatives by understanding data structures and relationships.
Collaborate with cross-functional stakeholders, communicating insights clearly and confidently.
Ensure data accuracy, consistency, and usability across reports and analysis.
Contribute to ongoing improvements in analytics processes and data quality.




Required Skills & Experience

2+ year of experience in a dedicated data analytics role.
Expert-level SQL skills — ability to write and optimise complex queries.
Strong experience building dashboards in Tableau, Power BI, or Sigma Computing.
Hands-on experience with applied statistics (A/B testing, regression, hypothesis testing).
Understanding of data warehousing and data modelling concepts.
Ability to work independently and communicate insights effectively to non-technical stakeholders.
Industry experience in iGaming (analytics or operational roles).




Preferred Skills

Experience with Python for data analysis.
Experience with dbt, Git, or modern ELT/warehouse tools.
Experience working with Snowflake or similar cloud data warehouses.
Exposure to automations, ETL optimization, or data engineering tasks.
Familiarity with data science techniques (even if not used day-to-day).




What We Offer

Competitive salary + performance-based bonus.
Hybrid work model from our Malta office.
Private health insurance and wellness benefits.
Opportunity to work with modern tools, scalable data architecture, and a high-growth team.
International environment with strong cross-team collaboration.
A culture that values ownership, curiosity, and continuous learning.

Title: BI Analyst
Description:
About kwiff:




kwiff isn’t gambling as you know it. We’re redefining the experience with a bold, player-first approach to sports betting and casino, powered by our proprietary tech platform, fully automated sportsbook, and standout UX across web and mobile. What truly sets us apart? Supercharging: our signature feature that allows players’ odds, cash outs, and more to be supercharged at random, creating surprise wins and a thrilling betting experience. ⚡




Role overview:




We're looking for a BI Analyst to join our growing team and play a key role in building a more data-driven business. You'll work with stakeholders across the organisation to turn data into actionable insights, helping us better understand our customers, improve performance, and identify opportunities for growth.




This is a hands-on role with real impact, offering the opportunity to shape our Business Intelligence capability while supporting both operational and strategic decision-making.




We're looking for someone who is naturally curious, analytical, and proactive, with the ability to uncover meaningful insights and communicate them effectively to a range of audiences.




Core responsibilities:




Data & Analytics Leadership: Act as the primary point of contact for data and analytics across the organisation, providing expert guidance and insight to stakeholders at all levels.




BI Development: Support the development and implementation of BI tools, reporting frameworks, and data processes that enable effective analysis and decision-making.




Data Quality: Ensure the accuracy, quality, and integrity of data through robust validation, cleansing, and governance practices.




Financial Planning & Analysis: Deliver financial and operational analysis to support budgeting, forecasting, performance monitoring, and strategic planning.




Commercial Insight: Produce analysis that helps drive revenue growth, improve profitability, and identify commercial opportunities.




Business Partnering: Work closely with operational and commercial teams to understand challenges, measure performance, and provide actionable insights.




Reporting & Recommendations: Translate complex data into clear, meaningful reporting and recommendations that support both operational and strategic decision-making.




Continuous Improvement: Proactively identify emerging reporting and analytical requirements arising from changes in customer behaviour, business processes, or new data sources, and develop scalable solutions to address them.




Dashboard Development: Design, build, and maintain dashboards, reports, and self-service analytics tools that provide stakeholders with timely and reliable information.




Trend Analysis: Identify trends, risks, and opportunities within business data, presenting findings and recommendations to relevant stakeholders.




Skills and qualifications:





Strong analytical background with a degree or equivalent experience in Mathematics, Statistics, Computer Science, Engineering, Economics, or a related quantitative discipline.


Advanced SQL skills, with the ability to write efficient, scalable, and reliable queries for reporting, analysis, and data validation.


Experience in Business Intelligence or Analytics, with at least 3 years' experience delivering insights in a BI, Data, or Analytics environment.


Data management expertise, including working with multiple data sources such as relational databases, APIs, and flat files, with a strong understanding of data quality, governance, and lineage.


BI and visualisation tools, with hands-on experience building dashboards, reports, and data models using tools such as Power BI, Metabase, or similar platforms.


Commercial acumen, with the ability to identify trends, opportunities, and risks, and translate analysis into meaningful business recommendations.


Strong stakeholder management and communication skills, capable of presenting complex findings in a clear and actionable way to both technical and non-technical audiences.


Proactive and curious mindset, with a willingness to challenge assumptions, investigate root causes, and independently identify opportunities for improvement.


Experience working with operational and commercial teams, partnering closely with stakeholders to understand business requirements and drive data-led decision-making.


Interest in sports, gaming, or online casino industries would be advantageous.





Perks and Benefits:




Opportunities for career growth – in a vibrant, inclusive and fast-paced industry.

Private Healthcare – Full coverage, including dental.

Performance Bonuses – Quarterly, based on team goals and business impact.

Well-being Allowance – Budget refreshed every six months to spend on wellness or fitness.

Free Lunch – Enjoy lunch on us when working from the office.

Long Service Rewards – Travel perks to celebrate your commitment.

Team Socials & Office Culture – Regular events, office dogs, offsites, plus ping pong, darts




Why join us?




Kwiff is an equal opportunity employer. We value diversity and are committed to creating an inclusive environment for all employees.




Join us, and you’ll be part of a team that’s shaping the future of iGaming with cutting-edge technology and a culture of bold innovation!




We aim for equity at all stages of the recruitment process. Please let us know if we can do anything to make the process more accessible to you.

Title: Player Insights Analyst
Description:
Rhino Entertainment is looking for a Player Insights Analyst to join our CRM Team You will be responsible for analyzing customer data and campaign outcomes to enhance engagement at each stage of the customer journey. Reporting to the Head of CRM, you will bridge the gap between commercial insights and operational CRM by designing and implementing strategies that foster business growth and operational efficiency. You will focus heavily on data analysis to make informed decisions regarding optimal offerings, campaign budgets, and player value. By collaborating closely with internal stakeholders, the Casino team, and the Business Intelligence (BI) team, you will ensure our campaigns and game performances are fully optimized to meet regional and global audience needs.

What your role will look like:


Analyze customer data to identify trends, behaviors, and opportunities for enhancing player engagement and retention
Predict, budget, monitor, and recap the impact of CRM activities to optimize campaign spend and effectiveness across various markets
Build models and perform statistical analysis to make predictions and drive informed data-based decision-making
Assess and drive insights to optimize player value, game play activity, and promotional offerings
Collaborate with the BI and Casino teams to analyze game and provider performance across different brands and regions
Identify engagement gaps and generate improvement strategies for CRM campaigns
Utilize tools like SQL and Tableau to develop, automate, maintain, and optimize reports and dashboards
Conduct database exploration in search of hidden potential


Requirements


Experience in this or a similar role within the iGaming industry
2 to 3 years of work experience in commercial or business analysis, including building visualizations and working with complex queries or large data sets
Strong understanding of operational CRM, customer segmentation, lifecycle marketing, and retention strategies
Knowledge of SQL for reporting and experience with visualization tools such as Tableau
Highly analytical mindset with the ability to interpret complex data and create actionable strategies
Degree in Marketing, Data Analytics, Business Administration, Economics, Statistics, or a related quantitative field
Excellent communication skills, both written and verbal
Strong problem-solving skills, a can-do attitude, and the ability to work efficiently under pressure
Knowledge of gamification principles and skill in applying them to boost user engagement is a plus


Benefits

Working for Rhino includes plenty of perks.

What we offer:


Competitive salary based on your knowledge & experience
Quarterly performance bonus split on personal objectives and company performance
€500 Yearly Wellness & Mindfulness allowance to support an active lifestyle
Flexibility & a work-life blend through our Hybrid Working environment
Work from anywhere with our 30 days "overseas working"
Diverse, dynamic, fun environment with happy and helpful colleagues
Learning & Development opportunities to invest in your personal development
Fresh fruit, healthy snacks & food are available at the office everyday - and of course, good tea & coffee
Free parking, table tennis, lounge area, plenty of meeting rooms and sea views at our ergonomic office
Regular social events to strengthen long lasting relationships
Enjoy a "Me Day" on your birthday
Paid time off, including extra days for special life events
AXA Private Hospital & Dental Insurance Coverage
Form an active part of our talent acquisition, with our Referral Bonus
And to top it off, a great culture!


Join us at Rhino Entertainment and unleash your full potential in a vibrant and rewarding career! 🎉

Our Story

Rhino Entertainment Group is a fast-growing online gaming operator that was founded in 2020. Winner of the EGR Rising Operator of the Year Award 2023 and iGaming Idol Employer of The Year Award 2023, the company has a multi-brand strategy and currently operates five brands: Casino Days, Buusti Kasino, Raketti, Big Boost and Lucky Spins. Rhino group is committed to providing its players with the best possible gaming experience supported by a cutting-edge gaming platform.

Our Culture

"We are a community of passionate, committed, driven and fun-loving people who are united by a shared mission to deliver world-class entertainment to our players in a vibrant and dynamic environment. Our culture identity is rooted in our curiosity, our sense of community and unwavering drive for success. We cherish each person's unique qualities and contributions, and we encourage our team members to take ownership, think creatively, find solutions and enjoy the process of achieving our goals!"

DEIB

We are proud to have so many diverse backgrounds, cultures, perspectives, and ideas represented within our Rhino community, which fuels an authentic workplace and a strong sense of belonging. This broad spectrum helps us achieve a supportive working environment where everyone can make the best use of their skills, free from discrimination or harassment. Diversity goes hand in hand with promoting equal opportunities for all current and future employees and at Rhino, we are committed to ensure fairness in our recruitment process.

How We Work

At Rhino we very much believe in supporting flexible working arrangements! By blending working from our vibrant offices and using the latest tech to work from home, through our Hybrid Working environment, we firmly believe that we can create an effective, balanced, and collaborative working experience to help us achieve our goals!

Support trading team with data insights

Title: Data Analyst
Description:
WE ARE LOOKING FOR DATA ANALYST

At Unikie, we develop secure, real-time technology solutions for demanding environments. Our expertise spans intelligent machines, connected vehicles, industry 4.0, and secure digital platforms. We work with leading global companies to build software and data solutions that operate reliably in real-world conditions. At the core of our work is a strong engineering culture, a low-hierarchy organization, and a collaborative way of working where people are trusted to take ownership.

Location: Oulu

We are now looking for a Data Analyst to join our team and help transform field data into actionable insights that guide hardware development and improve product robustness and feature quality.

About The Project

In this project, you will work with complex, real-world field data related to hardware performance and usage. Your mission is to analyze performance trends, uncover meaningful patterns, and turn current performance observations into clear design rules that inform where future hardware development should focus.

You will be working closely with cross-functional stakeholders, helping them translate business and engineering questions into data-driven analyses. The role combines hands-on data access and wrangling, statistical analysis, and the creation of clear visualizations that make complex findings easy to understand and act on.

Key Responsibilities


Access, clean, and analyze field data using AI/LLM tools, SQL, and Python.
Develop visualizations and dashboards that clearly communicate complex findings.
Apply statistical methods to uncover insights and support data-driven decision-making.
Translate business and engineering questions into well-structured technical analyses.
Communicate results and recommendations clearly to non-technical and technical stakeholders.
Collaborate with cross-functional teams in a fast-paced, iterative environment.


Your future colleagues are waiting for you to join our international team. Working with us is uncomplicated, there’s a good team spirit, low-hierarchy and nonexistent bureaucracy. Our way of working is flexible too, many of our team work remotely from home with visits to our office from time to time when needed. This role is equally challenging, and as such we offer a very good compensation.

Required Qualifications


2+ years of experience in data analysis or data science.
Strong proficiency in Python and SQL for data access, wrangling, and analysis.
Demonstrated ability to create clear, compelling visualizations (e.g., dashboards, reports, charts).
Comfortable working with ambiguity, experimentation, and iteration.
Ability to work in a highly collaborative environment with guided autonomy and flexibility.
Strong communication skills and ability to present findings to stakeholders.


NICE TO HAVE


Familiarity with a broad range of statistical techniques and good judgment on when to apply them.
Experience with AWS tools, such as SageMaker, for data processing or model development.
Experience working with hardware-related or field performance data.
Experience using AI/LLM-based tools to accelerate analysis and reporting.

Title: Data Analyst
Description:
Are you ready to join our digital revolution journey?

At Aleph , we’re not just part of the digital advertising landscape—we’re shaping its future. Representing the world’s leading platforms, including TikTok, Amazon, Google, and nearly 55 others, we operate in 130+ markets across new and existing geographies.

Our mission is to empower advertisers and brands to unlock the full potential of these platforms' advertising capabilities. By fostering long-lasting partnerships, we create limitless opportunities for people and businesses to advertise effectively at both local and global levels.

With a presence spanning continents, Aleph offers you the chance to be part of a fast-growing, innovative team where your work makes a direct impact. If you’re ambitious, forward-thinking, and eager to thrive in a dynamic, global environment, Aleph is the perfect place to build your career.

Role Overview

We are looking for a highly analytical Data Analyst to join our Business Intelligence team. You will act as a critical "backend engine," building the data foundations and analytical models that enable data-driven decisions for both our supply (platforms) and demand (sales) sides.

You will dive deep into complex datasets, build automated reporting pipelines, and uncover actionable insights that drive our strategy with global platforms like TikTok, Microsoft, Google, X, Spotify, and others.

What You 'll Do



Extract and analyze complex data.
Query, clean, and transform large datasets from multiple sources to uncover trends, performance metrics, and market insights that support strategic decision-making across the organization.
Build and maintain BI dashboards.
Design, develop, and maintain automated dashboards and reports using Looker Studio to provide real-time visibility into business performance for executive and partner discussions. Support strategic projects.
Conduct deep-dive analyses for platform RFP responses, Quarterly Business Reviews (QBRs), and strategic initiatives that shape Aleph’s partnerships and revenue strategy. Collaborate with cross-functional stakeholders.
Work closely with strategists, sales teams, and regional leadership to define data requirements, understand their business challenges, and deliver quantitative solutions.
Translate data into actionable recommendations.
Identify performance anomalies, revenue opportunities, and market risks by packaging raw data into structured, easy-to-consume analytical models and insights.
Ensure data integrity and governance.
Establish clear data validation frameworks, optimize queries, and maintain high standards for data accuracy and documentation internally.



Who You Are



Highly analytical and problem-solving oriented.
You have a strong quantitative background and enjoy tackling complex business questions through rigorous data analysis.
Technically proficient in data tools.
You possess strong SQL skills for data extraction and manipulation, and you are highly proficient in at least one major BI visualization tool (e.g., Power BI, Looker Studio). Advanced Excel/Google Sheets skills are a must; experience with Python a strong plus. Business-minded.You don’t just pull numbers; you understand the business context behind the data and can connect your analysis directly to revenue growth, efficiency, and key opportunities.
A clear communicator. You can translate complex technical findings and data models into clear, actionable insights for non-technical stakeholders and leadership
Structured and detail-oriented. You are meticulous about data accuracy, organize your code and queries logically, and ensure all deliverables meet a high-quality standard before they reach stakeholders.
Interested in digital media or advertising.
Experience in digital advertising analytics, media agencies, ad-tech, or similar environments is a strong plus, though not strictly required.



What You'll Love About Us



We care about your individuality by giving you freedom to grow and create within the company, regardless of your position.
Work in a dynamic team in a fast-paced industry of an international company with multi-cultural teams around the world .
Learn from the best: Our state of the art workshops guarantee the latest insights into digital advertising.
In line with the EU Pay Transparency Directive, we are committed to fair and open compensation practices. For this role, the minimum gross annual base salary is 35,000 EUR.



In addition to base salary, this role includes a significant performance-based variable component, paid quarterly.

The final offer will reflect your experience, skills, and the scope of the role.

#ALEPH

Why Join Us?

Aleph is a place of many cultures, perspectives, and talents. We support each other, creating an environment of giving and receiving. We value partnership and communication because we believe it takes a group of people to achieve great things. We are energized by our ever-changing industry, our curiosity keeps us learning and seeking out new opportunities.

We stay flexible and adaptable, and believe in moving with the speed of change. We encourage everyone to strive for more, providing our internal talent with growth opportunities and the ability to learn together.

#ALEPH

We may use artificial intelligence (AI) tools to support parts of the hiring process, such as reviewing applications, analyzing resumes, or assessing responses. These tools assist our recruitment team but do not replace human judgment. Final hiring decisions are ultimately made by humans. If you would like more information about how your data is processed, please contact us.

Title: Supply Chain AI & Automation Analyst
Description:
Over 50,000 customers globally trust our end-to-end, cloud-driven networking solutions and rely on our top-rated services and support to accelerate their digital transformation efforts and deliver progress like never before and with double digit growth year over year, no provider is better positioned to deliver better outcomes on scale, than Extreme.

Come become part of something big with us! We are a global leader, with hubs in Europe, North America, South America, Asia Pacific, and the Middle East.

Supply Chain AI & Automation Analyst

About The Role

We are seeking a “Supply Chain AI & Automation Analyst” to join our Extreme Networks Global Operations organization, where you'll turn complex operational data into clear, actionable insight that drives decisions and automation across our Global Supply Chan and Order Management teams. This role sits at the intersection of analytics, AI, and supply chain strategy, partnering with cross-functional teams to identify opportunities, build analytical products, and tell the stories behind the numbers. The ideal candidate is a curious, technically strong analyst who is equally comfortable writing SQL, using AI tools, framing a business problem, managing a project, and presenting findings to stakeholders. You should be excited about the rapid evolution of AI and agentic workflows and how they can be applied to supply chain decision-making and you should be actively using AI tools in your work today.

A Note On Experience

This role requires commercially delivered analytical work. We will ask you at interview to walk through a specific project from problem through to business outcome that you owned and delivered in a professional setting. Academic or course-based projects do not qualify. If your experience is primarily from an academic programme, this role is not the right fit at this time

Key Responsibilities


Partner with supply chain stakeholders to scope problems and translate them into structured, measurable, automated workstreams.
Build and maintain python/SQL/Snowflake-based data models, dashboards, and self-service analytics for our global operations teams
Help operationalize AI and agent-based solutions to automate recurring analyses, augment decision support, and supply chain processes.
Apply statistical methods to inform data driven decisions across our global operations function.
Manage your projects end-to-end: scoping, stakeholder alignment, data discovery, modelling, validation, and rollout.
Translate analytical findings into compelling narratives (written, visual, and verbal) tailored to operational, business, and technical audiences.
Define and track KPIs that measure supply chain performance and flag interventions when metrics drift.
Contribute to ongoing improvements in data quality, documentation, and analytical standards across the team.


Required Skills & Qualifications


3-5 years experience in business analytics, data analytics, or a comparable quantitative role.
Demonstrated use of AI tools such as Claude, MS Copilot, or ChatGPT – in an analytical workflow, with a genuine appetite to develop agentic and automation capabilities in role.
Strong python skills and hands-on experience with Snowflake (or a comparable cloud data warehouse such as BigQuery, Redshift, or Databricks).
Demonstrated project management ability, owning deliverables across multiple stakeholders and timelines.
Solid grounding in descriptive statistics, and analytics fundamentals.
Strong data storytelling skills: the ability to distil complexity into clear visuals and narratives that drive action.
Supply chain domain knowledge: familiarity with demand and supply planning, inventory optimization, order management, logistics, procurement, or manufacturing operations.
Bachelor's degree in a quantitative field (e.g., Statistics, Operations Research, Engineering, Physics, Economics, Data Science, Supply Chain, Mathematics, or related).


Technical Skills Required


Programming for analytics: Python or R for modelling, automation, and analytics workflows.
BI and visualization tools: Tableau, Sigma, Power BI, Looker, or similar — both for building and for design literacy.
ERP / source systems: Exposure to Oracle Fusion Cloud or similar transactional systems.


Preferred / Additional Skills: These are skills that would meaningfully strengthen a candidate for this role:


Practical experience applying agentic workflows (e.g., LLM-based assistants, retrieval-augmented analytics, automated reasoning agents) to analytical or operational problems.
Master's or PhD in a quantitative field (Statistics, Operations Research, Engineering, Physics, Economics, Data Science, Supply Chain, Mathematics, or related).
Forecasting and optimization: time-series methods as well as other optimization techniques.
Machine learning fundamentals: classification, regression, clustering, and an understanding of when ML is and isn't the right tool.
Process improvement methodologies: Lean, Six Sigma, or equivalent structured problem-solving frameworks.
Stakeholder management and influencing: navigating ambiguity, managing competing priorities, and building trust with non-technical partners.


What Success Looks Like


Within 3 months: deeply understand our supply chain data landscape, key stakeholders, and top-priority business questions.
Within 6 months: deliver at least one high-impact analytical product or AI-enabled workflow that changes how a decision is made.
Within 12 months: become a trusted analytics partner to supply chain stakeholders and a contributor to the broader analytics team's standards and practices.


Salary Is Based On Qualifications And Experience Between 60-70K
