Over 50,000 customers globally trust our end-to-end, cloud-driven networking solutions and rely on our top-rated services and support to accelerate their digital transformation efforts and deliver progress like never before and with double digit growth year over year, no provider is better positioned to deliver better outcomes on scale, than Extreme.

Come become part of something big with us! We are a global leader, with hubs in Europe, North America, South America, Asia Pacific, and the Middle East.

Supply Chain AI & Automation Analyst

About The Role

We are seeking a “Supply Chain AI & Automation Analyst” to join our Extreme Networks Global Operations organization, where you'll turn complex operational data into clear, actionable insight that drives decisions and automation across our Global Supply Chan and Order Management teams. This role sits at the intersection of analytics, AI, and supply chain strategy, partnering with cross-functional teams to identify opportunities, build analytical products, and tell the stories behind the numbers. The ideal candidate is a curious, technically strong analyst who is equally comfortable writing SQL, using AI tools, framing a business problem, managing a project, and presenting findings to stakeholders. You should be excited about the rapid evolution of AI and agentic workflows and how they can be applied to supply chain decision-making and you should be actively using AI tools in your work today.

A Note On Experience

This role requires commercially delivered analytical work. We will ask you at interview to walk through a specific project from problem through to business outcome that you owned and delivered in a professional setting. Academic or course-based projects do not qualify. If your experience is primarily from an academic programme, this role is not the right fit at this time

Key Responsibilities

  • Partner with supply chain stakeholders to scope problems and translate them into structured, measurable, automated workstreams.
  • Build and maintain python/SQL/Snowflake-based data models, dashboards, and self-service analytics for our global operations teams
  • Help operationalize AI and agent-based solutions to automate recurring analyses, augment decision support, and supply chain processes.
  • Apply statistical methods to inform data driven decisions across our global operations function.
  • Manage your projects end-to-end: scoping, stakeholder alignment, data discovery, modelling, validation, and rollout.
  • Translate analytical findings into compelling narratives (written, visual, and verbal) tailored to operational, business, and technical audiences.
  • Define and track KPIs that measure supply chain performance and flag interventions when metrics drift.
  • Contribute to ongoing improvements in data quality, documentation, and analytical standards across the team.

Required Skills & Qualifications

  • 3-5 years experience in business analytics, data analytics, or a comparable quantitative role.
  • Demonstrated use of AI tools such as Claude, MS Copilot, or ChatGPT – in an analytical workflow, with a genuine appetite to develop agentic and automation capabilities in role.
  • Strong python skills and hands-on experience with Snowflake (or a comparable cloud data warehouse such as BigQuery, Redshift, or Databricks).
  • Demonstrated project management ability, owning deliverables across multiple stakeholders and timelines.
  • Solid grounding in descriptive statistics, and analytics fundamentals.
  • Strong data storytelling skills: the ability to distil complexity into clear visuals and narratives that drive action.
  • Supply chain domain knowledge: familiarity with demand and supply planning, inventory optimization, order management, logistics, procurement, or manufacturing operations.
  • Bachelor's degree in a quantitative field (e.g., Statistics, Operations Research, Engineering, Physics, Economics, Data Science, Supply Chain, Mathematics, or related).

Technical Skills Required

  • Programming for analytics: Python or R for modelling, automation, and analytics workflows.
  • BI and visualization tools: Tableau, Sigma, Power BI, Looker, or similar — both for building and for design literacy.
  • ERP / source systems: Exposure to Oracle Fusion Cloud or similar transactional systems.

Preferred / Additional Skills: These are skills that would meaningfully strengthen a candidate for this role:

  • Practical experience applying agentic workflows (e.g., LLM-based assistants, retrieval-augmented analytics, automated reasoning agents) to analytical or operational problems.
  • Master's or PhD in a quantitative field (Statistics, Operations Research, Engineering, Physics, Economics, Data Science, Supply Chain, Mathematics, or related).
  • Forecasting and optimization: time-series methods as well as other optimization techniques.
  • Machine learning fundamentals: classification, regression, clustering, and an understanding of when ML is and isn't the right tool.
  • Process improvement methodologies: Lean, Six Sigma, or equivalent structured problem-solving frameworks.
  • Stakeholder management and influencing: navigating ambiguity, managing competing priorities, and building trust with non-technical partners.

What Success Looks Like

  • Within 3 months: deeply understand our supply chain data landscape, key stakeholders, and top-priority business questions.
  • Within 6 months: deliver at least one high-impact analytical product or AI-enabled workflow that changes how a decision is made.
  • Within 12 months: become a trusted analytics partner to supply chain stakeholders and a contributor to the broader analytics team's standards and practices.

Salary Is Based On Qualifications And Experience Between 60-70K

Title: Digital Analyst - Product
Description:
Patrianna is a fast-scaling product development company headquartered in Gibraltar, with a dynamic, global team powering our growth. We operate at the intersection of technology and entertainment, building innovative solutions that shape the future of social gaming and deliver outstanding experiences to millions of players worldwide.

We're driven by speed, ambition, and bold ideas. At our core, we're product creators and problem-solvers who thrive in a high-performance environment. We're looking for exceptional talent—smart, adaptable, and motivated individuals eager to make an impact, scale business functions at pace, and continuously improve.

The Role

Transform data into actionable insights that drive product decisions and enhance player experiences across our gaming platforms.

What You Will Be Doing


Guide product team members through complex data landscapes, providing valuable insights that support critical decision-making
Conduct deep-dive analyses of user behavior, app performance, and product features to boost player acquisition, engagement, and retention
Ensure top-tier behavioral data collection by collaborating with analytics developers on tracking implementation and issue resolution
Develop and maintain dashboards, reports, and alerting systems for real-time monitoring of key conversion metrics
Present insights clearly and effectively to stakeholders across the organization
Debug data issues and optimize tracking mechanisms to maintain data integrity


What We Are Looking For


Strong analytical and problem-solving abilities with exceptional attention to detail
Critical thinking mindset with proactive approach and high reliability
Solid SQL skills for querying and manipulating databases
Experience creating detailed reports and dashboards using tools like Looker or Tableau
Familiarity with digital analytics platforms such as Snowplow and GA4, plus tracking mechanisms and data layers
Experience with data modeling in Dataform and session replay/autotracking platforms is a bonus


Why you will love it

You'll be at the heart of product innovation, where your insights directly shape gaming experiences for millions of players. In our high-growth environment, you'll have the freedom to take ownership of impactful projects while collaborating with brilliant minds who value your expertise. Every analysis you deliver will drive real business decisions and help us stay ahead in the competitive gaming landscape.

Equal Opportunities

We hire based on skills, drive, and ideas—nothing else. Your background, gender, age, race, ethnicity, disability, sexual orientation, religion, neurodiversity, or educational path will never be a barrier to joining us. We also welcome candidates from non-traditional career journeys and value diverse perspectives that challenge conventional thinking. Diversity fuels our innovation, collaboration, and growth, and we're committed to creating an environment where everyone can contribute their best work and thrive.

Title: Data Analyst
Description:
Job description

We build consumer products that grow fast. You'll build the data function that keeps up.




In 2026, the Financial Times named Wire Salad one of Europe's fastest-growing companies. We build brands and apps across fitness, skincare, human health, and pet health - products used by real people, every day, across many markets.




**This is our first dedicated data hire.** 

You'll report directly to the Head of Product, inherit an existing stack (BigQuery, Airbyte, Looker, Statsig) while having a clean slate to build the data function the way you think it should be built. There are no politics around who owns which metric. Just a fast-growing company that knows it needs great data - and is betting on you to deliver it.




The problem you're solving:

Speed creates a specific kind of mess. Experiments run in parallel across four verticals. Teams hold strong opinions on different numbers for the same thing. Your job is to keep the scoreboard honest, catch what's working before anyone else does, and make sure every team is reading from the same page.




What you'll actually do:

Build the data backbone: pipelines in Airbyte, models in BigQuery, dashboards in Looker, experiments in Statsig. Clean inputs, consistent definitions, documented logic - the foundation everything else runs on.
Own the metrics that matter: LTV:CAC, ROAS, LTV, retention, funnel conversion, payback periods, channel attribution. Define them once, get everyone aligned, and put an end to "wait, which number is right?"
Go proactive, not reactive: don't wait for someone to ask what's broken - build AI-assisted anomaly detection that flags it first. Spot the channel quietly leaking spend at 3am. Catch the cohort converting 40% better and explain why.
Democratise the data: not every question should land in your inbox. Build self-serve dashboards people actually use, write docs people actually read, and raise data literacy across the company.
Use AI like it's 2026, because it is: if a tool makes you 10x faster or sharper, we want you using it - and teaching the rest of us.
Raise the bar: question inconsistencies, chase down edge cases, document what you build, and hold everyone (including yourself) to a high standard of accuracy.




Requirements:

2+ years in analytics, analytics engineering, or BI - ideally in a fast-moving environment (ecommerce, consumer apps, growth-stage tech).
Strong SQL. CTEs, window functions, complex joins, query optimisation. You can look at a slow query and figure out why it's slow.
Data engineering instincts. You've built or maintained pipelines, thought about schemas, written tests, and genuinely care about data reliability. dbt experience is a strong plus.
Familiarity with a modern stack. Ideally BigQuery, Looker, Airbyte, and Statsig - but we care about fundamentals over tool-logo matching. Equivalent experience transfers.
Commercial brain. You read a chart and your next thought is "what does this mean for the business?" You push back on vanity metrics.
Ownership, for real. You notice something is off and fix it without being asked. You proactively follow through. You ship.
AI-native mindset. You're already using AI tools to accelerate your work, and curious about where to push further.
Fluent English, written and spoken.




Why work at WIRE SALAD?

Additional performance-based bonuses
Hybrid work model – balance between office and remote work
Company provided work computer (MacBook or Windows)
Additional health insurance after the probation period
Home office budget after the probation period
Team-building events & annual workation
Perks.lt account with numerous discounts and partner offers
Commuting expenses covered by the company




Financial Remuneration:

The salary range is 2500-3000EUR NETO

Title: Business Intelligence Analyst
Description:
About MBRYONICS

MBRYONICS is at the forefront of free space optical communications. Our product portfolio covers a wide range of emerging technologies from photonic integrated circuits to advanced optical systems and embedded software solutions. Our optical communications systems are integrated and deployed in satellite communications systems. The work is exciting and challenging and is at the forefront of the technology domains. As part of our expansion, we are currently looking for a suitably motivated - Business Intelligence Analyst - to join our group.

As the BI Analyst at MBRYONICS, you will play a pivotal role in supporting the delivery of critical components and features within our mission to build the Internet in Space. You will be responsible for working with both our Strategic Bid team, the Program Management Office, Finance, Operations and the Research and Development teams to support the delivery of solutions to partners and end-customers that contribute to our overall success.

What You'll Do


Data Analysis and Reporting: Collate and analyse data from several MB systems such as sales, planning, finance, operations and research and development. Respond to questions on the data from the Head of PMO by providing relevant reports and visualization/dashboards.
Collaborate: Collaborate with departments to systematise the identification, collation, integration and aggregation of key business data to provide business insights
Manage: Create and maintain a centralized database for the purposes of BI reporting
Automate: Assist the Head of PMO to automate data collection, integration and aggregation
Communicate: Communicate findings verbally, through written reports and visually through dashboards using tools such as Tableau and Power BI.
Maintain Standards and Processes: Maintain the PMO repository of relevant project standards, processes, forecasts and change records.
Innovate: Work with the Head of PMO, Project Managers and Product Owners to innovate on data collection, maintenance and reporting


Who You Are


Analysis and Reporting: Experienced in database management and analysing data from project, resource and financial systems and reporting on key outcomes in a concise and clear manner to provide business insights and decision-grade information.
Technical/Software: Experienced with one or more business intelligence and visualisation tools such as Tableau, Power BI and Python/R
Communication: Excellent verbal and written communication skills, with the ability to analyse complex data sets and formulate these into reports and dashboards that provide the right level of information for the audience and that enable timely decision-making.
Collaboration: Able to foster great collaboration and working relationships across departments to provide the business with timely business insights
Problem-solving: Strong analytical, critical thinking, problem-solving, and decision-making abilities.
Technical Acumen: Able to understand complex technical concepts related to optical communications, satellite systems, or high-speed data transfer.
Strategic Thinking: Able to connect data trends to overarching strategic objectives and company vision.


Desirable Skills & Experience


Third-level qualification in data analysis or related discipline
5+ years of experience in data analysis and visualization/reporting role, preferably in the telecommunications, space or technology industry or regulated manufacturing.


Nice to Have


Familiarity with ECSS (European Co-operation for Space Standards), Aerospace standards, space technologies and space industry trends
Experience working in a research and development environment.
Background in a fast-paced, high-tech environment.


What's In It For You

Career Growth: Contribute to a world-class space-age communication program while developing your leadership skills in a state-of-the-art manufacturing environment

Collaboration: Work with talented professionals across disciplines to drive impactful solutions

Innovation: Be part of a team pioneering advancements in manufacturing engineering for cutting-edge FSO systems

Work-Life Balance: Enjoy flexible work schedules and a comprehensive benefits package

If you're ready to take your career to new heights and drive the future of space-age communication, apply now and join the MBRYONICS team as our Business Intelligence Analyst .

Diversity, Inclusion and Equal Opportunities

Core ethical values, diversity and inclusion are invaluable to MBRYONICS’ business success. We recognise that the broad perspectives that result from recruiting, developing and retaining a globally diverse workforce promotes innovation and helps us succeed as a business enterprise. It is the policy and practice of the Company to provide equal opportunities to all employees and applicants. The Company will not tolerate discrimination on the basis of gender, race, religion, sexual orientation, gender identity, civil status, family status, disability, age, or membership of the Traveller community.

Title: Data Analyst
Description:
About Euromonitor:

Euromonitor International leads the world in data analytics and research into markets, industries, economies and consumers. We provide truly global insight and data on thousands of products and services; we are the first destination for organisations seeking growth. With our guidance, our clients can make bold, strategic decisions with confidence.

The Analytics team is hiring a Data Analyst to help improve the analytics processes and code underpinning Euromonitor's internal Forecast Model, supporting updates to our Passport data.

The role requires a strong programming foundation in Python or R (preferred), an understanding of cloud computing, and a genuine interest in data analytics. Alongside internal forecasting work, the role may also support client consulting projects, applying analytical solutions from problem definition through to insight delivery.

Key Responsibilities


Develop and enhance code for data processing, mathematical transformations, and statistical analysis
Contribute to peer reviews and solution design discussions
Translate analytical outputs into clear conclusions
Stay up to date with modern programming and cloud technologies
Maintain clear documentation and share best practices across the team


Requirements


Advanced Python knowledge
Proficiency with R (preferred)
Advanced knowledge of SQL
Ability to automate complex data analysis processes
Understanding of data flows, migrations, ETLs, pipelines, and other similar tools
Understanding of process scaling and parallelization
Understanding on how to write a production-ready code
A knowledge in econometrics, statistics and economics.
Knowledge of Dockers, Rest API, JSON data format would be an advantage
Knowledge of cloud computing services platforms providers (Azure, GCS, other)
Experience in SPARQL, JavaScript, HTML or similar would be an advantage
A genuine interest in Artificial Intelligence and related fields like data science and Machine learning would be an advantage.


Nice to have:


M.A. degree in computer science, statistics, econometrics, data science, mathematics, economics, physics or similar field. Highly skilled candidates with a B.A. will also be considered.


Benefits


Flexible work time with the perfect work/life balance
Competitive salary with wide variety of benefits like longer holidays, additional health insurance, social responsibility initiatives you can take part in, office perks
Intelligent and fun colleagues working in a constructive and inspiring atmosphere
Salary: EUR 30,000 - EUR 35,000


Why work for Euromonitor

Our values


We act with integrity
We are curious about the world
We are stronger together
We seek to empower
We find strength in diversity


International: not only do we have a very multinational workforce in each office but we are all dealing with our 16 offices worldwide on a daily basis. With 16 offices globally there are regular opportunities for international transfer.

Hardworking but sociable: our staff know how to work hard but also how to enjoy themselves! We pride ourselves on creating an appropriate work-life balance, with flexible hours and regular socialising including frequent after work meet ups, summer and Christmas parties and a whole range of sports and other groups to be involved with.

Committed to making a difference: We think that people are looking for something worthwhile in a company beyond the workplace. Our extensive Corporate Social Responsibility Programme gives each member of staff two volunteering days a year in addition to holidays. It sees us reaching out into the local community with our mentoring, group volunteering, and fundraising initiatives as well as supporting international charities through our website sales, matching staff sponsorship fundraising, and carbon offsetting all our flights, amongst many other activities.

Excellent benefits: we offer highly competitive salaries, healthcare insurance, food vouchers, saving fund, plus generous holiday allowances and in many offices a Core Hours policy allowing flexible start and finish times to each day.

Opportunities to grow: we offer extensive training and development opportunities at all levels. The vast majority of our managers and directors have been promoted from within and many have moved across departments as well as upwards. We pride ourselves on identifying and rewarding talent.

Equal Employment Opportunity Statement: Euromonitor International does not discriminate in employment on the basis of race, colour, religion, sex, national origin, political affiliation, sexual orientation, gender identity, marital status, disability and genetic information, age, membership in an employee organization, or other non-merit factor.

International: not only do we have a very multinational workforce in each office but we are all dealing with our 16 offices worldwide on a daily basis. With 16 offices globally there are regular opportunities for international transfer.

Hardworking but sociable: our staff know how to work hard but also how to enjoy themselves! We pride ourselves on creating an appropriate work-life balance, with flexible hours and regular socialising including frequent after work meet ups, summer and Christmas parties and a whole range of sports and other groups to be involved with.

Committed to making a difference: We think that people are looking for something worthwhile in a company beyond the workplace. Our extensive Corporate Social Responsibility Programme gives each member of staff two volunteering days a year in addition to holidays. It sees us reaching out into the local community with our mentoring, group volunteering, and fundraising initiatives as well as supporting international charities through our website sales, matching staff sponsorship fundraising, and carbon offsetting all our flights, amongst many other activities.

Excellent benefits: we offer highly competitive salaries, healthcare insurance, food vouchers, saving fund, plus generous holiday allowances and in many offices a Core Hours policy allowing flexible start and finish times to each day.

Opportunities to grow: we offer extensive training and development opportunities at all levels. The vast majority of our managers and directors have been promoted from within and many have moved across departments as well as upwards. We pride ourselves on identifying and rewarding talent.

Equal Employment Opportunity Statement: Euromonitor International does not discriminate in employment on the basis of race, colour, religion, sex, national origin, political affiliation, sexual orientation, gender identity, marital status, disability and genetic information, age, membership in an employee organization, or other non-merit factor.

Title: Data Analyst - Internal Audit
Description:
Company Description

Wise is a global technology company, building the best way to move and manage the world’s money.

Min fees. Max ease. Full speed.

Whether people and businesses are sending money to another country, spending abroad, or making and receiving international payments, Wise is on a mission to make their lives easier and save them money.

As part of our team, you will be helping us create an entirely new network for the world's money.

For everyone, everywhere.

Job Description

More about our mission and what we offer.

We are looking for a Data Analyst to join the Internal Audit branch of the Compliance & Internal Audit Analytics team at Wise.

You’ll partner with Internal Audit (IA) to develop and implement the analytics strategy across the function.

Internal Audit serves as an independent 3rd line of defence that oversees and provides an independent assessment and reasonable assurance on the effectiveness of the company’s corporate governance structure + internal controls.

The IA Analytics team supports IA by using analytics to provide more coverage, efficiency and accuracy to audit testing, as well as building innovative tools and using automation to support continuous testing and other IA activities beyond the annual audit plan.

This is an IC2 role. For more information on our Analytics Career Map and levelling structure, click here.

What You’ll Be Doing


Partnering with stakeholders across the IA function covering all regions, products and domains of the business (including Financial Crime, Ops, Risk, Finance and Technology)
Identifying & implementing opportunities to do the most impactful analytics within audits and influencing and upskilling your partners in IA to do the same
Working with analytics, engineering, and business specialists to seek out and understand interesting and varied datasets, perform analytics using modern approaches, and develop a strong understanding of operations at Wise
Championing innovation and data-led insights, not just within audits but across all IA activities where analytics can add value
Working on automation and tooling projects to increase efficiency across IA including workflow automation, continuous auditing, leveraging AI, and building MI dashboards


Qualifications

What you’ll bring:


3+ years of experience in data analytics
1+ years of data analytics experience within an audit, risk, or compliance related role
Advanced stakeholder management skills; you know how to leverage multiple different teams to deliver high-impact change including analytics, engineering, and business specialists
Extensive experience using SQL; you can build and maintain complex data sources and can leverage visualisation tools to surface insights


Nice To Have


Experience of working in regulated environments, Fintech or Financial Services.
Knowledge of financial crime.
Experience using dbt, Superset, or Looker.
Knowledge of advanced analytics techniques, including the use of Python and visualisation techniques.


Additional Information

For everyone, everywhere. We're people building money without borders — without judgement or prejudice, too. We believe teams are strongest when they are diverse, equitable and inclusive.

We're proud to have a truly international team, and we celebrate our differences.

Inclusive teams help us live our values and make sure every Wiser feels respected, empowered to contribute towards our mission and able to progress in their careers.

If you want to find out more about what it's like to work at Wise visit Wise.Jobs.

Keep up to date with life at Wise by following us on LinkedIn and Instagram.

Title: Data Analyst – Marketing & Campaign Analytics
Description:
If you need support in completing the application or if you require a different format of this document, please get in touch with at UKI.recruitment@tcs.com with the subject line: “Application Support Request”.




Careers at TCS: It means more

TCS is a purpose-led transformation company, built on belief. We do not just help businesses to transform through technology. We support them in making a meaningful difference to the people and communities they serve - our clients include some of the biggest brands in the UK and worldwide. For you, it means more to make an impact that matters, through challenging projects which demand ambitious innovation and thought leadership.

Work closely with the range of teams within the business to bring ideas to life.
Develop and maintain a unified strategy that aligns operational efficiency with marketing and growth objectives.
Work with customers and create high-impact customer experiences for our business.




The Role

We are seeking a Data Analyst to support Marketing and Distribution teams within the Financial Services sector, with a particular focus on Asset Management, Life Insurance, and Retirement. This role is hands‑on and delivery‑focused, supporting marketing campaigns through data extraction, audience segmentation, campaign list pulls, reporting, and performance analysis in a regulated environment.







Your responsibilities:

Support end to end marketing campaign delivery by providing accurate and timely data outputs, including:

-- Campaign list pulls and audience extracts

-- Segment creation and validation

-- Post campaign response and performance analysis

-- Ensuring all activity complies with data governance, and internal usage controls.




Use SQL and Python to extract, transform, and prepare data from enterprise data platforms to support campaign execution, targeting activities, and ongoing marketing analytics.
Work closely with marketing, CRM, and distribution teams to translate campaign briefs into clear data requirements, validating logic and outputs prior to campaign deployment.
Develop and maintain Power BI dashboards and reports to support:

-- Campaign and channel performance tracking

-- Audience and segment analysis

-- Trend and insight reporting for marketing stakeholders

-- Reports should be clear, accurate, and suitable for non technical business users




Access and analyze data hosted on AWS and Azure platforms, working within established cloud, security, and compliance frameworks typical of Financial Services organizations.
Support the application of segmentation and propensity outputs produced by advanced analytics or data science teams, helping operationalize insights into campaign execution.
Assist with data inputs and reporting related to marketing technology and signal based platforms (e.g. 6Sense, Bombora, or similar), without responsibility for model development.
Perform basic data quality checks and validation, identifying inconsistencies or anomalies and working with data engineering or platform teams to resolve issues.
Maintain awareness of regulatory and organizational constraints associated with customer and advisor data, including appropriate handling of sensitive financial information




Your Profile

Essential skills/knowledge/experience:

This role is suited to candidates with approximately 3–6 years of experience in Data Analytics, Marketing Analytics, or a related discipline, ideally gained within Financial Services or another regulated industry.
The position will appeal to individuals who enjoy hands‑on campaign support, working closely with marketing teams, and turning business requirements into reliable, well‑governed data outputs, while continuing to build technical skills and industry knowledge.




Rewards & Benefits

TCS is consistently voted a Top Employer in the UK and globally. Our competitive salary packages feature pension, health care, life assurance, laptop and access to extensive training resources and discounts within the larger Tata network.

We offer health & wellness initiatives and sports events; we are the proud sponsor of the London Marathon and partner with our local communities in Ireland.




Diversity, Inclusion and Wellbeing

Tata Consultancy Services UK&I is committed to meeting the accessibility needs of all individuals in accordance with the Ireland Employment Equality Acts 1998-2011 (as amended) and the Equal Status Acts 2000-2012 (as amended).




We welcome and embrace diversity in race, nationality, ethnicity, disability, neurodiversity, gender identity, age, physical ability, gender reassignment, sexual orientation. We are a disability inclusive employer and encourage disabled people to apply for this role.

As a Disability Confident Employer, we offer an interview to applicants with disabilities or long-term conditions who meet the minimum criteria for the role. Please email us at UKI.recruitment@tcs.com if you would like to opt in.




If you are an applicant who needs any adjustments to the application process or interview, please contact us at UKI.recruitment@tcs.com with the subject line: “Adjustment Request” or email rebecca.mclelland@tcs.com to request an adjustment. We welcome requests prior to you completing the application and at any stage of the recruitment process.




Beware of Fraudulent offers

This is to notify you that TCS does not ask for any sort of payment or security deposit from candidates at any stage of the recruitment process. The firm never sends out job offers from free internet email services like Gmail, Yahoo Mail, and so on. TCS has not authorised any third-party company to collect money on their behalf. As a vigilant job seeker, beware of fraudulent recruitment activity and protect your interests! You can write to UKI.recruitment@tcs.com to report any fraudulent activity.




Due to the high volume of applications, we will be unable to contact each applicant individually on the status of their application. If you have not received a direct response within 30 days, then it should be deemed unsuccessful on this occasion.




Join us and do more of what matters. Apply online now.

Title: Digital Analyst Technical
Description:
We are B2Spin, and we are dedicated to delivering the best Free-to-Play Social Casino and gaming experiences by creating captivating, engaging, and unique products that are accessible to everyone. We have built a team of talented professionals from all over the globe, who are working to continually improve the experiences of our players. We are not looking to just be part of the industry, but to revolutionize it.

The Role

You'll be the tracking mastermind ensuring our data collection is bulletproof across web and mobile platforms.

What You Will Be Doing


Partner with developers, product, marketing and data teams on tracking strategy, implementation and quality assurance
Configure, optimize and audit analytics tools while setting up monitoring alerts to maintain data accuracy
Guide BI specialists through client-side data complexities for smooth integration
Build comprehensive reports and dashboards that turn data into actionable insights
Execute tracking planning and quality control across both web and app environments
Stay on top of privacy regulations and their impact on our analytics ecosystem


What We Are Looking For


Advanced hands-on experience with analytics tools like Google Analytics, Firebase or Snowplow
Strong grasp of Tag Management Systems (client-side and server-side)
Solid SQL skills and data visualization capabilities
Sharp analytical mindset with exceptional attention to detail
Experience with tracking implementation and QA processes
Understanding of privacy regulations in digital analytics


Why you will love it

Working with us means diving deep into the exciting world of gaming analytics where your technical expertise directly impacts millions of players' experiences. You'll collaborate with brilliant minds across the globe, have access to cutting-edge tools, and play a crucial role in revolutionizing the social casino industry. Plus, you'll be part of a team that values innovation, precision, and the thrill of turning complex data into game-changing insights.

We hire based on skills, drive, and ideas—nothing else. Your background, gender, age, race, ethnicity, disability, sexual orientation, religion, neurodiversity, or educational path will never be a barrier to joining us. We also welcome candidates from non-traditional career journeys and value diverse perspectives that challenge conventional thinking. Diversity fuels our innovation, collaboration, and growth, and we're committed to creating an environment where everyone can contribute their best work and thrive.

Work closely with product teams to analyze feature performance and player behavior. You'll run experiments, analyze results, and provide data-driven recommendations for product improvements.

Analyze player funnels and journeys
Support product roadmap with data

Title: Product Analyst
HTML:
Mayflower is a technology company that alters the entertainment industry to a new level of perception and engagement.


We are building the future of live entertainment. We believe that by providing people with the best and highest-quality live streaming experience in entertainment, we can tackle the world’s “problems” better, together!


We are seeking an experienced Product Analyst to join our dynamic team in Limassol office.


Job responsibilities

  • Generate actionable insights based on data to support product and business decisions;
  • Design, run, and analyze A/B tests end-to-end - from hypothesis to implementation and reporting;
  • Define and develop new business and product metrics to measure performance and identify opportunities;
  • Continuously monitor key metrics and detect anomalies or trends that require attention;
  • Conduct deep-dive analyses to identify problem areas and uncover growth opportunities;
  • Write clean and efficient SQL and Python code to extract, transform, and analyze data;
  • Collaborate on implementing new tracking events and properties to improve product analytics coverage;
  • Work closely with CPO, Product Managers, Analysts, Product Designers, and Data Scientists to drive data-informed decisions.


Job requirements

  • 3+ years of experience in analytics;
  • Strong understanding of A/B testing and solid knowledge of statistics;
  • Excellent SQL skills - ability to write complex queries, automate reports, build dashboards and data visualizations;
  • Proficient in Python for data analysis, visualization, and automation;
  • Experience working with BI tools, especially Tableau (other tools like Power BI or Amplitude are also acceptable);
  • Deep understanding of metrics and the ability to define them from scratch, grounded in product logic;
  • Strong product thinking - ability to connect data insights with business goals;


Qualifications that can be a plus:

  • ML expertise: cluster analysis, regression models, etc;
  • Airflow;
  • GitLab;
  • dbt;


Conditions

We know that great talent deserves great conditions, so here's what you can expect when joining us:

  • EU-based employment contract and a 3-year Cyprus work visa with full support for your relocation and visa processes, including assistance for your family.
  • Full relocation package: flights to Limassol for you and your family, a company-covered apartment for the first month, and full relocation support to make your move smooth and hassle-free.
  • Transparent performance reviews twice a year, with bonus opportunities and salary adjustments.
  • Private medical insurance for you and your family, a corporate mobile plan (unlimited in Cyprus with roaming included), and interest-free support for car purchases.
  • Mindfulness & well-being support, including psychological assistance with 50% coverage.
  • 50% coverage of school and kindergarten fees for your children.
  • Fully covered sports benefits, and also access to in-house electric scooters and bike rentals, and cycling purchase compensation.
  • Investment in your growth: paid language courses and access to suited-for-you development programs, including conferences, training programs, and coaching to support your professional journey.
  • A culture of recognition: a peer reward program to celebrate your contributions.
  • A fully equipped office in Limassol’s city center, with everything you need for deep work and collaboration.
  • Free catering in the office and an in-house coffee bar with high-quality drinks and a health bar stocked with nutritious snacks.
  • A strong engineering culture: international teams, corporate events, team buildings, and hackathons—because great work happens in great communities.


Recruitment process

  1. HR interview (45 min);
  2. Technical interview (1,5 hours);
  3. Final interview (1 hour).

  • Uncover customer preferences and market trends that inform our product strategy

What we are looking for

  • Strong analytical and problem-solving abilities with sharp attention to detail
  • Solid SQL skills for database querying and data manipulation
  • Hands-on experience with data analytics and visualization tools
  • Practical knowledge of A/B testing methodologies and statistical analysis
  • Familiarity with digital analytics platforms, tracking mechanisms, and data layers
  • Proactive mindset with critical thinking skills and high reliability

Why you will love it

You'll be at the heart of product innovation, where your insights directly shape experiences for millions of players worldwide. Working with cutting-edge analytics tools and a collaborative global team, you'll have the freedom to experiment, learn, and drive meaningful impact in the fast-evolving social gaming industry.

Equal Opportunities Statement

We hire based on skills, drive, and ideas—nothing else. Your background, gender, age, race, ethnicity, disability, sexual orientation, religion, neurodiversity, or educational path will never be a barrier to joining us. We also welcome candidates from non-traditional career journeys and value diverse perspectives that challenge conventional thinking. Diversity fuels our innovation, collaboration, and growth, and we're committed to creating an environment where everyone can contribute their best work and thrive.

Title: Quantitative/Data Analyst
HTML:
Humbility is an algorithmic cryptocurrency trading firm operating across DeFi and CeFi on multiple chains and exchanges. We build low-latency, market-neutral arbitrage systems in a highly competitive environment.


Our team of 50+ Software Engineers and Data Analysts thrives on solving complex mathematical and logical challenges. Winning at the microsecond level means constantly pushing the boundaries of trading logic and code performance.


We're looking for quantitative researchers interested in mathematics and trading logic optimization. Join us!


In this position, you will:

  • Develop and improve our trading logic;
  • Develop tools to monitor our trading performance;
  • Dive deep into latencies to the microsecond level;
  • Analyze competitors' trading logic and latencies.


We expect you to have:

  • At least two years of working experience with data (Python and SQL experience is a must);
  • Strong mathematical skills and passion for solving complex problems;
  • Ability and motivation to learn new concepts quickly;
  • Proficiency in English and Lithuanian languages;
  • Availability to work on-site at our Simbiocity Vilnius office.


You will stand out if you have (not required):

  • Achievements showcasing your mathematical skills;
  • Experience within trading domain.


We promise:

  • A culture of excellence. Join a team of top-tier engineers and analysts solving hard problems in a fast-moving market.
  • Get rewarded for impact. Yearly bonuses and performance reviews tied directly to your contributions.
  • Connect, compete, unwind. Regular team-building activities, company events, sports, chess tournaments, game nights, and more.
  • Health comes first. Private health insurance included.
  • The little things, covered. Fully stocked kitchen and free parking at the office.


The salary range for this position is €3,500 – €10,000 gross per month.

Title: Credit / Data Analyst
Description:
Credit / Data Analyst (*Currently Located in Malta & Hold an EU Passport)




We are seeking an experienced analyst, with a technical background, to shape all aspects of credit strategy. Responsible for building credit risk strategies for our credit card portfolio. Developing an infrastructure of data, reporting & analytics, to underpin credit optimisation & support product growth.




Responsibilities:




Take ownership of credit risk strategies at both customer acquisition and customer management stages. Including cut-off setting, initial-line-assignment, affordability, authorisation & line management (increase/ decrease) programs.
Accountable for leading ad hoc and regular analytics on key risk indicators; tracking variance to plan and informing above noted strategy setting.
Develop an improved management information suite, covering risk appetite. With a dynamic capability to review live tests and interrogate trends.
Manage the credit risk messaging across the bank; opportunity to discuss on-going performance and propose value add strategies to bank committee(s).
Work closely with various bank teams, including Finance, Operations and Decision Science areas.
Generate enhanced documentation outlining Credit Risk Processes, including consideration of (changing) Regulatory Standard.
Input into Collections & Recoveries, and Fraud Strategies.
Facilitate growth plans by delivering effective & timely Credit Solutions, with consideration of continuous improvement and customer experience.




Skills / Experience:




2+ years’ experience within an Analytical role (Credit & Financial Services is preferred but not required).
Bachelor’s or Master’s degree in a Quantitative/ Numerical Discipline.
Problem solving and analytical (Useful: SAS/ SQL/ Python or similar).
Data visualization and dashboarding (useful: Tableau/ Power BI)
Exceptional communication and organisation skills.
Ability to manage time and prioritise work.
Pro-active outlook and desire to innovate.
Experience with credit cards or other retail lending products (e.g. loans, current accounts or mortgages) is preferred.




Excellent Salary + Benefits + Bonus




We Are Looking For You!




Apply via LinkedIn or via this Recruitment Bamboo Link:




https://novumbank.bamboohr.com/careers/76?source=aWQ9MzM%3D




Our Recruitment / HR-team will reach out to you directly.

Title: Data Analyst - Process Management (12 Month FTC) | PGIM Ireland
Description:
12 Month FTC

Hybrid Role (2 days on-site in Letterkenny)

PGIM Ireland are seeking to hire a Data Analyst - Process Management for PGIM Real Estate Loan Services who are responsible for the servicing and asset management of commercial loans originated by PGIM Real Estate (PGIM RE) outside of the United States. Irish hours will apply from Monday to Friday, however there is a requirement to be flexible, specifically during peak times. Hours are subject to change in line with role requirements.

What You Can Expect


Cultivate both internal and external relationships to ensure proper service levels are achieved for each portfolio function
Reporting to internal and external stakeholders based on multiple SLA’s
Compilation & delivery of data across multiple platforms
Proactively identify & communicate trends/issues to relevant stakeholders
Lead and manage special projects as assigned, as well as propose and drive process enhancements and automation
Collaborate with multiple areas of PGIM RE to ensure the highest level of service
Servicing/Cash Management – Calculation of debt service, delivery of billing letters
Surveillance – Review of compliance certificates, calculations to confirm covenant adherence, review and analysis of property monitoring reports
Banking Admin - Managing multiple international bank and borrower relationships
Treasury – cash movements for multiple
Anticipate issues that may adversely affect portfolio performance and take action to reduce risks
Review policies and procedures and ensure consistency across the regions


Skills & Experience


Bachelor’s Degree required
Experience in database design and maintenance
Proficiency in programming languages such as SQL, Python, or R
Strong background in Data Analytics and/or Data Science
Ability to analyse complex datasets and identify underlying drivers of performance
Experience managing multiple responsibilities within tight and challenging deadlines
Advanced skills in Microsoft Office, particularly Excel
Experience with data visualization tools such as Power BI or Tableau (desirable)
Hands-on experience with automation and low-code platforms, including: Microsoft Power Automate, Microsoft Power Apps, Microsoft Fabric (Data Engineering, Data Warehousing, and Analytics)
Strong organizational, analytical, and problem-solving skills
Excellent interpersonal skills with the ability to collaborate effectively across teams and stakeholders
Self-motivated, proactive, detail-oriented, and highly organized, with strong time management capabilities
Excellent verbal and written communication skills
Knowledge of or interest in the financial services industry is desirable
PGIM welcomes all applicants, even if you don't meet every requirement. If your skills align with the role, we encourage you to apply.


What We Offer You


Health Insurance: PGIM Ireland partner with Laya and BUPA to provide health insurance schemes that cover eligible employees day to day medical and hospital expenses.
Annual Leave of 23 days at full pay.
Pension Scheme: Members of the scheme can contribute up to 8% of salary per annum and PGIM Ireland matches contributions up to 8% of salary. Members can also make voluntary contributions to the scheme.
Annual Bonus Programme & Shop LK Vouchers / CleverCards (subject to eligibility): Along with an annual bonus employees are rewarded with Shop LK Vouchers/ CleverCards which are paid tax free.
Life Assurance: fully paid by PGIM Ireland, employees are covered from their start date and beneficiaries are provided with a lump sum of four times an employee’s salary.
Education Assistance: PGIM Ireland have an Education Assistance Programme that reimburses eligible employees for furthering their education.


-

PGIM Ireland is proud to be an equal opportunity employer and is committed to equal employment opportunity regardless of applicants’ gender, civil status, family status, sexual orientation, religion, age, disability, race or membership of the traveler community. PGIM Ireland's aim is to hire the best people for the open roles and all appointments will be made on merit.

Any offer of employment made by PGIM Ireland will be contingent on receiving satisfactory references. Applicants should be aware that background checks will be carried out on all candidates offered a position within PGIM Ireland.

Title: Business Intelligence Analyst
Description:
About Block Labs

Block Labs is a premier technology studio operating at the bleeding edge of Web3, Artificial Intelligence, and iGaming. We don't just ship features; we engineer high-scale, production-grade platforms that power the next generation of digital products.

We are a collective of senior engineers, product strategists, and builders who refuse to compromise on architecture. Whether we are designing autonomous multi-agent AI systems, building decentralized financial infrastructure, or architecting high-frequency iGaming platforms, our standard is excellence.

We move fast, but we build for the long term. If you are looking to work alongside a team that values deep technical expertise, thoughtful system design, and product ownership, Block Labs is where you belong.

About The Role

We’re looking for a data-obsessed BI Analyst to turn blockchain and product data into crisp insights that drive growth, retention, and risk controls across our crypto-enabled iGaming products. You’ll own the end-to-end analytics stack for wallet funnels, wagering behavior, lifecycle marketing, and compliance—using SQL, DBT, Python, Amplitude and Tableau as your primary tools. In the future we planning transition from Tableau to other BI tools alternatives like Metabase or Apache Superset or others.

Key Responsibilities


Drive the business forward through proactive analytics - don't just fulfill dashboard requests; spot anomalies, frame the right question yourself, and bring actionable recommendations to Product / CRM / Risk before they have to ask..
Build, maintain, and iterate dashboards surfacing KPIs (GGR/NGR, ARPU, LTV, churn, deposit→wager conversion, fraud loss rate).
Write production-grade SQL (CTEs, window functions, incremental models) to model clean datasets from Clickhouse and on-chain tables.
Build and maintain AI-agent workflows that automate recurring analytics - anomaly investigation, root-cause loops etc.
Use Python (pandas, seaborn, sklearn, scipy) for analysis, data quality checks, lightweight ETL/backfills, API pulls (e.g., blockchain providers), and automation.
Work with on-chain data: parse EVM logs, token transfers (ERC-20/721), join labeled address datasets, analyze exchange/bridge flows, gas/fee dynamics, and wallet clustering.
Partner with Product, Marketing/CRM, Risk/Compliance, and Engineering to define event schemas and tracking plans; ensure data quality, lineage, and documentation.
Design and analyze experiments, cohort analyses, and attribution within iGaming and regional compliance constraints.
Investigate anomalies (bonus abuse, botting, arbitrage, suspicious wallet rings) and recommend mitigations.
Automate recurring reporting and alerting for KPI movements.


Must-haves


2+ years in BI or Data Analytics; crypto or iGaming exposure prefered.
Ambitions about building great analytics infrastructure.
Good SQL: complex joins, window functions, query optimization, and dimensional modeling.
Solid AI-collaboration craft — prompt & context engineering, spec-driven task framing, and eval-driven iteration. Comfortable decomposing business questions into precise, agent-executable steps and designing the right tools for the agent to call.
BI Versatility: Expert knowledge of Tableau (LODs, parameters, performance tuning) combined with a strong interest or experience in modern, open-source BI tools like Metabase or Apache Superset.
Proficient Python for analytics & data engineering: pandas, modular scripts/notebooks, API integrations, scheduling small jobs, and writing maintainable code.
Solid statistics and probability fundamentals for experimentation - hypothesis testing, sample size and power, confidence intervals, and the discipline to distinguish real signal from noise.
Practical understanding of crypto wallets and transaction flows.


Nice-to-haves


dbt, Great Expectations/Monte Carlo for data quality; orchestration with Airflow/Prefect.
Built or contributed to production AI agents for analytics — ReAct loops, RAG over a knowledge base , MCP servers for data sources, multi-agent orchestration. Knows how to curate domain knowledge for agents so they stop hallucinating.
Ability to produce on-chain analytics (Dune/Flipside/Goldsky/Nansen tables or self-indexed data; contract ABIs; entity labeling).
Warehouse ecosystems knowledge Clickhouse, AWS, S3 buckets.
Data governance & privacy (GDPR, data minimization, retention policies).
Understanding of iGaming economics & KPIs: deposits, wagers, hold, GGR/NGR, RTP, bonus efficiency, RG metrics, and common fraud/AML patterns.
Experience with product analytics services like Amplitude, Mixpanel, Google Analytics.


What kind of culture can I expect?

Mature, mission-driven, and low-ego. We value clarity over noise, outcomes over theatrics, and pace without chaos. If you’re one of the smartest minds in your craft and want to build with other experts, you’ll feel at home here.

Title: Data Analyst
Description:
Why this role matters

The Data & Analytics team plays a pivotal role in the success of our CRM Marketing function and whole Marketing division by providing high-quality experimentation and timely analytical insights as well as data-backed recommendations, maintaining data operations and ensuring data quality.

The role is based in our office in Limassol, Cyprus. In case of relocation, we offer full relocation support for you and your family to make your move smooth and worry-free.

What you'll actually do


Design and execute A/B tests, set hypotheses, measure experiment results, apply statistical methodologies and provide actionable recommendations to optimise CRM campaigns.
Perform data analysis from clients and partners to understand user behavior, identify trends, and uncover opportunities for growth and retention.
Work closely with CRM Marketing and other Marketing teams to gather requirements, align on objectives, and define the scope of data tasks.
Understand the CRM Marketing landscape to identify areas of improvement in automated journeys and channels for greater efficiency and impact.
Develop, maintain, and monitor data-driven processes, ensuring data accuracy, consistency, and quality across CRM systems.
Provide actionable, data-driven insights, research, and quantitative analyses on both regular and ad hoc basis to support decision-making.
Support the integration of new data sources into the marketing automation system, ensuring seamless deployment of use cases.
Apply advanced analytics and predictive modeling techniques to forecast customer behavior and identify monetization opportunities.


Who We’re Looking For


Bachelor’s degree or equivalent in Mathematics, Statistics, Physics or a related field
Data Analytics 4+ years (With strong focus on A/B testing)
Strong experience in Python
Strong experience in SQL (complex queries, analytic functions)
Excellent understanding of mathematical statistics
Ability to translate business problems into quantitative analyses and produce insights
Proven ability to efficiently formalize and visualize the results of the data analysis
English language is a must; any other language will be considered as an advantage
Ability to communicate complex statistical & mathematical concepts to a non-technical audience
Ability to organize and prioritize tasks efficiently and to handle multiple, simultaneous deadlines
Ability to adapt to requirement changes when needed and produce fast solutions
Excellent verbal and written communication, upper-intermediate English
Proactive, detail-oriented, and capable of handling multiple projects simultaneously in a fast-paced environment


What We Offer Along The Way


Competitive salary and annual performance bonus
Full relocation support for you and your family — flights, housing, visas, and legal assistance included
Top-tier health insurance with full family coverage — medical, dental, vision, mental health — plus life insurance for peace of mind
Unlimited learning opportunities: external courses, English lessons, career and leadership development
Education allowance covering school and kindergarten fees
21 working days of annual leave, plus public holidays and fully paid sick, maternity, and paternity leave
Employee appreciation program: branded gifts, birthday day-offs, celebration budgets for weddings, newborns, and milestones
“Get to know Team” trips — meet colleagues across our global hubs, along with company-wide offsites that raise the bar
Employee share scheme — grow with us
Branded MINI Cooper Countryman company car and private parking
Free in-house sports clubs, Sanctum Club gym access, and jet skis
Access to a Corporate doctor
Exclusive discount program with cafes, gyms, and local services
Expat tax perks: up to 50% income tax exemption
Support with the naturalisation process for relocated employees


What Your Journey Looks Like


Intro call with Recruiter (30 minutes)
English check (for all non-native English speakers)
Tech interview (90 minutes)
Behavioural interview (60 minutes)
Team fit interview (60 minutes)


Sounds like you? Apply.

Please note: We occasionally amend or withdraw Exness jobs and reserve the right to do so at any time, including prior to the advertised closing date. Before applying, you are advised to read our data protection policy. This policy describes the processing that may be associated with your personal data and informs you that your personal data may be transferred to Exness/Exness Group companies around the world. Exness Group and its approved recruitment consultants will never ask you for a fee to process or consider your application for a career with Exness. Anyone who demands such a fee is not an authorized Exness representative and you are strongly advised to refuse any such demand.

At Exness, we're an equal opportunity employer where every individual is valued. No matter your race, color, religion, sex, national origin, sexual orientation, gender identity or disability, we welcome you. As an international fintech company, we embrace the richness of our diverse team, respecting each individual and promoting gender equality for all genders in our workforce.

Anyone who demands such a fee is not an authorized Exness representative and you are strongly advised to refuse any such demand.

At Exness, we're an equal opportunity employer where every individual is valued. No matter your race, color, religion, sex, national origin, sexual orientation, gender identity or disability, we welcome you. As an international fintech company, we embrace the richness of our diverse team, respecting each individual and promoting gender equality for all genders in our workforce.

Title: Product Analyst
Description:
We are a leading trading platform that is ambitiously expanding to the four corners of the globe. Our top-rated products have won prestigious industry awards for their cutting-edge technology and seamless client experience. We deliver only the best, so we are always in search of the best people to join our ever-growing talented team.

Our Product Analytics role is a great chance to grow in many areas. You’ll work on a variety of tasks that will build your technical, analytical, and business skills. Joining us means you’ll not only gain top skills in product analytics, but also play a key role in shaping our platform’s growth.

Responsibilities:


Build new analytics and reporting capabilities to support product evaluation and operations
Intake on business needs and apply contextual knowledge in order to scope data requests, synthesize insights, and recommend solutions in collaboration with others
Work with product leaders to enable market-facing solutions using data insights
Effectively communicate with cross-functional partners and internal teams to deliver solutions in a timely fashion
Establish A/B testing processes across product teams



Requirements:


SQL/Database experience
Experience with Tableau
Advanced working knowledge and experience with Python or R
Basic statistical knowledge
A successful history of manipulating, processing, and extracting value from large disconnected datasets



Nice to have:


Experience in fintech, finance, or trading
Experience in machine learning (ML), deep learning, search, data science, or computer vision is a big plus



What you will get in return:


Competitive Salary: We believe great work deserves great pay! Your skills and talents will be rewarded with a salary that makes you feel valued and motivated
Work-Life Harmony: Join a company that genuinely cares about you—because your life outside of work matters just as much as your time on the clock
Annual Performance Bonus: Your hard work doesn’t go unnoticed! Celebrate your achievements with a well-deserved annual bonus tied to your performance
Generous Time Off: Need a breather? Our annual leave policy lets you recharge and enjoy life outside of work without a worry
Employee Referral Program: Love working here? Share the love! Bring your talented friends on board and get rewarded for growing our awesome team
Comprehensive Health & Pension Benefits: From medical insurance to pension plans, we’ve got your back. Plus, location-specific benefits and perks!
Workation Wonderland: Live your digital nomad dreams with 30 extra days to work remotely from anywhere in the world (some restrictions apply). Adventure awaits!
Volunteer Days: Make a difference! Take two additional paid days each year to support causes you care about and give back to the community



Be a key player at the forefront of the digital assets movement, propelling your career to new heights! Join a dynamic and rapidly expanding company that values and rewards talent, initiative, and creativity. Work alongside one of the most brilliant teams in the industry.

Title: Commercial Business Analyst
Description:
At SolidStake, we believe that powerful ideas deserve bold storytelling. As a venture studio at the intersection of marketing, digital entertainment, and operations, we specialize in launching and scaling brands that captivate audiences and redefine online experiences.

We’re not just strategists—we’re creatives, brand builders, and growth architects. From crafting breakthrough campaigns to driving data-informed performance, our teams blend imagination with insight to shape narratives that resonate and convert. Whether it’s bringing a new venture to life or accelerating one that’s already growing, we move fast, think big, and execute with purpose.

At SolidStake, we foster a culture where collaboration fuels creativity. We champion diverse talent and fresh perspectives, creating space for every team member to take ownership, elevate their craft, and make a real impact. Join us and help shape the brands—and the stories of tomorrow.

Position Overview:

If you’re someone who gets excited by the combination of deep data analysis and commercial performance, this role will be a strong fit.

You enjoy going beyond surface-level reporting, identifying trends, diagnosing performance issues, and turning data-backed insights into actions with key stakeholders that improve business outcomes.

Partnering closely with CRM, VIP, Operations, and Affiliate teams, you’ll help shape decisions by providing a clear view on what is driving performance and where the biggest opportunities lie.

Responsibilities within the team:


Thrive in a dynamic, fast-paced environment, providing timely analysis and insight on emerging trends and adhoc business needs, ensuring stakeholders can make informed decisions when it matters most
Monitor and analyse key commercial performance metrics (e.g. FTDs, deposits, ROI), proactively identifying trends, gaps, and opportunities
Continuously evolve commercial reporting, ensuring stakeholders have clear, accurate, and actionable insights to support decision-making
Conduct deep-dive analyses into performance fluctuations across acquisition channels, CRM activity, and market-level trends, identifying root causes and recommended actions
Partner closely with CRM, VIP, Operations, and Affiliate teams to analyze campaign and lifecycle performance, providing insights that shape engagement, retention, and loyalty strategies
Generate customer behaviour insights (segmentation, lifecycle movement, product engagement) to support the development of more effective player journeys and commercial strategies
Identify inefficiencies and performance gaps across the commercial funnel, challenging assumptions and driving data-backed recommendations to improve outcomes
Drive continuous improvement of reporting frameworks, enhancing clarity, reducing noise, and enabling faster insight discovery



Facing the challenges of the role requires:


2+ years’ experience in a commercial analyst, business analyst, data analyst or related function within the iGaming industry
Strong analytical capability – you excel at working with large datasets to uncover and simplify issues, opportunities and generate actionable insights
Proficiency in BI Tools such as PowerBI, Qlikview, Tableau or others (we use Tableau)
A bachelor’s or master’s degree in mathematics, statistics, computer science or related quantitative field
You possess the ability to simplify and present complex insights to a wide variety of stakeholders

Title: Data Analyst
Description:
Who We Are

Ovoko is transforming Europe’s €30B+ used car parts market - a massive, fragmented industry still largely offline. We connect 6,000+ scrapyard owners, car parts sellers and dismantlers with millions of buyers through our marketplace, SaaS and logistics platform, making it effortless to trade parts across borders. With over 35 million parts listed, we’ve grown into one of Europe’s fastest-scaling e-commerce companies, but the real opportunity lies ahead, as we build a real category-defining platform worldwide.

About The Role

Fuelled by a recent $20M Series B funding round and consistent triple-digit year-over-year growth, Ovoko is continuing to scale its Data & Analytics function. Over the past year, our Data Org has focused on building strong foundations - modernising data architecture, tooling, and ways of working. Now, the focus is shifting towards driving real business impact through data.

To support this next phase, we are expanding our Data Team and looking for a Data Analyst to join us. This role will be part of a growing team working closely with multiple product and business areas across Ovoko: Product, Money, Business, Marketing and more. The exact scope may evolve depending on your strengths and interests - from product analytics to business operations or other domains.

If you're curious, proactive, and excited about solving real business problems with data - this role is a great opportunity to grow and make an impact in one of Europe’s fastest-growing e-commerce startups.

IN THIS ROLE, YOU WILL


Define KPIs, identify business opportunities, and estimate their impact using data insights.
Build dashboards, implement simple ETL, and utilize ML techniques for complex solutions.
Design and analyze experiments (A/B tests).
Use advanced analytics and ML techniques for complex data solutions.
Work with modern tooling such as Snowflake, Looker, Mixpanel, and explore opportunities in the evolving AI landscape.


What We’re Looking For


Experience working with SQL and BI tools (Power BI, Tableau, Looker, etc.).
Ability to translate data into clear insights and business recommendations.
Strong analytical thinking and problem-solving mindset.
Good data visualisation and story-telling skills.
Business-driven mindset.
Good communication skills and ability to work with different stakeholders.
Fluent English is required.


SALARY

The offered salary for this position starts at 3000€ (gross) per month. Please keep in mind that we are open to discuss your salary expectations based on your competencies and experience.

What We Offer


Private health insurance
Employee stock option plan (ESOP)
Learning budget for your personal and professional growth
Commuting perks (covered parking or public transport to choose from)
Everyday snacks and drinks
Onsite gym
Top-notch hardware and software (MacOS or Windows to choose from)
The opportunity to work from anywhere up to 30 days per year
Employee referral program


Discover all our perks by visiting our website: https://about.ovoko.com/career/#perks

Title: Growth Analyst
Description:
We are looking for an experienced Growth Analyst to be a central part of the operational side of the Growth team, specifically within our Delivery & Rides vertical, as we embark on an amazing journey to take Bolt to new heights!

About Us

Bolt is one of the fastest-growing tech companies in Europe and Africa, with over 100 million customers in 45+ countries. We’re a global team of more than 100 nationalities joined on a common mission – to make cities for people, not cars. And we need you to make it happen!

About The Role

As a Growth Analyst, You will be taking the ownership of our campaigns spending decision and contributing to the marketplace's success by meticulously analyzing the return on investment (ROI). With your expertise, we'll be able to accurately determine the most strategic allocation of resources, including the exact location, timing, method, and amount spent on campaigns.

You will be working closely with other team members of Growth domain, as well as Expansion Managers, Marketing and Operation teams. A Data Analyst will need to have good attention to detail and communication skills to succeed. Solid business sense and analytical mindset? You might be the campaign magician we’ve been waiting for.

This is a Tallinn-based role. We will only be able to sponsor relocation for candidates who are already residing within Europe.

Your daily adventures will include:


Own campaign spending decisions for your assigned markets and optimising such decisions to help the country team reach their growth targets
Wrangle complex data-sets to come up with testing hypotheses, setting up A/B tests (and other experiments) and analysing results using statistical techniques in order to guide your decision making
Proactively working together with Expansion Managers, Marketing, Operations and local teams to drive collaboration and get the most out of our investments
Identify valuable opportunities in the team and contribute to global campaigns team playbook, processes and product requests



About you:


BS in Mathematics, Economics, Computer Science, Information Management or Statistics
At least 3 years experience in an analytical role dealing with large volumes of data, Experience in consulting, pricing/discount strategy and/or on-demand is a plus
Solid grasp of statistics and demonstrated experience with A/B testing
Amazing SQL and Excel skills. Experience with Python or R is a plus
Experience with some BI tools and data visualization like Tableau, Power BI or Looker
Fluent in English

Experience is great, but what we really look for is drive, intelligence, and integrity. So even if you don’t tick every box, please consider applying if you feel you’re the kind of person described above!

Why you’ll love it here:


Play a direct role in shaping the future of mobility.
Impact millions of customers and partners in 500+ cities across 45 countries.
Work in fast-moving autonomous teams with some of the smartest people in the world.
Accelerate your professional growth with unique career opportunities.
Get a rewarding salary and stock option package that lets you focus on doing your best work.
Enjoy the flexibility of working in a hybrid mode.
Take care of your physical and mental health with our wellness perks.
Some perks may differ depending on your location.
