Title: Data Analyst with AI focus
Description:
At Exness, we are not just a leading trading broker—we’ve reimagined what it takes to be a leader. With 40M+ trades a day and 2,000+ people across 13 countries, we combine scale, care, and real tech to make trading better for 1M+ clients worldwide.

Recognised globally as a Best Place to Work, we’re a people-first company where long-term wins always matter more. As part of our team, you will shape the future of fintech with real technology, care, and purpose.

Why this role matters

As a Data Analyst within the fintech product department, you will deliver actionable insights, analytical solutions, and data-driven intelligence that empower strategic decision-making across core business operations and financial services. You will serve as the analytical backbone of the team, transforming complex datasets into meaningful visualizations, developing robust reporting frameworks, and ensuring that insights are delivered with accuracy, timeliness. Your work will directly support Product improvements, risk assessment, A/B testing initiatives, and operational performance optimization, while maintaining data integrity and compliance standards critical to fintech operations.

The role is based in Limassol, Cyprus. In case of relocation, we offer full relocation support for you and your family to make your move smooth and worry-free.

What you'll actually do


The Data Analyst is accountable for extracting, analyzing, and interpreting complex datasets to deliver actionable insights that drive business strategy and operational improvements. This includes building reports, performing trend analysis, and translating data into clear recommendations for leadership and stakeholders
The Data Analyst drives experimentation frameworks by designing, executing, and analyzing A/B tests to optimize user experience, conversion rates, and product features. This includes defining success metrics, statistical validation, and presenting findings to product and marketing teams
The Data Analyst serves as a bridge between technical data infrastructure and business units, translating complex analytical findings into compelling narratives and visualizations that enable data-driven decision-making across departments
The Data Analyst is accountable for tracking and analyzing operational performance across partnership programs, and customer journeys. This includes identifying inefficiencies, measuring campaign effectiveness, and providing insights that improve business outcomes and customer satisfaction
The Data Analyst owns the validation of his objects, ensuring that all analytical outputs are accurate, reliable, and audit-ready. This includes identifying anomalies, resolving data inconsistencies, and maintaining documentation
Explore and implement AI use cases that improve analytical efficiency and stakeholder support.
Build AI-assisted workflows for recurring analysis, reporting, and documentation tasks.
Evaluate and monitor the quality of AI outputs and define appropriate validation checks.
Collaborate with cross-functional teams to embed AI into existing analytics processes.
Document best practices and help scale AI adoption within the data team


Who We’re Looking For


Bachelor’s degree in Data Science, Computer Science, Statistics, Mathematics, or a related field.
3+ years of hands-on experience in data analysis or a related field.
Experience working in Agile environments or collaborating with development teams is a plus.
Strong proficiency in SQL, including experience with analytical databases (Vertica, PostgreSQL, ClickHouse).
Good Python skills for data analysis and integration with APIs or AI services.
Strong understanding of statistical analysis, including descriptive statistics, hypothesis testing, confidence intervals, statistical significance, and experiment evaluation.
Ability to build simple machine learning models (e.g., classifiers, scoring models, logistic regression, tree-based models) for analytical and operational use cases.
Ability to perform exploratory data analysis (EDA) and draw actionable insights from complex datasets
Hands-on experience using LLM-based tools in analytical or business workflows.
Ability to design effective prompts and structured inputs for repeatable business tasks.
Experience validating AI-generated outputs for accuracy, consistency, and business relevance.
Understanding of responsible AI principles, including privacy, bias, and hallucination risks.


Nice to have


Experience with workflow automation, AI agents, RAG, embeddings, or internal AI assistants.


What We Offer Along The Way


Competitive salary and annual performance bonus
Full relocation support for you and your family — flights, housing, visas, and legal assistance included
Top-tier health insurance with full family coverage — medical, dental, vision, mental health — plus life insurance for peace of mind
Unlimited learning opportunities: external courses, English lessons, career and leadership development
Education allowance covering school and kindergarten fees
21 working days of annual leave, plus public holidays and fully paid sick, maternity, and paternity leave
Employee appreciation program: branded gifts, birthday day-offs, celebration budgets for weddings, newborns, and milestones
“Get to know Team” trips — meet colleagues across our global hubs, along with company-wide offsites that raise the bar
Employee share scheme — grow with us
Branded MINI Cooper Countryman company car and private parking
Free in-house sports clubs, Sanctum Club gym access, and jet skis
Access to a Corporate doctor
Exclusive discount program with cafes, gyms, and local services
Expat tax perks: up to 50% income tax exemption
Support with the naturalisation process for relocated employees


At Exness, we know that changing jobs - and changing countries - is a big step. That’s why relocation with Exness is different. We make it smooth, supported, and truly life-changing.

What Your Journey Looks Like


Intro call with Recruiter (30 minutes)
English check (for non-native English speakers)
Tech interview (90 minutes)
Behavioural interview (60 minutes)


What it's like here

Curious about what working at Exness really looks like? Follow us on Instagram and LinkedIn.

We share the real Exness experience - our people, ideas, moments, and everything in between.

Sounds like you? Apply.

Please note: We occasionally amend or withdraw Exness jobs and reserve the right to do so at any time, including prior to the advertised closing date. Before applying, you are advised to read our data protection policy. This policy describes the processing that may be associated with your personal data and informs you that your personal data may be transferred to Exness/Exness Group companies around the world. Exness Group and its approved recruitment consultants will never ask you for a fee to process or consider your application for a career with Exness. Anyone who demands such a fee is not an authorized Exness representative and you are strongly advised to refuse any such demand.

At Exness, we're an equal opportunity employer where every individual is valued. No matter your race, color, religion, sex, national origin, sexual orientation, gender identity or disability, we welcome you. As an international fintech company, we embrace the richness of our diverse team, respecting each individual and promoting gender equality for all genders in our workforce.

  • Ability to perform exploratory data analysis (EDA) and draw actionable insights from complex datasets
  • Hands-on experience using LLM-based tools in analytical or business workflows.
  • Ability to design effective prompts and structured inputs for repeatable business tasks.
  • Experience validating AI-generated outputs for accuracy, consistency, and business relevance.
  • Understanding of responsible AI principles, including privacy, bias, and hallucination risks.

Nice to have

  • Experience with workflow automation, AI agents, RAG, embeddings, or internal AI assistants.

What We Offer Along The Way

  • Competitive salary and annual performance bonus
  • Full relocation support for you and your family — flights, housing, visas, and legal assistance included
  • Top-tier health insurance with full family coverage — medical, dental, vision, mental health — plus life insurance for peace of mind
  • Unlimited learning opportunities: external courses, English lessons, career and leadership development
  • Education allowance covering school and kindergarten fees
  • 21 working days of annual leave, plus public holidays and fully paid sick, maternity, and paternity leave
  • Employee appreciation program: branded gifts, birthday day-offs, celebration budgets for weddings, newborns, and milestones
  • “Get to know Team” trips — meet colleagues across our global hubs, along with company-wide offsites that raise the bar
  • Employee share scheme — grow with us
  • Branded MINI Cooper Countryman company car and private parking
  • Free in-house sports clubs, Sanctum Club gym access, and jet skis
  • Access to a Corporate doctor
  • Exclusive discount program with cafes, gyms, and local services
  • Expat tax perks: up to 50% income tax exemption
  • Support with the naturalisation process for relocated employees

At Exness, we know that changing jobs - and changing countries - is a big step. That’s why relocation with Exness is different. We make it smooth, supported, and truly life-changing.

What Your Journey Looks Like

  • Intro call with Recruiter (30 minutes)
  • English check (for non-native English speakers)
  • Tech interview (90 minutes)
  • Behavioural interview (60 minutes)

What it's like here

Curious about what working at Exness really looks like? Follow us on Instagram and LinkedIn.

We share the real Exness experience - our people, ideas, moments, and everything in between.

Sounds like you? Apply.

Please note: We occasionally amend or withdraw Exness jobs and reserve the right to do so at any time, including prior to the advertised closing date. Before applying, you are advised to read our data protection policy. This policy describes the processing that may be associated with your personal data and informs you that your personal data may be transferred to Exness/Exness Group companies around the world. Exness Group and its approved recruitment consultants will never ask you for a fee to process or consider your application for a career with Exness. Anyone who demands such a fee is not an authorized Exness representative and you are strongly advised to refuse any such demand.

At Exness, we're an equal opportunity employer where every individual is valued. No matter your race, color, religion, sex, national origin, sexual orientation, gender identity or disability, we welcome you. As an international fintech company, we embrace the richness of our diverse team, respecting each individual and promoting gender equality for all genders in our workforce.

Title: Product Data Analyst
HTML:
Working at Omnisend is... kind of boring. Not the soul-crushing kind of boring — more like the “no drama, no chaos, just smart people doing great work” kind. The kind that lets you focus on what matters, without pointless meetings, micromanagement, or bureaucracy. We’re data-driven. We test, we iterate, we improve. Gut feelings don’t drive decisions — insights do. We trust each other. We’re transparent, give real feedback, and actually listen. No egos, no hand-holding — just a team of experienced professionals moving fast, adapting easily, and delivering impact. Omnisend is customer-funded and customer-driven. Our work is rooted in solving real problems that help ecommerce brands grow. No empty promises — we bring in results. And yes, we have fun. But the grown-up kind — workations, team gatherings, the occasional party. No forced fun, no awkward icebreakers. Just genuine moments with a team that actually enjoys working together.

We are looking for a Product Data Analyst to join our Data Ops team. In this position, you will collaborate closely with product teams. You will look for the biggest business opportunities, explain user behavior, raise and test hypotheses, use advanced statistical methods and predictive models, forecast future trends, and perform deep-dive analysis. You will not work on small ad-hoc data requests, we expect that our data driven product managers will take care of this, while you will be taking care of the advanced statistics. We are looking for an independent and experienced professional who would contribute to a data driven approach in the company. Interpreting data and deducting actionable insights should be your main competence, with a rich record of successful high-impact projects over your career.

Some tools we use: BigQuery, Superset, dbt, Hightouch, Mixpanel, Dagster, Python and Airbyte.

What you’ll do @ Omnisend:


  • Build predictive data models using advanced statistical methods;
  • Provide actionable insights from data;
  • Identify business opportunities and estimate their impact;
  • Lead data-driven decision-making in a group of stakeholders;
  • Build dashboards;
  • Coach on best practices in data analysis to foster a data-driven culture.


We expect that you’ll bring:


  • Highly skilled in data visualization and story-telling;
  • Ability to translate complex data into clear business insights;
  • Experience building predictive models using advanced statistical methods;
  • Experience in Superset, Power BI, Looker, Tableau, or similar visualization tools;
  • Working experience with SQL;
  • Business-driven mindset;
  • Good knowledge of statistical analysis;
  • Education in statistics, mathematics, econometrics, economics, or related fields;
  • Fluency in English.


Snapshot of your impact, by the 3-month mark:


  • Complete Omnisend new-hire onboarding and job-specific training for the Marketing Data Analyst role;
  • Gain clarity on expectation of your role and gain understanding of your team’s processes;
  • Identify needs for your TEAM and ROLE and build out your OKRs for the quarter;
  • Fully onboard on using team’s tools like Airbyte, dbt, Hightouch, Dagster and Superset;
  • Get familiar with all product teams;
  • Gain clarity on Product team’s processes;
  • Start building the foundation of strong relationships with Omnisend teammates.


What we offer in return:


  • Gross salary starting point from 4200 EUR/month based on experience;
  • Quarterly salary reviews;
  • An unlimited learning budget for self-improvement complying with the best interest of Omnisend (after 3 months);
  • Working methods and best practices inspired by the best tech companies in Silicon Valley;
  • Senior colleagues enable us to have a high-performing, productive environment where your growth will accelerate;
  • Flexible working hours and remote work possibilities;
  • Private health insurance;
  • Unlimited access to psychotherapy;
  • Workstation budget (420 Eur);
  • Work anniversary gifts like house cleaning services, bikes, or scooters;
  • Attractive workations and team-led team-building events.


Omnisend is an email and SMS marketing platform built for ecommerce brands. With one-click integration to major ecommerce platforms, intuitive automation, and powerful email templates, we make sophisticated marketing effortless — no learning curve required.

Award-winning 24/7/365 customer support? Check. A platform trusted by growing brands and seasoned pros alike? Absolutely. The idea that “great marketing needs to be expensive”? Our 150,000+ customers prove that wrong every day.

We may use artificial intelligence (AI) tools to support parts of the hiring process, such as reviewing applications, analyzing resumes, or assessing responses. These tools assist our recruitment team but do not replace human judgment. Final hiring decisions are ultimately made by humans. If you would like more information about how your data is processed, please contact us.

Title: Decision Scientist, Trust & Safety
Description:
Brief Info About Vinted

Our mission is to make second-hand the first choice, and we're looking for people who want to help us get there. Every day, we work together to help our members buy and sell pre-loved clothing and lifestyle items, giving each piece a second life – or even a third.

The Vinted Group is made up of three business units that support this mission:

Vinted Marketplace is Europe’s leading platform for second-hand fashion and a go-to destination for all kinds of pre-loved items, with a growing range of categories. Our platform connects millions of members across 20+ markets, helping great items find a new life.

Vinted Go enhances the shipping experience with a vast network of over 500,000 pick-up and drop-off points, partnering with more than 60 carriers across Europe, with added services like item verification for peace of mind on high-value pieces.

Vinted Pay is the newest part of the Vinted Group, dedicated to bringing secure, reliable payments to buyers and sellers across Europe. Seamlessly integrated into the Vinted app, it helps keep every transaction safe, efficient, and easy for our members.

Founded in 2008 in Lithuania, Vinted began as a way for friends to find new homes for clothes they no longer needed. In 2019, we became Lithuania's first unicorn! Today, our headquarters remain in Vilnius, and we've grown with offices across Europe, supported by a team of over 2,000 people.

Information About The Position

You’ll join Trust & Safety’s Actioning team, whose mission is to protect the Vinted community from harmful activities and content. The team owns critical mechanisms, including user reporting, moderation workflows, policy enforcement, and interventions against malicious users.

We’re looking for a Data Analyst / Decision Scientist to join the team and help shape better product and policy decisions through data. In this role, you’ll be embedded in a cross-functional product team, working closely with product managers, engineers, and other decision scientists to solve meaningful problems and turn insights into action.

This is a great opportunity for someone who enjoys combining analytical depth with real product impact. You’ll lead hypothesis-driven experimentation, uncover opportunities, measure outcomes, and help your team make confident, data-driven decisions.

In this position, you’ll


Partner closely with your product team and stakeholders to identify opportunities, answer important business questions, and guide decision-making with data.
Drive product experimentation from hypothesis to analysis, helping the team validate ideas and understand their real impact.
Analyse moderation, reporting, policy, and sanctioning flows to uncover insights and improve how we protect the Vinted community.
Define, calculate, and monitor key performance indicators, spot issues early, and investigate root causes.
Build dashboards and self-service solutions that help teams independently access and use data.
Work with other decision scientists to raise the bar for analytics, experimentation, and data-driven thinking across the domain.


About You


Have 3+ years of experience in Data Analytics, Decision Science, or a similar role.
Are strong in analytical thinking and problem-solving, and know how to balance rigor with business relevance.
Have a solid understanding of statistical methods, including A/B testing (designing, running, and interpreting A/B tests), and other inferential techniques.
Have hands-on experience with SQL and Python for data analysis, or a strong willingness to learn them quickly.
Are familiar with BI tools such as Looker, Tableau, or Power BI, or a strong willingness to learn them quickly.
Enjoy working in iterative environments where priorities evolve and learning quickly matters.
Can communicate clearly with both technical and non-technical audiences.
Are fluent in English, both written and spoken.
Nice to have: Experience in Trust & Safety, fraud prevention, or a related area.


Work perks


The opportunity to benefit from our share options programme
25 working days of holiday
Access to all the tools & tech needed for work
Home office support: we provide IT workstation equipment and a personal budget of up to €540 for home workplace furniture
Private health insurance
Mental and emotional health support through the Mindletic app
Frequent team-building events
A personal monthly budget for shopping on Vinted
The opportunity to spend up to 90 days per year - 21 of which can be spent working outside of the EU - on workation
A dog-friendly office
In Vilnius office: Gym & in-house meals at friendly prices
In Kaunas office: a monthly lunch allowance, and a once-a-week provided in-house lunch and breakfast


Working at Vinted

Individual Learning Budget

We invest in your professional growth! As part of our commitment to continuous learning, we offer an annual learning budget to support your personal and career development through courses, certifications, workshops and more.

Hybrid Work

We’ve adopted a hybrid workplace model where 2 days in office are recommended but not enforced. It’s up to you and your team to decide on the exact days you’ll spend working together in person.

Equal Opportunity

The Vinted Group is committed to building an inclusive workplace where people from all walks of life feel a sense of belonging. We welcome applications from people of all backgrounds, identities and life experiences. At Vinted, all applicants are treated fairly without regard to their race, age, religion or belief, sex, national origin, citizenship, gender identity, sexual orientation, disability, or any other protected characteristic.

The gross monthly salary range for this position is:

€4.608—€6.233 EUR

Title: Lead Data Analyst
Description:
Company Description

Wise is a global technology company, building the best way to move and manage the world’s money.

Min fees. Max ease. Full speed.

Whether people and businesses are sending money to another country, spending abroad, or making and receiving international payments, Wise is on a mission to make their lives easier and save them money.

As part of our team, you will be helping us create an entirely new network for the world's money.

For everyone, everywhere.

Job Description

More about our mission and what we offer.

We’re looking for a Lead Data Analyst who is passionate about our mission of Money Without Borders to partner with our operational teams to help drive data-driven decisions that would support our fast-growing product through scaling and optimising the team.

As a Lead Data Analyst, you'll be driving our analytics efforts in our operations teams, who do everything from support our customers when they need help, to screening for criminal activity, to verifying customer identities at scale.

Most importantly, you’ll collaborate closely with your operational leads, as well as workforce management, quality, training, and knowledge management, to bring your insights into real change for our customers and help drive our mission!

Here’s How You Will Be Contributing


Analytical Capacity Planning and Forecasting. Focus on building and refining analytical models for strategic capacity planning. Take ownership of forecasting efforts to align with business growth and operational demands.
Data Pipeline Ownership. Take ownership of data pipelines to maintain and improve data flow, ensuring reliability and accuracy.
Predictive Modeling and Cause and Effect Analysis. Develop and implement robust models to predict outcomes and perform cause and effect analysis to identify key drivers, optimise processes, and enhance decision-making and strategic planning.
Strategic Support and Analysis. Provide critical insights to assess the operational health of the KYC/FinCrime function, conduct in-depth cost analysis, and offer detailed analysis of operational metrics (including quality) to understand impacts on customer experiences.
Performance Tracking and Initiative Optimisation. Monitor and track the performance of key strategic initiatives, capitalising on optimisation opportunities to enhance operational outcomes.
KPI Implementation and Target Setting. Lead the development and implementation of the operations KPI tree and the target-setting framework, integrating these within reporting pipelines and strategic operations.
Stakeholder Collaboration and Process Standardisation. Collaborate closely with various stakeholders to standardise processes across forecasting, scheduling, and real-time operations, promoting continuous improvement and strategic alignment.


This is an IC3 role. For more information on our Analytics Career Map and levelling structure, click here.

What You Will Bring


Quantitative Foundation. Ideally, you have a background in statistics, maths, physics, engineering, economics, or another scientific field. You apply first-principles thinking to break down complex operational problems.
A Statistical Mindset. You have a natural grasp of data logic. You don't just look at averages; you understand distributions, variance, and significance. You enjoy applying that rigor to messy, real-world operational data to separate signal from noise.
You have 4+ years of experience in analytics with demonstrated ability to approach complex problems with a strategic mindset, identifying innovative solutions that drive operational improvements.
You have a background working with operational team analytics including capacity planning, forecasting, efficiency analysis, quality assurance, predictive analytics, and experimentation.
You have experience working with complex data models in SQL (our warehouse is Snowflake) and analysing it using advanced SQL/Python/R
Able to demonstrate that you can tell a story and proactively give guidance on strategy based on insights
You have experience with data visualisation tools (Looker, PowerBI, Tableau etc.) and demonstrate storytelling ability with data
You have a bias to action - you identify what needs to be done and make it happen.


Some Extra Skills That Are Great (but Not Essential)


Prior experience in Operation domains
You have experience working with a WFM or Quality team.
You have experience working with WFM Systems
You have experience with forecasting techniques such as ARIMA, Holt-Winters, and other time series analysis methods


Additional Information

For everyone, everywhere. We're people building money without borders — without judgement or prejudice, too. We believe teams are strongest when they are diverse, equitable and inclusive.

We're proud to have a truly international team, and we celebrate our differences.

Inclusive teams help us live our values and make sure every Wiser feels respected, empowered to contribute towards our mission and able to progress in their careers.

If you want to find out more about what it's like to work at Wise visit Wise.Jobs.

Keep up to date with life at Wise by following us on LinkedIn and Instagram.

Title: Risk Analyst
Description:
Role Overview

EEZE is expanding rapidly across multiple live casino studios and international offices and as a result, we are seeking a Risk Analyst to join our growing team. The Risk Analyst plays a key role in monitoring, assessing, and managing the integrity of live games and gaming environments. The primary goal of the role is to proactively identify and mitigate fraudulent activities affecting game integrity, ensuring prompt detection, analysis, and resolution.

Key Responsibilities


Investigate and assess game performance and player data for signs of advantage play, fraud, collusion and game malfunctions.
Conduct detailed analyses of player data and game metrics to identify risks and trends that may impact gaming integrity.
Provide support and assistance to Risk Analysts in assessing game performance and identifying potential risks.
Collaborate with GIRM Team and the Product team to assess game performance and provide insights for improvements.
Maintain familiarity with the games at a facility and with strategies and techniques used by players at such games.
Assist in implementing and maintaining systems and tools for continuous risk monitoring.
Provide reporting and documentation on identified risks and ongoing investigations.
Report any abnormal issues or situations to the Head of Gaming Integrity and Risk Management.
Perform additional duties as assigned by the manager in relation to the position.


Qualifications & Skills


Higher education or ongoing studies.
Previous experience in related field.
A mathematical or quantitative academic background, relevant professional experience, or demonstrable expertise in data analysis.
Excellent knowledge of English (both written and spoken).
Mathematical and analytical mindset with the ability to interpret complex data.
Solid understanding of gaming security, fraud prevention, and risk management practices.
Advanced knowledge of MS Office, especially Excel and Word.
Key personal traits: critical thinking, detail oriented and capable of working independently.


What’s in it for you?


Experience a dynamic and team-orientated work environment.
Opportunities for personal growth and learning.
An open, inclusive and supportive team where you will be valued, and your suggestions will be welcome.
24 days paid holiday per year. This is in addition to local public holidays.
Hybrid Working (4 days office/1 day home).
Life Assurance (2x annual salary).
Private Medical Insurance.
€400 annual wellness allowance.
Team Building Opportunities.
€80 Monthly lunch allowance
Parking (limited).
Local discounts and more.

Title: Data Analyst (Dealing / Trading Analytics)
Description:
We are a leading trading platform that is ambitiously expanding to the four corners of the globe. Our top-rated products have won prestigious industry awards for their cutting-edge technology and seamless client experience. We deliver only the best, so we are always in search of the best people to join our ever-growing talented team.

We are hiring a Data Analyst to partner closely with our Dealing team to improve trading and operational decision-making through data. This person will build and maintain analytics and reporting capabilities, translate business needs into well-scoped data solutions, and deliver actionable insights to support product evaluation and day-to-day operations. Strong SQL and Python skills are required, along with proven experience in trading/fintech/financial markets.

Responsibilities:


Partner day-to-day with the Dealing team to support trading and operational workflows with data and insights
Build and enhance analytics and reporting capabilities to support product evaluation, trading performance, and operational monitoring
Gather business requirements and apply market/trading context to scope data requests, define metrics, and recommend practical solutions
Analyze large, disconnected datasets to synthesize insights and highlight risks/opportunities
Collaborate with business leaders and cross-functional stakeholders to enable market-facing solutions powered by data
Communicate findings clearly (written and verbal) and deliver solutions reliably and on time
Ensure data quality by validating inputs, monitoring key metrics, and improving definitions/documentation where needed



Requirements:


Strong SQL and database experience (querying, joining, modeling/structuring data for analysis)
Advanced working knowledge and experience with Python
Basic statistical knowledge and ability to apply it appropriately in analysis and reporting
Demonstrated experience manipulating, processing, and extracting value from large, messy, and/or disconnected datasets
Strong understanding of trading/dealing concepts and market microstructure (execution, pricing, liquidity, slippage, exposure, hedging basics)
Familiarity with anti-fraud / trading abuse patterns and investigation workflows
Experience working with API connections (REST/WebSocket)
Experience with machine learning / deep learning / data science methods (nice to have)



What you will get in return:


Competitive Salary: We believe great work deserves great pay! Your skills and talents will be rewarded with a salary that makes you feel valued and motivated
Work-Life Harmony: Join a company that genuinely cares about you - because your life outside of work matters just as much as your time on the clock
Generous Time Off: Need a breather? Our annual leave policy lets you recharge and enjoy life outside of work without a worry
Employee Referral Program: Love working here? Share the love! Bring your talented friends on board and get rewarded for growing our awesome team
Comprehensive Health & Pension Benefits: From medical insurance to pension plans, we’ve got your back. Plus, location-specific benefits and perks!
Workation Wonderland: Live your digital nomad dreams with 30 extra days to work remotely from anywhere in the world (some restrictions apply). Adventure awaits!
Volunteer Days: Make a difference! Take two additional paid days each year to support causes you care about and give back to the community



Be a key player at the forefront of the digital assets movement, propelling your career to new heights! Join a dynamic and rapidly expanding company that values and rewards talent, initiative, and creativity. Work alongside one of the most brilliant teams in the industry.

Title: Decision Scientist, Trust & Safety
Description:
Brief Info About Vinted

Our mission is to make second-hand the first choice, and we're looking for people who want to help us get there. Every day, we work together to help our members buy and sell pre-loved clothing and lifestyle items, giving each piece a second life – or even a third.

The Vinted Group is made up of three business units that support this mission:

Vinted Marketplace is Europe’s leading platform for second-hand fashion and a go-to destination for all kinds of pre-loved items, with a growing range of categories. Our platform connects millions of members across 20+ markets, helping great items find a new life.

Vinted Go enhances the shipping experience with a vast network of over 500,000 pick-up and drop-off points, partnering with more than 60 carriers across Europe, with added services like item verification for peace of mind on high-value pieces.

Vinted Pay is the newest part of the Vinted Group, dedicated to bringing secure, reliable payments to buyers and sellers across Europe. Seamlessly integrated into the Vinted app, it helps keep every transaction safe, efficient, and easy for our members.

Founded in 2008 in Lithuania, Vinted began as a way for friends to find new homes for clothes they no longer needed. In 2019, we became Lithuania's first unicorn! Today, our headquarters remain in Vilnius, and we've grown with offices across Europe, supported by a team of over 2,000 people.

Information About The Position

You’ll join Trust & Safety’s Actioning team, whose mission is to protect the Vinted community from harmful activities and content. The team owns critical mechanisms, including user reporting, moderation workflows, policy enforcement, and interventions against malicious users.

We’re looking for a Data Analyst / Decision Scientist to join the team and help shape better product and policy decisions through data. In this role, you’ll be embedded in a cross-functional product team, working closely with product managers, engineers, and other decision scientists to solve meaningful problems and turn insights into action.

This is a great opportunity for someone who enjoys combining analytical depth with real product impact. You’ll lead hypothesis-driven experimentation, uncover opportunities, measure outcomes, and help your team make confident, data-driven decisions.

In this position, you’ll


Partner closely with your product team and stakeholders to identify opportunities, answer important business questions, and guide decision-making with data.
Drive product experimentation from hypothesis to analysis, helping the team validate ideas and understand their real impact.
Analyse moderation, reporting, policy, and sanctioning flows to uncover insights and improve how we protect the Vinted community.
Define, calculate, and monitor key performance indicators, spot issues early, and investigate root causes.
Build dashboards and self-service solutions that help teams independently access and use data.
Work with other decision scientists to raise the bar for analytics, experimentation, and data-driven thinking across the domain.


About You


Have 3+ years of experience in Data Analytics, Decision Science, or a similar role.
Are strong in analytical thinking and problem-solving, and know how to balance rigor with business relevance.
Have a solid understanding of statistical methods, including A/B testing (designing, running, and interpreting A/B tests), and other inferential techniques.
Have hands-on experience with SQL and Python for data analysis, or a strong willingness to learn them quickly.
Are familiar with BI tools such as Looker, Tableau, or Power BI, or a strong willingness to learn them quickly.
Enjoy working in iterative environments where priorities evolve and learning quickly matters.
Can communicate clearly with both technical and non-technical audiences.
Are fluent in English, both written and spoken.
Nice to have: Experience in Trust & Safety, fraud prevention, or a related area.


Work perks


The opportunity to benefit from our share options programme
25 working days of holiday
Access to all the tools & tech needed for work
Home office support: we provide IT workstation equipment and a personal budget of up to €540 for home workplace furniture
Private health insurance
Mental and emotional health support through the Mindletic app
Frequent team-building events
A personal monthly budget for shopping on Vinted
The opportunity to spend up to 90 days per year - 21 of which can be spent working outside of the EU - on workation
A dog-friendly office
In Vilnius office: Gym & in-house meals at friendly prices
In Kaunas office: a monthly lunch allowance, and a once-a-week provided in-house lunch and breakfast


Working at Vinted

Individual Learning Budget

We invest in your professional growth! As part of our commitment to continuous learning, we offer an annual learning budget to support your personal and career development through courses, certifications, workshops and more.

Hybrid Work

We’ve adopted a hybrid workplace model where 2 days in office are recommended but not enforced. It’s up to you and your team to decide on the exact days you’ll spend working together in person.

Equal Opportunity

The Vinted Group is committed to building an inclusive workplace where people from all walks of life feel a sense of belonging. We welcome applications from people of all backgrounds, identities and life experiences. At Vinted, all applicants are treated fairly without regard to their race, age, religion or belief, sex, national origin, citizenship, gender identity, sexual orientation, disability, or any other protected characteristic.

The gross monthly salary range for this position is:

€4.608—€6.233 EUR

Title: Duomenų analitikas (-ė)
Description:
„IKI“ – tai vieta, kur žmonės, nepriklausomai nuo to, ar dirba biure, parduotuvėse, gamyboje ar logistikoje, aistrą pirmauti verčia ambicingais pasiekimais! Pirmieji Lietuvoje pristatėme prancūziškus kruasanus ir bagetes, atidarėme autonominę parduotuvę, įdiegėme savitarnos kasas ir elektronines kainų etiketes. Šie žingsniai atspindi mūsų nuolatinį siekį būti žingsniu priekyje ir kurti ateities mažmeninę prekybą – inovatyvią bei tvarią. Jei ieškai dinamiškos, idėjoms atviros aplinkos, kurioje galėtum augti ir kurti naujoves, gali būti, kad darbas „IKI“ yra arčiau tavęs!

Šiame darbe tu:


Analizuosi duomenis įvairiuose projektuose (dalyvavimas projektų komandose, reikalingų duomenų išgavimas ir apdorojimas) bei verslo atvejų skaičiavimuose;
Dalyvausi rodiklių (KPI) stebėsenoje (nustatyti kasdienės veiklos ir projektų įgyvendinimo KPI rodiklius bei užtikrinti jų matavimą);
Teiksi pagalbą biudžeto sudarymo ir kontrolės procesuose;
Dalyvausi tiekimo grandinės parametrų nustatyme siekiant optimizuoti visuminius tiekimo grandinės kaštus.


Tikimės, kad tu:


Turi aukštąjį universitetinį išsilavinimą kiekybinėje srityje, tokioje kaip matematika, informatikos mokslai, programų inžinerija, ekonomika, fizika ar panašioje srityje.;
Turi 2+ metų darbo patirties duomenų analizės srityje;
Turi gerus MS Excel ir darbo su PowerBI įgūdžius;
Gali pasigirti stipriais analitiniais ir loginio mąstymo gebėjimais;
Turi programavimo kalbų įgūdžių (SQL, Python ir kt.) – būtų privalumas.


„IKI“ pasiūlymas tau:

Atlyginimas ir finansinė gerovė


atlyginimas: 2200–3400 €/mėn. (neatskaičius mokesčių, priklausomai nuo tavo patirties ir įgūdžių),
galėsi pigiau apsipirkti „IKI“ prekybos centruose,
turėsi nuolaidas „IKI“ asortimento naujienoms,
daugiau nei 300 MELP ir partnerių nuolaidų kone visiems gyvenimo atvejams.  


Darbo aplinka ir lankstumas


hibridinis darbo modelis,
lankstumas pasirenkant darbo valandas,
įkvėpimui – darbostogos iki 30 d. per metus.


Karjera ir tobulėjimas


karjeros galimybės (tiek Lietuvoje, tiek užsienyje),
profesinio tobulėjimo galimybės (tiek su kolegomis, tiek ir individualiai).


Sveikata ir emocinė gerovė


papildomas sveikatos draudimas, kurį galėsi rinktis net iš 3 planų,
nemokamos sveikatos patikros biure,
dalyvavimas „Walk15“, vidinėse badmintono, dviračių, žygių mėgėjų sporto grupėse,
streso valdymo ir kiti emocinės gerovės mokymai, galimybė gauti anonimines psichologo konsultacijas telefonu.     


Stipri bendruomenė


stiprybėmis grįsta kultūra, kurioje vertinamos visų darbuotojų unikalios savybės ir pasiekimai,
kolegas pagal laisvalaikio veiklas kviečiame burtis į „Hobby Spotlight” klubus,
darbuotojų fondas „VeIKIme drauge“,
esame aktyvūs bendruomenės nariai – savanoriaudami ir finansine parama prisidedame prie „Maisto banko“ ir kitų organizacijų.  


Inovacijos ir dinamiškumas


modernūs darbo įrankiai ir programos,
inovatyvūs sprendimai prekyboje, tvarumo ir kitose srityse,
mūsų komandos projektus ir pasiekimus kviečiame pažinti čia: Tvarumas | IKI. Arčiau Tavęs!


Skamba kaip darbas arčiau tavęs ir tavo tikslų?

Pasidalink savo CV arba susisiek su mūsų atrankų specialiste Dovile telefonu +37065696339 arba LinkedIn platformoje.

Title: Business Analyst
Description:
Summary

We're looking for a Business Analyst who is passionate about transforming data into actionable insights. In this role, you'll help build the analytical foundation that powers decision-making across the company, from customer engagement and product usage to subscriber conversion and retention.

You'll work closely with product, growth, customer success, and leadership teams to uncover opportunities, answer key business questions, and ensure everyone has access to reliable, meaningful data.

Key Responsibilities


Build and maintain data frameworks that enable quick, reliable reporting and self-serve insights across the organization.
Build reusable analytical models and tools (e.g. cohort frameworks, conversion models) to enable fast, flexible decision-making.
Design and maintain reports and dashboards covering customer engagement and product analytics, with emphasis on event tracking and behavioral patterns.
Deliver ad-hoc analyses that support key business questions, especially around subscriber conversion, retention, and feature usage.
Ensure data quality, consistency and reliability across tools and databases.
Help scale our analytics foundation: improving structure, expanding coverage, and keeping documentation up to date.


Key Qualifications


3+ years of experience in a business analyst, product analyst, or data analyst role, ideally in a consumer tech or SaaS environment.
Strong analytical skills with ability to work with complex datasets.
Strong skills in Sheets/Excel and BI tools.
Strong working knowledge of SQL; experience with event-based product analytics tools (e.g. PostHog, Mixpanel, or similar) is a plus.
Ability to create, manage and automate custom dashboards and reporting pipelines.
A good communicator who can translate business requirements into analytical outputs and relay findings into clear recommendations for non-technical stakeholders.
Self-directed, organized, and comfortable operating in a fast-moving startup environment.
Familiarity with tools like Google Colab or Python for data pipeline and analysis work.
Strong communication skills in English
Experience with subscription or RevenueCat-based data models is a plus.
Background in consumer apps or connected hardware products is a plus.


Join us


Work on a project that can truly change how millions of people perceive nature and help them make a lasting impact on global biodiversity for generations to come.
Engage with a passionate, dynamic, and talented team.
Be part of the fast-paced, innovative environment of a rapidly growing startup, with abundant opportunities for personal and professional growth.
Enjoy competitive stock options, at least 25 days of paid leave, and an open-ended contract with a trial period.
Receive your own Birdbuddy Feeder and Birdbuddy Premium Family subscription.


If this describes you, we invite you to submit your application to help us learn more about you and your potential contributions to our mission to connect people with nature through technology.

Title: Data Analyst - Limerick
Description:
Unijobs on behalf of our public sector client have a requirement for a suitably skilled Data Analyst to join their team. The duration of this post is 12 months initially with possible extensions thereafter. This role will be based in the Mid-West region & will be a Flexible/Hybrid Working – The successful candidate must be able to attend meetings/workshops as required.




Working 35 hours per week, the successful candidate will be employed as an agency employee and will be paid an hourly rate of €33.19 per hour based on an annualised salary of €60,613. You will accrue 30 days annual leave per year and paid Bank Holidays.




Role Description:

In order to meet day 1 data requirements, the business data analyst must have the ability to build the required routines to extract, transform and manipulate the legacy data which resides on a local application to meet the MFF specifications.
The analyst must possess the analytical abilities to compare the extracted data to identify potential problems. design and create SQL Server queries, stored procedures, functions, and views.
This role needs the required skills to troubleshoot items placed on the working item log.
The analyst must support the digitisation, cleansing, and validation of legacy data of all data that resides currently on legacy applications and paper-based systems to produce prepared datasets aligned to the Day-1 cohort
The analyst must support the mapping, standardisation, and structuring of prepared datasets to align with planned solution data requirements
The analyst must support the conversion of structured datasets into load-ready files aligned to system input formats, sequencing rules, and execution requirements.
The analyst must work with Service SMEs to support the validation, reconciliation, and formal approval of loaded data to ensure accuracy, completeness, and readiness for operational use
Implementing data validation and cleansing procedures to ensure the quality, integrity, and dependability of the data.
Improve the scalability and efficiency of data pipelines across multiple services
Monitoring and resolving data pipeline problems to guarantee consistency and availability of the data.
Optimisation of queries for performance and index usage




Required:

Proficiency in SQL, with experience in querying and manipulating databases and writing complex SQL queries/views/functions.
Proficiency in preparing data for uploading to Health and Social Care clinical management systems




Optional/Desired:

Experience operating in the health and social care environment in Ireland
Experience in Agile project management environment




Business Competencies:

Excellent analytical, problem solving and decision-making skills.
The ability to quickly grasp and understand complex issues and the impact on service delivery.
The ability to successfully manage a range of different projects and work activities concurrently, utilising computer technology effectively and assigning work to others as appropriate to meet strict deadlines.
The ability to build and maintain relationships with colleagues and other stakeholders and to achieve results through collaborative working.
Evidence of incorporating the needs of the service user into service delivery.
Evidence of practicing and promoting a strong focus on delivering high quality customer service for internal and external customers.
Evidence of setting high standards of performance for self and others, ensuring accurate attention to detail and consistent adherence to procedures and current standards within area of responsibility.




*Applicants must be eligible to work in Ireland at the time of application*




*This position may be subject to Garda Vetting and Foreign Police Clearance, if applicable you will be required to obtain these prior to commencing in this role*




Unijobs is an equal opportunities employer

Title: Supply Chain Master Data Analyst
Description:
The Company

Dexcom Corporation (NASDAQ DXCM) is a pioneer and global leader in continuous glucose monitoring (CGM). Dexcom began as a small company with a big dream: To forever change how diabetes is managed. To unlock information and insights that drive better health outcomes. Here we are 25 years later, having pioneered an industry. And we're just getting started. We are broadening our vision beyond diabetes to empower people to take control of health. That means personalized, actionable insights aimed at solving important health challenges. To continue what we've started: Improving human health.

We are driven by thousands of ambitious, passionate people worldwide who are willing to fight like warriors to earn the trust of our customers by listening, serving with integrity, thinking big, and being dependable. We've already changed millions of lives and we're ready to change millions more. Our future ambition is to become a leading consumer health technology company while continuing to develop solutions for serious health conditions. We'll get there by constantly reinventing unique biosensing-technology experiences. Though we've come a long way from our small company days, our dreams are bigger than ever. The opportunity to improve health on a global scale stands before us.

Meet The Team

New ideas have a way of becoming not only great products but also best in class practices that can significantly enhance the customer experience. At Dexcom you can make a difference in the lives of millions across the world!

Reporting to the Sr Supply Chain Manager, this role contributes to the coordination of Engineering Change Orders (ECOs) and supports product lifecycle activities, ensuring that system data is accurate and updated in a timely manner. You will help enable effective planning, scheduling, warehouse and execution processes aligned with Dexcom’s operational standards.

Where You Come In


You will collect, validate, and update master data required for Purchasing (Buy Items), Production Planning (Make Items), Warehousing and Inventory processes
You will ensure data accuracy and completeness within ERP systems
You will support coordination and implementation of Engineering Change Orders across make and buy items, assist with updating system data in line with approved changes, Track ECO progress and escalate issues where required
You will assist in managing Phase-in / phase-out of parts, Product revision changes
You will ensure updates are reflected accurately in systems to support production and procurement readiness
You will provide accurate data inputs to support MRP and planning activities
You will support resolution of data-related issues impacting Supply Chain operations
You will identify and support improvements in data accuracy and processes
You will follow established data governance and change control procedures


What Makes You Successful


You have had exposure to master data, planning and purchasing processes
You have familiarity with item master data structures
You are an advanced user of Microsoft Excel, Copilot, Outlook, Teams and Smartsheets is preferable
You have experience in the medical device or CPG industry
You have experience with Oracle EBS, Oracle Fusion, PLM and Anaplan systems networks is advantageous


Experience And Education Requirements


Typically requires a Bachelor’s degree in Supply Chain Management, Business Administration, Operations Management, or a related field and a minimum of 2-5 years of related experience.


What You’ll Get


A front row seat to life changing CGM technology.
A full and comprehensive benefits program.
Growth opportunities on a global scale.
Access to career development through in-house learning programs and/or qualified tuition reimbursement.
An exciting and innovative, industry-leading organization committed to our employees, customers, and the communities we serve.


Travel Required


0-5%


To all Staffing and Recruiting Agencies: Our Careers Site is only for individuals seeking a job at Dexcom. Only authorized staffing and recruiting agencies may use this site or to submit profiles, applications or resumes on specific requisitions. Dexcom does not accept unsolicited resumes or applications from agencies. Please do not forward resumes to the Talent Acquisition team, Dexcom employees or any other company location. Dexcom is not responsible for any fees related to unsolicited resumes/applications.

Title: Business Intelligence Analyst
Description:
Business Intelligence Analyst – Cyprus

The Role:

We are looking for an enthusiastic professional to join our friendly team of Business Intelligence experts. A Business Intelligence (BI) Analyst plays a crucial role in helping organizations make informed business decisions by collecting, analyzing, and presenting data. You will work on cutting-edge projects by designing and mapping data models to turn raw data into meaningful insights and utilize different tools to build interactive and visually appealing dashboards and reports. You will also be able to expand your knowledge in a pleasant and inspiring environment.







The main responsibilities of the position include:

Collect, process, and analyze large sets of data from various sources.
Utilize BI tools and technologies to extract meaningful insights from raw data.
Create and maintain dashboards, reports, and visualizations to communicate Key Business Metrics.
Present findings and insights to key stakeholders in a clear and understandable manner.
Collaborate with business users to understand their data and reporting needs.
Translate business requirements into technical specifications for data analysis and reporting.
Design and implement data models to support business reporting and analysis.
Ensure data accuracy, consistency, and reliability.
Identify trends and patterns in data to support forecasting and planning activities.
Provide recommendations for business strategy based on data analysis.
Develop and implement data governance policies and procedures.
Ensure compliance with data privacy and security regulations.
Stay informed about industry trends, emerging technologies, and best practices in BI.
Recommend and implement improvements to existing BI processes and tools.




Main requirements:

BSc/MSc in Mathematics / Statistics / Engineering or Information Technology or other relevant field
Strong analytical and problem-solving skills.
Ability to interpret complex data sets and draw meaningful conclusions.
Proficient in using BI tools (e.g., Tableau, Power BI, SAPBI).
Knowledge of database technologies (SQL, data warehouses) and data modelling
Excellent verbal and written communication skills.
Ability to convey technical concepts to non-technical stakeholders.
Ability to work collaboratively with cross-functional teams.
Understanding of business processes and the ability to align BI solutions with organizational goals.
Strong attention to detail to ensure accuracy in data analysis and reporting.
Proactive attitude towards problem-solving and continuous improvement.




Benefit from:

Attractive remuneration package plus performance related reward
Private health insurance
Corporate pension fund
Intellectually stimulating work environment
Continuous personal development and international training opportunities




The Hiring Experience: What Awaits You

Let’s Connect – Intro Chat with Talent Acquisition
Test your edge – Aptitude Assessment
Deep Dive – First Interview with Your Future Team




Type of employment: Full time

Location: Cyprus

Please visit our website www.xm.com/careers to submit your online application for this position.




All applications will be treated with strict confidentiality!

Title: Data Lead (Growth & Analytics)
Description:
Mid-senior position | Part-time or Full-time | On-site @Ljubljana




About the role

We're building a real-world social platform. Matched meetups that get people out of their homes and turn strangers into friends. It works. Now we need someone to build the data infrastructure that helps us scale it.




As our Data Lead (Growth & Analytics) you'll own everything data: event tracking, pipelines, the dashboards that drive our decisions. If a metric matters to the business, you built the system that surfaces it.




We're an AI-first team - that includes BI. We're building AI-powered dashboards and we want someone who finds that exciting and has done this in the past.




You'll be one of our first 10 team members. Early enough to shape how we instrument, what we trust, and how decisions get made. If you've spent years in a SaaS or subscription business and you intuitively know which numbers lie and which ones matter, you'll fit right in.




It's fast. It's sometimes messy. The problems are real and the impact is direct.




You’ll be a great fit if you:

Work best without permission
Default to "how could I systemize this"
Had weird teenage hobbies
Spend your Sundays on side projects
Shipped something this week
Are good at chess, poker or Monopoly
Forget to grab lunch because you’re in the zone
Get the best ideas while showering
Have left a job because the pace was too slow
Are more motivated by winning than by being right
Find bureaucracy physically painful




What you’ll be doing

Data infrastructure

Own data architecture decisions and tooling selection across marketing, revenue, finance, product, and operations
Set up and maintain a data warehouse
Set up experimentation tooling
Ensure data accuracy, freshness, and consistency
Implement data validation, anomaly detection, and monitoring

Tracking

Build attribution tracking for Meta Ads, Google Ads, email and other channels
Measure reliable data at every step of the acquisition funnel
Diagnose and resolve tracking discrepancies across platforms
Work with other teams to implement and QA tracking changes

Data analysis

Translate data into clear, actionable recommendations for leadership
Build forecasting models for revenue, churn, and growth scenarios
Identify leading indicators and growth constraints
Support strategic decisions around pricing, roadmap, and scale strategy
Implement cohort dashboards, churn prediction tools, and LTV/CAC reporting
Segmentation by geographies, niches, channels, etc
User feedback analysis
Operational efficiency analysis

Experimentation

Set success metrics and guardrails for experiments
Run A/B tests on landing pages, onboarding funnels, pricing pages, upsells, etc
Evaluating A/B tests and suggesting new ones

Product & business insights

Analyze user behavior across onboarding, activation, engagement, and retention
Identify friction points and drop-offs in core user journeys
Partner with product, marketing, and ops to drive improvements




Requirements

Essential:

Fluent in English (written and spoken)
At least 3 years of relevant experience
Experience working with a subscription/SaaS business (familiar with metrics such as MRR, churn, cash conversion cycle,...)
Comfortable working with SQL, data modeling, BI, retention/LTV analysis, attribution, and forecasting.
AI-first mindset - ability to heavily rely on AI tools and build in such a way that we can keep leveraging AI in the future
Strong revenue strategy mindset: understands monetisation, pricing, payback logic, and CAC/LTV trade-offs.
Comfortable working in ambiguity, adapting to changes and wearing multiple hats
Ability to identify ideal software and integrate it into the company’s workflow
Attention to detail and the ability to work independently without constant oversight
A lot of drive, energy, and time.
Ability to work from our office in Ljubljana city center

Bonus points:

Previous startup experience (especially early-stage)
Experience building teams from scratch
You are based close to Ljubljana (or willing to move)




About us

We help people meet new friends through real-life events all around the world.




We’re building something ambitious. Small team. Global startup. Serious brains. Real responsibility. Fast growth. Mobile app / B2C SaaS.




We are past product-market fit and are now in fast-scaling mode.




If you want to ship fast, work hard, own systems end-to-end, and grow with the company - this is for you.




Founded by Jani Pravdič and Matic Jesenovec – entrepreneurs who've together built multiple businesses impacting hundreds of thousands of people and generating well over eight figures in revenue. We're now building something that we believe has the potential to grow much larger and have a greater impact than anything we’ve done before.




What’s in it for you

Working closely with the founders
Working in a high-performance team
It takes us hours instead of weeks to launch/implement something
We test things and we listen to the numbers, not who is the loudest in the room
Failure is part of the process; we accept it and learn from it
Opportunity to grow into a higher role
The chance to genuinely impact many thousands of lives globally
A front-row seat in an exciting, fast-growing start-up
Competitive salary
Performance bonuses




Our values

Strategic Boldness: We do what it takes to deliver faster, farther, and louder.
Agility > Perfection: We value action over polish, effectiveness over effort. Ship fast, refine later.
Scrappy & Curious: We test, learn, and adapt constantly. Resourceful beats resource-rich.
Ambition + Humility: We learn from the best, then push beyond. Inspiration fuels our innovation.
Long Game Thinking: We’re impatient with action, patient with results. Correct quickly, stay the course.




Next steps

We’re looking for someone to start as soon as possible. So if the role sounds exciting, please follow these steps:

Fill out the application form.
Our team carefully reviews every application, so the selection process may take some time.
The best candidates will receive an additional test assignment.
Those who perform best on this task will be invited to an extensive interview.




If you do not hear from us within 14 days of submitting your application, it means we have decided to continue the process with other candidates at this time.

We’re looking for someone to start as soon as possible. So if the role sounds exciting, please follow these steps:

  1. Fill out the application form.
  2. Our team carefully reviews every application, so the selection process may take some time.

Title: BI Data Analyst
Description:
Growth Leads is an online lead generation company that creates quality leads in highly competitive verticals, such as iGaming, Financials, Health and Communications. We focus on building high-end websites with quality content that let customers make an informed decision about the product they’re about to buy or sign-up to. We pride ourselves on adopting a customer-focused approach to quality and usability.

About The Role

We are looking for an experienced Data Analyst who is motivated to take on an amazing new adventure: developing and maintaining data analysis structures and derive actionable recommendations to boost company revenue.

What you'll do


Monitor, analyze, and report on the performance of our different acquisition channels.
Develop and maintain dashboards to track key metrics such as traffic sources, conversion rates, lifetime values.
Design and implement data strategies to optimize our marketing efforts.
Translate complex datasets into actionable insights to guide marketing decisions and growth initiatives.
Analyze user journeys to assess the potentials for optimization for our acquisition channels
Design and analyze A/B tests for landing pages, offers, and affiliate traffic strategies.
Develop predictive models to forecast traffic, conversion, and revenue outcomes across affiliate segments.
Ensure the accuracy, integrity, and consistency of affiliate marketing data by working closely with engineering and product teams.
Define and enforce data collection standards and best practices.
Communicate findings and recommendations clearly to non-technical stakeholders.
Work with tools such as SQL, Python/R, Google Analytics, Looker/Tableau
Identify reporting issues through anomaly detection and behavioral analysis.
Share best practices and help foster a data-driven culture.


What we're looking for


5+ years of experience in data analytics, ideally with 2+ years specifically in performance or affiliate marketing analytics.
Proficiency in SQL: Strong ability to query large datasets across multiple tables and optimize complex joins.
Experience with Analytical Tools: Hands-on experience with data visualization and BI platforms (e.g., Looker, Tableau, Power BI).
Statistical Programming: Working knowledge of Python or R for advanced analytics, data wrangling, and automation.
A/B Testing & Experimentation: Experience designing, executing, and analyzing split tests with an understanding of statistical significance.
Data Modeling & Forecasting: Ability to create predictive models and perform regression, cohort, and lifetime value analysis.
Deep knowledge of affiliate channels, performance marketing KPIs, attribution models, and conversion funnels is a plus
Proven track record of turning data into actionable insights that drive business decisions and ROI improvements.
Experience working in fast-paced digital or e-commerce environments.
Strong Communication Skills: Ability to present complex data in a clear, concise, and actionable manner to non-technical stakeholders.
Business Acumen: Capable of understanding marketing strategy and aligning data initiatives with business goals.
Problem-Solving Mindset: Analytical thinker with a proactive approach to identifying and solving problems.


What we offer


Real Impact: Your insights will directly shape marketing strategy and revenue decisions — this is a role where data drives the business, not just the reports
Interesting & Diverse Data: Work across multiple competitive verticals and acquisition channels, with complex, real-world datasets that will challenge and grow your analytical skill set
Collaborative Environment: Work closely with engineering, product, and marketing teams in a dynamic, data-driven culture that values your expertise
Competitive Compensation: A competitive salary, performance-based incentives, and private healthcare benefits
Flexible & Remote-Friendly: Work remotely, on-site, or hybrid — with the flexibility to suit your lifestyle

Title: Business Intelligence Tableau Analyst
Description:
Company Description

We are SkillOnNet, leading the igaming entertainment by providing our customers with the most entertaining and trustworthy experience possible, while also reinventing the gambling industry. We are home to more than 30 well-known brands, including PlayOJO, DruckGluck, BacanaPlay, Genting, and many more. We are committed to long-term development and sustainability, and we are trying to revolutionise our industry for the benefit of our players, ourselves, and the entertainment industry as a whole.

Job Description

We are seeking an experienced detail-oriented Business Intelligence Tableau Analyst to join our Business Intelligence and data Analytics team in Cyprus. Ideally, he is proficient in data analysis and can provide data-driven strategic insights to influence business decisions. This role focuses on Designing, developing, and maintaining interactive Tableau dashboards and reports translating complex data into business insights, and maintaining data integrity. This is a unique opportunity for a talented, self-motivated individual looking to build on their existing experience to join a dynamic Team and contribute to the growth of an already successful business.

Responsibilities Include


Design, develop, and maintain interactive Tableau dashboards and reports
Connect Tableau to various data sources for real-time or batch data
Perform detailed data exploration and validation from a variety of sources, testing and data quality checks to ensure visualizations are accurate, efficient, and accessible to users
Leverage advanced Tableau functionality (parameters, actions, tooltip modifications, API, etc.) to create analytical dashboards
Establish and promote design guidelines, best practices and standards.
Conduct in-depth data analysis and interpret complex data, analyse results, and provide ongoing reports to identify trends, patterns, and insights
Collaborate with business units to understand and prioritise their data requirements and provide relevant solutions
Optimize user experience through interactive dashboards, reports, and Tableau story points
Present findings and insights to stakeholders in a clear and concise manner
Provide an ongoing training to team members and support to end-users on Tableau


What We Are Looking For


Bachelor’s degree/master’s degree in quantitative discipline such as statistics, mathematics, economics, operations research etc.
At least 3 years' experience in data science or related field
Proficient in data visualization and creation of impactful dashboards using Tableau (working experience with Tableau is a must)
Strong knowledge of SQL, MS-Excel; and Python skills
Strong analytical mindset and problem-solving skills, attention to detail with ability to focus and prioritize workload
Knowledge of database management and data warehousing concepts
Ability to work independently and as part of a team
Strong communication with fluency in English is required, excellent presentation and data storytelling skills with the ability to explain technical concepts to non-technical stakeholders.
Ability to think strategically and primarily cooperate and interact with senior stakeholders.
High drive, can-do attitude, proactive, and flexible to changing business demands.
Working experience in the gaming industry would be considered a strong plus


What's in it for YOU?


Excellent work environment
Monetary vouchers on Birthdays and other special occasions
Fully equipped kitchen and in-house entertaining space
Options to enroll in Company's medical insurance plan
Possibilities to enrol in Company's pension plan
Exciting company activities including monthly lunches, corporate gatherings, an intercompany football team, competitions, and many other activities
Casual dress code
A chance to advance professionally inside one of the world's largest iGaming organisations


What Life at SkillOnNet is like!

SkillOnNet is a firm believer in putting people first and our “family oriented” multinational culture is what drives us. We care and focus on our staff and ensure that you are provided with the most relevant and valuable tools, privileges and amenities.

Title: Data Analyst
Description:
Who We Are

Ovoko is transforming Europe’s €30B+ used car parts market - a massive, fragmented industry still largely offline. We connect 6,000+ scrapyard owners, car parts sellers and dismantlers with millions of buyers through our marketplace, SaaS and logistics platform, making it effortless to trade parts across borders. With over 35 million parts listed, we’ve grown into one of Europe’s fastest-scaling e-commerce companies, but the real opportunity lies ahead, as we build a real category-defining platform worldwide.

About The Role

Fuelled by a recent $20M Series B funding round and consistent triple-digit year-over-year growth, Ovoko is continuing to scale its Data & Analytics function. Over the past year, our Data Org has focused on building strong foundations - modernising data architecture, tooling, and ways of working. Now, the focus is shifting towards driving real business impact through data.

To support this next phase, we are expanding our Data Team and looking for a Data Analyst to join us. This role will be part of a growing team working closely with multiple product and business areas across Ovoko: Product, Money, Business, Marketing and more. The exact scope may evolve depending on your strengths and interests - from product analytics to business operations or other domains.

If you're curious, proactive, and excited about solving real business problems with data - this role is a great opportunity to grow and make an impact in one of Europe’s fastest-growing e-commerce startups.

IN THIS ROLE, YOU WILL


Define KPIs, identify business opportunities, and estimate their impact using data insights.
Build dashboards, implement simple ETL, and utilize ML techniques for complex solutions.
Design and analyze experiments (A/B tests).
Use advanced analytics and ML techniques for complex data solutions.
Work with modern tooling such as Snowflake, Looker, Mixpanel, and explore opportunities in the evolving AI landscape.


What We’re Looking For


Experience working with SQL and BI tools (Power BI, Tableau, Looker, etc.).
Ability to translate data into clear insights and business recommendations.
Strong analytical thinking and problem-solving mindset.
Good data visualisation and story-telling skills.
Business-driven mindset.
Good communication skills and ability to work with different stakeholders.
Fluent English is required.


SALARY

The offered salary for this position starts at 3000€ (gross) per month. Please keep in mind that we are open to discuss your salary expectations based on your competencies and experience.

What We Offer


Private health insurance
Employee stock option plan (ESOP)
Learning budget for your personal and professional growth
Commuting perks (covered parking or public transport to choose from)
Everyday snacks and drinks
Onsite gym
Top-notch hardware and software (MacOS or Windows to choose from)
The opportunity to work from anywhere up to 30 days per year
Employee referral program


Discover all our perks by visiting our website: https://about.ovoko.com/career/#perks

Title: DATA ANALYST (F/M/D) | IGNITIS RENEWABLES
Description:
Do You want to be part of „Ignitis Group“?

We are looking for a Data Analyst to join the Operations and Maintenance department at Ignitis Renewables. You will play a key role in quantifying asset performance and identifying trends to ensure responsible ownership. You’ll collaborate closely with Asset Supervisors, using Python and SQL to transform data into reliable dashboards and statistical analyses. If that speaks to you, we’d be happy to welcome you to our team!

You will contribute to green and secure energy ecosystem creation by:



Using data to solve complex problems and find ways to improve asset performance, even when working with uncertain information
Writing and maintaining the code and tools (using Python, SQL) that keep our data workflows organized and reliable
Monitoring operational data to identify trends, detect anomalies, and highlight potential issues or opportunities related to asset performance
Contributing to the team’s roadmap and analytical priorities by providing insights that support operational decision-making and long-term performance optimization
Presenting analytical findings clearly through dashboards, reports, and discussions with stakeholders to support data-informed decisions
Documenting analytical workflows, datasets, dashboards, and coding standards to ensure transparency, reproducibility, and knowledge sharing within the team



Required for this position:



A degree in a technical field such as data analytics, engineering, mathematics, or a related discipline, or equivalent experience working in a data-driven environment
2–5 years of experience in data analytics, delivering dashboards, reports, and analyses that support operational or business decision-making
Strong proficiency in SQL and Python for data cleaning, statistical analysis, and extracting insights from complex datasets
Practical experience with version control tools such as Git and GitLab to maintain organized, reproducible, and auditable code
Experience building and maintaining Power BI dashboards connected to SQL databases to support operational and ad-hoc reporting
Familiarity with collaboration tools such as Jira, Confluence, and Microsoft Teams for coordinating work and documenting analytical outputs
High proficiency in English to clearly communicate analytical findings, write reports, and collaborate with international stakeholders
Ability to work independently on recurring analytical tasks while applying statistical reasoning and creativity to solve new or complex problems



In return for creating a more sustainable future, we promise:



To take part in an unfolding energy transformation process to create a 100% green and secure energy ecosystem for both current and future generations
International team that is working on dynamic renewable energy projects
A hybrid work model (3+2) and flexible working hours
Extensive learning and qualification enhancement programs
Attention to physical and emotional health: wellness mentor initiatives and psychological support etc.
Additional paid days for health improvement, short-term illness, volunteering, and accumulated work experience



Join a growing and increasingly international team of energy smart people united by a common purpose. We all play our part in creating a 100% green and secure energy ecosystem for current and future generations.
