Title: Supply Chain AI & Automation Analyst
Description:
Over 50,000 customers globally trust our end-to-end, cloud-driven networking solutions and rely on our top-rated services and support to accelerate their digital transformation efforts and deliver progress like never before and with double digit growth year over year, no provider is better positioned to deliver better outcomes on scale, than Extreme.

Come become part of something big with us! We are a global leader, with hubs in Europe, North America, South America, Asia Pacific, and the Middle East.

Supply Chain AI & Automation Analyst

About The Role

We are seeking a “Supply Chain AI & Automation Analyst” to join our Extreme Networks Global Operations organization, where you'll turn complex operational data into clear, actionable insight that drives decisions and automation across our Global Supply Chan and Order Management teams. This role sits at the intersection of analytics, AI, and supply chain strategy, partnering with cross-functional teams to identify opportunities, build analytical products, and tell the stories behind the numbers. The ideal candidate is a curious, technically strong analyst who is equally comfortable writing SQL, using AI tools, framing a business problem, managing a project, and presenting findings to stakeholders. You should be excited about the rapid evolution of AI and agentic workflows and how they can be applied to supply chain decision-making and you should be actively using AI tools in your work today.

A Note On Experience

This role requires commercially delivered analytical work. We will ask you at interview to walk through a specific project from problem through to business outcome that you owned and delivered in a professional setting. Academic or course-based projects do not qualify. If your experience is primarily from an academic programme, this role is not the right fit at this time

Key Responsibilities


Partner with supply chain stakeholders to scope problems and translate them into structured, measurable, automated workstreams.
Build and maintain python/SQL/Snowflake-based data models, dashboards, and self-service analytics for our global operations teams
Help operationalize AI and agent-based solutions to automate recurring analyses, augment decision support, and supply chain processes.
Apply statistical methods to inform data driven decisions across our global operations function.
Manage your projects end-to-end: scoping, stakeholder alignment, data discovery, modelling, validation, and rollout.
Translate analytical findings into compelling narratives (written, visual, and verbal) tailored to operational, business, and technical audiences.
Define and track KPIs that measure supply chain performance and flag interventions when metrics drift.
Contribute to ongoing improvements in data quality, documentation, and analytical standards across the team.


Required Skills & Qualifications


3-5 years experience in business analytics, data analytics, or a comparable quantitative role.
Demonstrated use of AI tools such as Claude, MS Copilot, or ChatGPT – in an analytical workflow, with a genuine appetite to develop agentic and automation capabilities in role.
Strong python skills and hands-on experience with Snowflake (or a comparable cloud data warehouse such as BigQuery, Redshift, or Databricks).
Demonstrated project management ability, owning deliverables across multiple stakeholders and timelines.
Solid grounding in descriptive statistics, and analytics fundamentals.
Strong data storytelling skills: the ability to distil complexity into clear visuals and narratives that drive action.
Supply chain domain knowledge: familiarity with demand and supply planning, inventory optimization, order management, logistics, procurement, or manufacturing operations.
Bachelor's degree in a quantitative field (e.g., Statistics, Operations Research, Engineering, Physics, Economics, Data Science, Supply Chain, Mathematics, or related).


Technical Skills Required


Programming for analytics: Python or R for modelling, automation, and analytics workflows.
BI and visualization tools: Tableau, Sigma, Power BI, Looker, or similar — both for building and for design literacy.
ERP / source systems: Exposure to Oracle Fusion Cloud or similar transactional systems.


Preferred / Additional Skills: These are skills that would meaningfully strengthen a candidate for this role:


Practical experience applying agentic workflows (e.g., LLM-based assistants, retrieval-augmented analytics, automated reasoning agents) to analytical or operational problems.
Master's or PhD in a quantitative field (Statistics, Operations Research, Engineering, Physics, Economics, Data Science, Supply Chain, Mathematics, or related).
Forecasting and optimization: time-series methods as well as other optimization techniques.
Machine learning fundamentals: classification, regression, clustering, and an understanding of when ML is and isn't the right tool.
Process improvement methodologies: Lean, Six Sigma, or equivalent structured problem-solving frameworks.
Stakeholder management and influencing: navigating ambiguity, managing competing priorities, and building trust with non-technical partners.


What Success Looks Like


Within 3 months: deeply understand our supply chain data landscape, key stakeholders, and top-priority business questions.
Within 6 months: deliver at least one high-impact analytical product or AI-enabled workflow that changes how a decision is made.
Within 12 months: become a trusted analytics partner to supply chain stakeholders and a contributor to the broader analytics team's standards and practices.


Salary Is Based On Qualifications And Experience Between 60-70K

Extreme Networks Benefits

We may use artificial intelligence (AI) tools to support parts of the hiring process, such as reviewing applications, analyzing resumes, or assessing responses. These tools assist our recruitment team but do not replace human judgment. Final hiring decisions are ultimately made by humans. If you would like more information about how your data is processed, please contact us.

Title: Data Analyst - Internal Audit
Description:
Company Description

Wise is a global technology company, building the best way to move and manage the world’s money.

Min fees. Max ease. Full speed.

Whether people and businesses are sending money to another country, spending abroad, or making and receiving international payments, Wise is on a mission to make their lives easier and save them money.

As part of our team, you will be helping us create an entirely new network for the world's money.

For everyone, everywhere.

Job Description

More about our mission and what we offer.

We are looking for a Data Analyst to join the Internal Audit branch of the Compliance & Internal Audit Analytics team at Wise.

You’ll partner with Internal Audit (IA) to develop and implement the analytics strategy across the function.

Internal Audit serves as an independent 3rd line of defence that oversees and provides an independent assessment and reasonable assurance on the effectiveness of the company’s corporate governance structure + internal controls.

The IA Analytics team supports IA by using analytics to provide more coverage, efficiency and accuracy to audit testing, as well as building innovative tools and using automation to support continuous testing and other IA activities beyond the annual audit plan.

This is an IC2 role. For more information on our Analytics Career Map and levelling structure, click here.

What You’ll Be Doing


Partnering with stakeholders across the IA function covering all regions, products and domains of the business (including Financial Crime, Ops, Risk, Finance and Technology)
Identifying & implementing opportunities to do the most impactful analytics within audits and influencing and upskilling your partners in IA to do the same
Working with analytics, engineering, and business specialists to seek out and understand interesting and varied datasets, perform analytics using modern approaches, and develop a strong understanding of operations at Wise
Championing innovation and data-led insights, not just within audits but across all IA activities where analytics can add value
Working on automation and tooling projects to increase efficiency across IA including workflow automation, continuous auditing, leveraging AI, and building MI dashboards


Qualifications

What you’ll bring:


3+ years of experience in data analytics
1+ years of data analytics experience within an audit, risk, or compliance related role
Advanced stakeholder management skills; you know how to leverage multiple different teams to deliver high-impact change including analytics, engineering, and business specialists
Extensive experience using SQL; you can build and maintain complex data sources and can leverage visualisation tools to surface insights


Nice To Have


Experience of working in regulated environments, Fintech or Financial Services.
Knowledge of financial crime.
Experience using dbt, Superset, or Looker.
Knowledge of advanced analytics techniques, including the use of Python and visualisation techniques.


Additional Information

For everyone, everywhere. We're people building money without borders — without judgement or prejudice, too. We believe teams are strongest when they are diverse, equitable and inclusive.

We're proud to have a truly international team, and we celebrate our differences.

Inclusive teams help us live our values and make sure every Wiser feels respected, empowered to contribute towards our mission and able to progress in their careers.

If you want to find out more about what it's like to work at Wise visit Wise.Jobs.

Keep up to date with life at Wise by following us on LinkedIn and Instagram.

Title: Data Analyst - Process Management (12 Month FTC) | PGIM Ireland
Description:
12 Month FTC

Hybrid Role (2 days on-site in Letterkenny)

PGIM Ireland are seeking to hire a Data Analyst - Process Management for PGIM Real Estate Loan Services who are responsible for the servicing and asset management of commercial loans originated by PGIM Real Estate (PGIM RE) outside of the United States. Irish hours will apply from Monday to Friday, however there is a requirement to be flexible, specifically during peak times. Hours are subject to change in line with role requirements.

What You Can Expect


Cultivate both internal and external relationships to ensure proper service levels are achieved for each portfolio function
Reporting to internal and external stakeholders based on multiple SLA’s
Compilation & delivery of data across multiple platforms
Proactively identify & communicate trends/issues to relevant stakeholders
Lead and manage special projects as assigned, as well as propose and drive process enhancements and automation
Collaborate with multiple areas of PGIM RE to ensure the highest level of service
Servicing/Cash Management – Calculation of debt service, delivery of billing letters
Surveillance – Review of compliance certificates, calculations to confirm covenant adherence, review and analysis of property monitoring reports
Banking Admin - Managing multiple international bank and borrower relationships
Treasury – cash movements for multiple
Anticipate issues that may adversely affect portfolio performance and take action to reduce risks
Review policies and procedures and ensure consistency across the regions


Skills & Experience


Bachelor’s Degree required
Experience in database design and maintenance
Proficiency in programming languages such as SQL, Python, or R
Strong background in Data Analytics and/or Data Science
Ability to analyse complex datasets and identify underlying drivers of performance
Experience managing multiple responsibilities within tight and challenging deadlines
Advanced skills in Microsoft Office, particularly Excel
Experience with data visualization tools such as Power BI or Tableau (desirable)
Hands-on experience with automation and low-code platforms, including: Microsoft Power Automate, Microsoft Power Apps, Microsoft Fabric (Data Engineering, Data Warehousing, and Analytics)
Strong organizational, analytical, and problem-solving skills
Excellent interpersonal skills with the ability to collaborate effectively across teams and stakeholders
Self-motivated, proactive, detail-oriented, and highly organized, with strong time management capabilities
Excellent verbal and written communication skills
Knowledge of or interest in the financial services industry is desirable
PGIM welcomes all applicants, even if you don't meet every requirement. If your skills align with the role, we encourage you to apply.


What We Offer You


Health Insurance: PGIM Ireland partner with Laya and BUPA to provide health insurance schemes that cover eligible employees day to day medical and hospital expenses.
Annual Leave of 23 days at full pay.
Pension Scheme: Members of the scheme can contribute up to 8% of salary per annum and PGIM Ireland matches contributions up to 8% of salary. Members can also make voluntary contributions to the scheme.
Annual Bonus Programme & Shop LK Vouchers / CleverCards (subject to eligibility): Along with an annual bonus employees are rewarded with Shop LK Vouchers/ CleverCards which are paid tax free.
Life Assurance: fully paid by PGIM Ireland, employees are covered from their start date and beneficiaries are provided with a lump sum of four times an employee’s salary.
Education Assistance: PGIM Ireland have an Education Assistance Programme that reimburses eligible employees for furthering their education.


-

PGIM Ireland is proud to be an equal opportunity employer and is committed to equal employment opportunity regardless of applicants’ gender, civil status, family status, sexual orientation, religion, age, disability, race or membership of the traveler community. PGIM Ireland's aim is to hire the best people for the open roles and all appointments will be made on merit.

Any offer of employment made by PGIM Ireland will be contingent on receiving satisfactory references. Applicants should be aware that background checks will be carried out on all candidates offered a position within PGIM Ireland.

Title: Duomenų analitikas (-ė)
Description:
„IKI“ – tai vieta, kur žmonės, nepriklausomai nuo to, ar dirba biure, parduotuvėse, gamyboje ar logistikoje, aistrą pirmauti verčia ambicingais pasiekimais! Pirmieji Lietuvoje pristatėme prancūziškus kruasanus ir bagetes, atidarėme autonominę parduotuvę, įdiegėme savitarnos kasas ir elektronines kainų etiketes. Šie žingsniai atspindi mūsų nuolatinį siekį būti žingsniu priekyje ir kurti ateities mažmeninę prekybą – inovatyvią bei tvarią. Jei ieškai dinamiškos, idėjoms atviros aplinkos, kurioje galėtum augti ir kurti naujoves, gali būti, kad darbas „IKI“ yra arčiau tavęs!

Šiame darbe tu:


Analizuosi duomenis įvairiuose projektuose (dalyvavimas projektų komandose, reikalingų duomenų išgavimas ir apdorojimas) bei verslo atvejų skaičiavimuose;
Dalyvausi rodiklių (KPI) stebėsenoje (nustatyti kasdienės veiklos ir projektų įgyvendinimo KPI rodiklius bei užtikrinti jų matavimą);
Teiksi pagalbą biudžeto sudarymo ir kontrolės procesuose;
Dalyvausi tiekimo grandinės parametrų nustatyme siekiant optimizuoti visuminius tiekimo grandinės kaštus.


Tikimės, kad tu:


Turi aukštąjį universitetinį išsilavinimą kiekybinėje srityje, tokioje kaip matematika, informatikos mokslai, programų inžinerija, ekonomika, fizika ar panašioje srityje.;
Turi 2+ metų darbo patirties duomenų analizės srityje;
Turi gerus MS Excel ir darbo su PowerBI įgūdžius;
Gali pasigirti stipriais analitiniais ir loginio mąstymo gebėjimais;
Turi programavimo kalbų įgūdžių (SQL, Python ir kt.) – būtų privalumas.


„IKI“ pasiūlymas tau:

Atlyginimas ir finansinė gerovė


atlyginimas: 2200–3400 €/mėn. (neatskaičius mokesčių, priklausomai nuo tavo patirties ir įgūdžių),
galėsi pigiau apsipirkti „IKI“ prekybos centruose,
turėsi nuolaidas „IKI“ asortimento naujienoms,
daugiau nei 300 MELP ir partnerių nuolaidų kone visiems gyvenimo atvejams.  


Darbo aplinka ir lankstumas


hibridinis darbo modelis,
lankstumas pasirenkant darbo valandas,
įkvėpimui – darbostogos iki 30 d. per metus.


Karjera ir tobulėjimas


karjeros galimybės (tiek Lietuvoje, tiek užsienyje),
profesinio tobulėjimo galimybės (tiek su kolegomis, tiek ir individualiai).


Sveikata ir emocinė gerovė


papildomas sveikatos draudimas, kurį galėsi rinktis net iš 3 planų,
nemokamos sveikatos patikros biure,
dalyvavimas „Walk15“, vidinėse badmintono, dviračių, žygių mėgėjų sporto grupėse,
streso valdymo ir kiti emocinės gerovės mokymai, galimybė gauti anonimines psichologo konsultacijas telefonu.     


Stipri bendruomenė


stiprybėmis grįsta kultūra, kurioje vertinamos visų darbuotojų unikalios savybės ir pasiekimai,
kolegas pagal laisvalaikio veiklas kviečiame burtis į „Hobby Spotlight” klubus,
darbuotojų fondas „VeIKIme drauge“,
esame aktyvūs bendruomenės nariai – savanoriaudami ir finansine parama prisidedame prie „Maisto banko“ ir kitų organizacijų.  


Inovacijos ir dinamiškumas


modernūs darbo įrankiai ir programos,
inovatyvūs sprendimai prekyboje, tvarumo ir kitose srityse,
mūsų komandos projektus ir pasiekimus kviečiame pažinti čia: Tvarumas | IKI. Arčiau Tavęs!

„IKI“ – tai vieta, kur žmonės, nepriklausomai nuo to, ar dirba biure, parduotuvėse, gamyboje ar logistikoje, aistrą pirmauti verčia ambicingais pasiekimais! Pirmieji Lietuvoje pristatėme prancūziškus kruasanus ir bagetes, atidarėme autonominę parduotuvę, įdiegėme savitarnos kasas ir elektronines kainų etiketes. Šie žingsniai atspindi mūsų nuolatinį siekį būti žingsniu priekyje ir kurti ateities mažmeninę prekybą – inovatyvią bei tvarią. Jei ieškai dinamiškos, idėjoms atviros aplinkos, kurioje galėtum augti ir kurti naujoves, gali būti, kad darbas „IKI“ yra arčiau tavęs!

Šiame darbe tu:

  • Analizuosi duomenis įvairiuose projektuose (dalyvavimas projektų komandose, reikalingų duomenų išgavimas ir apdorojimas) bei verslo atvejų skaičiavimuose;
  • Dalyvausi rodiklių (KPI) stebėsenoje (nustatyti kasdienės veiklos ir projektų įgyvendinimo KPI rodiklius bei užtikrinti jų matavimą);
  • Teiksi pagalbą biudžeto sudarymo ir kontrolės procesuose;
  • Dalyvausi tiekimo grandinės parametrų nustatyme siekiant optimizuoti visuminius tiekimo grandinės kaštus.

Tikimės, kad tu:

  • Turi aukštąjį universitetinį išsilavinimą kiekybinėje srityje, tokioje kaip matematika, informatikos mokslai, programų inžinerija, ekonomika, fizika ar panašioje srityje.;
  • Turi 2+ metų darbo patirties duomenų analizės srityje;
  • Turi gerus MS Excel ir darbo su PowerBI įgūdžius;
  • Gali pasigirti stipriais analitiniais ir loginio mąstymo gebėjimais;
  • Turi programavimo kalbų įgūdžių (SQL, Python ir kt.) – būtų privalumas.

„IKI“ pasiūlymas tau:

Atlyginimas ir finansinė gerovė

  • atlyginimas: 2200–3400 €/mėn. (neatskaičius mokesčių, priklausomai nuo tavo patirties ir įgūdžių),

  • turėsi nuolaidas „IKI“ asortimento naujienoms,
  • daugiau nei 300 MELP ir partnerių nuolaidų kone visiems gyvenimo atvejams.  

Darbo aplinka ir lankstumas

  • hibridinis darbo modelis,
  • lankstumas pasirenkant darbo valandas,
  • įkvėpimui – darbostogos iki 30 d. per metus.

Karjera ir tobulėjimas

  • karjeros galimybės (tiek Lietuvoje, tiek užsienyje),
  • profesinio tobulėjimo galimybės (tiek su kolegomis, tiek ir individualiai).

Sveikata ir emocinė gerovė

  • papildomas sveikatos draudimas, kurį galėsi rinktis net iš 3 planų,
  • nemokamos sveikatos patikros biure,
  • dalyvavimas „Walk15“, vidinėse badmintono, dviračių, žygių mėgėjų sporto grupėse,
  • streso valdymo ir kiti emocinės gerovės mokymai, galimybė gauti anonimines psichologo konsultacijas telefonu.

Title: Analyst (Financial and Market Statistics)
Description:
About The Department

The Financial Stability function is responsible for identifying and assessing risks that may threaten the stability of the financial system and the broader economy. This includes analysing systemic risks and evaluating how developments in the domestic economy, the financial services sector, and the external environment may influence the resilience of the financial system. The function is composed of two complementary teams. The Financial Stability Team focuses on the assessment and monitoring of systemic and sector-specific risks through the use of sensitivity analysis, stress testing and forward-looking risk assessments. This work is complemented by macroprudential research across the banking, insurance, and securities sectors, as well as broader areas such as cyber, climate, geopolitical and concentration risks. It also contributes to the design and calibration of macroprudential policies, assessing their impact and effectiveness. The Intelligence and Research Team is responsible for the function’s statistical capabilities, to carry out quantitative and qualitative research and analysis to assess the national and global economic environment and financial markets, structural shifts as well as market practices that may influence the evolution of the financial services sector. The team also evaluates the impact of these developments, including any measures and initiatives proposed to address them.

About The Role

As an Analyst (Financial and Market Statistics) within the Intelligence and Research team you will be assigned to carry out analytical work that includes the following duties:


Conducting analysis of economic, financial and market developments and trends, both local and international, and assessing their potential impact on the evolution of the financial services industry;
Collecting, organising and interpreting data related to cross-border investment flows, market behaviour and business trends to support the Authority’s strategic initiatives;
Designing and conducting surveys and data collection exercises to support research, regulatory, and policy development needs;
Performing routine and ad-hoc data analysis, including financial modelling and preparation of datasets for internal use or external reporting;
Developing, implementing, and maintaining Power BI dashboards to monitor developments and trends within the financial services sector, ensuring data accuracy, accessibility, and timely updates;
Drafting analytical reports and technical papers, both for internal and publication purposes;
Participating in and contributing to technical meetings within the MFSA as well as at national and international levels;
Carrying out any other related duties as may be assigned by line management or senior leadership in line with the function’s remit and/or the Authority’s exigencies.
Undertake any other duties as assigned by the Head and/or as may be required by the MFSA from time to time based on the exigencies of the Authority.


About You

We are looking for candidates with strong academic background in statistics, mathematics, banking and finance, economics or other relevant studies at National Qualification Framework Level 6 or better duly certified by the Malta Qualifications Recognition Information Centre (MQRIC) or a widely recognised professional qualification at a comparable level, and a minimum of three (3) years experience in a related area.

Candidates with experience of working with business intelligence tools, such as MS PowerBI, and who have knowledge of programming languages and data analysis packages, such as R, Python or SQL, will be considered as an asset.

If you do not have the necessary academic or professional qualifications but you have at least seven (7) years of relevant experience in a similar role, we would still be interested in speaking with you.

The selected candidate is expected to be a confident individual who feels at ease working in a multidisciplinary environment, is flexible, proactive, dynamic, has the ability to prioritise tasks, has a critical and analytical mind-set and a high attention to detail even when under time pressure. The individual is also required to demonstrate good research and have excellent oral, written and presentation skills.

Other Important Information

The MFSA is an Equal Opportunities Employer as certified by the NCPE (National Commission for the Protection of Equality) and is committed to a policy of equal opportunity in all aspects of employment and will take care to avoid any form of discrimination in its recruitment procedures. The MFSA reserves the right to withdraw this call at any time and not to select any of the Candidates.

It is the responsibility of applicants in possession of qualifications awarded by Universities and other similar institutions outside Malta to produce a recognition statement on comparability of qualifications issued by the Malta Qualifications Recognition Information Centre (MQRIC). Applicants should do so preferably at application stage or otherwise at the preliminary interview should an applicant be selected for such interview. Details can be obtained by accessing the National Commission for Further and Higher Education website on www.ncfhe.gov.mt under MQRIC heading.

The MFSA shall ensure that any processing of personal data is in accordance with Regulation (EU) 2016/679 (General Data Protection Regulation), the Data Protection Act (Chapter 586 of the Laws of Malta) and any other relevant European Union and national law. For further details, you may refer to the Data Protection Policy on the MFSA webpage www.mfsa.mt. Candidates are to note that the submission of any false statement/s or omission, even if unintended, may lead to the cancellation of their application and may render the candidate's appointment liable to termination.

Furthermore, please note that candidates may be asked to submit any documentation in support of the information provided, including but not limited to, proof of qualifications and Police Conduct Certificates.

Title: Product Data Analyst
HTML:
Working at Omnisend is... kind of boring. Not the soul-crushing kind of boring — more like the “no drama, no chaos, just smart people doing great work” kind. The kind that lets you focus on what matters, without pointless meetings, micromanagement, or bureaucracy. We’re data-driven. We test, we iterate, we improve. Gut feelings don’t drive decisions — insights do. We trust each other. We’re transparent, give real feedback, and actually listen. No egos, no hand-holding — just a team of experienced professionals moving fast, adapting easily, and delivering impact. Omnisend is customer-funded and customer-driven. Our work is rooted in solving real problems that help ecommerce brands grow. No empty promises — we bring in results. And yes, we have fun. But the grown-up kind — workations, team gatherings, the occasional party. No forced fun, no awkward icebreakers. Just genuine moments with a team that actually enjoys working together.

We are looking for a Product Data Analyst to join our Data Ops team. In this position, you will collaborate closely with product teams. You will look for the biggest business opportunities, explain user behavior, raise and test hypotheses, use advanced statistical methods and predictive models, forecast future trends, and perform deep-dive analysis. You will not work on small ad-hoc data requests, we expect that our data driven product managers will take care of this, while you will be taking care of the advanced statistics. We are looking for an independent and experienced professional who would contribute to a data driven approach in the company. Interpreting data and deducting actionable insights should be your main competence, with a rich record of successful high-impact projects over your career.

Some tools we use: BigQuery, Superset, dbt, Hightouch, Mixpanel, Dagster, Python and Airbyte.

What you’ll do @ Omnisend:


  • Build predictive data models using advanced statistical methods;
  • Provide actionable insights from data;
  • Identify business opportunities and estimate their impact;
  • Lead data-driven decision-making in a group of stakeholders;
  • Build dashboards;
  • Coach on best practices in data analysis to foster a data-driven culture.


We expect that you’ll bring:


  • Highly skilled in data visualization and story-telling;
  • Ability to translate complex data into clear business insights;
  • Experience building predictive models using advanced statistical methods;
  • Experience in Superset, Power BI, Looker, Tableau, or similar visualization tools;
  • Working experience with SQL;
  • Business-driven mindset;
  • Good knowledge of statistical analysis;
  • Education in statistics, mathematics, econometrics, economics, or related fields;
  • Fluency in English.


Snapshot of your impact, by the 3-month mark:


  • Complete Omnisend new-hire onboarding and job-specific training for the Marketing Data Analyst role;
  • Gain clarity on expectation of your role and gain understanding of your team’s processes;
  • Identify needs for your TEAM and ROLE and build out your OKRs for the quarter;
  • Fully onboard on using team’s tools like Airbyte, dbt, Hightouch, Dagster and Superset;
  • Get familiar with all product teams;
  • Gain clarity on Product team’s processes;
  • Start building the foundation of strong relationships with Omnisend teammates.


What we offer in return:


  • Gross salary starting point from 4200 EUR/month based on experience;
  • Quarterly salary reviews;
  • An unlimited learning budget for self-improvement complying with the best interest of Omnisend (after 3 months);
  • Working methods and best practices inspired by the best tech companies in Silicon Valley;
  • Senior colleagues enable us to have a high-performing, productive environment where your growth will accelerate;
  • Flexible working hours and remote work possibilities;
  • Private health insurance;
  • Unlimited access to psychotherapy;
  • Workstation budget (420 Eur);
  • Work anniversary gifts like house cleaning services, bikes, or scooters;
  • Attractive workations and team-led team-building events.


Omnisend is an email and SMS marketing platform built for ecommerce brands. With one-click integration to major ecommerce platforms, intuitive automation, and powerful email templates, we make sophisticated marketing effortless — no learning curve required.

Award-winning 24/7/365 customer support? Check. A platform trusted by growing brands and seasoned pros alike? Absolutely. The idea that “great marketing needs to be expensive”? Our 150,000+ customers prove that wrong every day.

We may use artificial intelligence (AI) tools to support parts of the hiring process, such as reviewing applications, analyzing resumes, or assessing responses. These tools assist our recruitment team but do not replace human judgment. Final hiring decisions are ultimately made by humans. If you would like more information about how your data is processed, please contact us.

Title: Staff Data Analyst
Description:
About Analog Devices

Analog Devices, Inc. (NASDAQ: ADI ) is a global semiconductor leader that bridges the physical and digital worlds to enable breakthroughs at the Intelligent Edge. ADI combines analog, digital, and software technologies into solutions that help drive advancements in digitized factories, mobility, and digital healthcare, combat climate change, and reliably connect humans and the world. With revenue of more than $9 billion in FY24 and approximately 24,000 people globally, ADI ensures today's innovators stay Ahead of What's Possible™. Learn more at www.analog.com and on LinkedIn and Twitter (X).

Staff Data Analyst, Engineering Analytics

Location: Limerick, Ireland

Overview

As a Staff Data Analyst on the Engineering Enablement team, you will own the development and evolution of analytics dashboards that surface insights on product development and engineering tool usage. These dashboards synthesize large amounts of engineering data from various sources and are broadly used by both engineering teams and executive leadership. You will rely on sound judgment, curiosity, and strong communication skills to translate ambiguous engineering workflows into well-defined metrics and clear, meaningful reports.

Responsibilities


Engage directly with engineering domain teams (design, verification, IP, AI etc.) to understand workflows, identify high-value data sources, and define key telemetry and productivity metrics.
Define data schemas, grain, and transformation logic needed for each data source, and communicate those requirements clearly to the Central Data team for implementation.
Design and maintain a Power BI semantic model (data model, relationships, DAX measures) that serves as a shared analytical foundation across multiple thin reports.
Build, publish, and iterate on Power BI dashboards and reports that deliver clear insights to engineering managers and leadership.
Collaborate with Central Data team to scope, prioritize, and validate pipeline work; serve as the functional owner of data correctness and metric definitions.
Document metric definitions, data lineage, and report logic to ensure institutional knowledge is durable and auditable.
Identify opportunities to expand impact — new data sources, new engineering personas, new use cases — and drive those through requirements definition and delivery.


Qualifications


8+ years of experience in data analytics, business intelligence, or a related field.
Degree in Computer Science, Electrical Engineering, Business Analytics, Statistics, or a related quantitative field.
Expert-level skills in Power BI or similar tool (Qlikview, Tableau, DataStudio, etc.), including data modeling, DAX, report design, workspace/deployment pipeline management.
Strong SQL skills; ability to write and review transformation logic even when not directly authoring pipelines.
Demonstrated experience working directly with non-technical stakeholders to define metrics and translate domain knowledge into data requirements.
Strong written and verbal communication skills; comfortable presenting findings and influencing priorities with engineering and executive leadership.
Experience owning an analytics product end-to-end — from requirements through delivery and ongoing maintenance.


Additional Qualifications (nice To Have)


Familiarity with consuming data from Snowflake, dbt, or similar modern data stack components.
Certifications in Power BI or related Microsoft data tools.
Experience with Python for data validation, exploration, or lightweight transformation.
Experience in the semiconductor industry or familiarity with EDA tools and flows.


For positions requiring access to technical data, Analog Devices, Inc. may have to obtain export licensing approval from the U.S. Department of Commerce - Bureau of Industry and Security and/or the U.S. Department of State - Directorate of Defense Trade Controls. As such, applicants for this position – except US Citizens, US Permanent Residents, and protected individuals as defined by 8 U.S.C. 1324b(a)(3) – may have to go through an export licensing review process.

Analog Devices is an equal opportunity employer. We foster a culture where everyone has an opportunity to succeed regardless of their race, color, religion, age, ancestry, national origin, social or ethnic origin, sex, sexual orientation, gender, gender identity, gender expression, marital status, pregnancy, parental status, disability, medical condition, genetic information, military or veteran status, union membership, and political affiliation, or any other legally protected group.

Job Req Type: Experienced

Required Travel: Yes, 10% of the time

Shift Type: 1st Shift/Days

Title: Data Lead (Growth & Analytics)
Description:
Mid-senior position | Part-time or Full-time | On-site @Ljubljana




About the role

We're building a real-world social platform. Matched meetups that get people out of their homes and turn strangers into friends. It works. Now we need someone to build the data infrastructure that helps us scale it.




As our Data Lead (Growth & Analytics) you'll own everything data: event tracking, pipelines, the dashboards that drive our decisions. If a metric matters to the business, you built the system that surfaces it.




We're an AI-first team - that includes BI. We're building AI-powered dashboards and we want someone who finds that exciting and has done this in the past.




You'll be one of our first 10 team members. Early enough to shape how we instrument, what we trust, and how decisions get made. If you've spent years in a SaaS or subscription business and you intuitively know which numbers lie and which ones matter, you'll fit right in.




It's fast. It's sometimes messy. The problems are real and the impact is direct.




You’ll be a great fit if you:

Work best without permission
Default to "how could I systemize this"
Had weird teenage hobbies
Spend your Sundays on side projects
Shipped something this week
Are good at chess, poker or Monopoly
Forget to grab lunch because you’re in the zone
Get the best ideas while showering
Have left a job because the pace was too slow
Are more motivated by winning than by being right
Find bureaucracy physically painful




What you’ll be doing

Data infrastructure

Own data architecture decisions and tooling selection across marketing, revenue, finance, product, and operations
Set up and maintain a data warehouse
Set up experimentation tooling
Ensure data accuracy, freshness, and consistency
Implement data validation, anomaly detection, and monitoring

Tracking

Build attribution tracking for Meta Ads, Google Ads, email and other channels
Measure reliable data at every step of the acquisition funnel
Diagnose and resolve tracking discrepancies across platforms
Work with other teams to implement and QA tracking changes

Data analysis

Translate data into clear, actionable recommendations for leadership
Build forecasting models for revenue, churn, and growth scenarios
Identify leading indicators and growth constraints
Support strategic decisions around pricing, roadmap, and scale strategy
Implement cohort dashboards, churn prediction tools, and LTV/CAC reporting
Segmentation by geographies, niches, channels, etc
User feedback analysis
Operational efficiency analysis

Experimentation

Set success metrics and guardrails for experiments
Run A/B tests on landing pages, onboarding funnels, pricing pages, upsells, etc
Evaluating A/B tests and suggesting new ones

Product & business insights

Analyze user behavior across onboarding, activation, engagement, and retention
Identify friction points and drop-offs in core user journeys
Partner with product, marketing, and ops to drive improvements




Requirements

Essential:

Fluent in English (written and spoken)
At least 3 years of relevant experience
Experience working with a subscription/SaaS business (familiar with metrics such as MRR, churn, cash conversion cycle,...)
Comfortable working with SQL, data modeling, BI, retention/LTV analysis, attribution, and forecasting.
AI-first mindset - ability to heavily rely on AI tools and build in such a way that we can keep leveraging AI in the future
Strong revenue strategy mindset: understands monetisation, pricing, payback logic, and CAC/LTV trade-offs.
Comfortable working in ambiguity, adapting to changes and wearing multiple hats
Ability to identify ideal software and integrate it into the company’s workflow
Attention to detail and the ability to work independently without constant oversight
A lot of drive, energy, and time.
Ability to work from our office in Ljubljana city center

Bonus points:

Previous startup experience (especially early-stage)
Experience building teams from scratch
You are based close to Ljubljana (or willing to move)




About us

We help people meet new friends through real-life events all around the world.




We’re building something ambitious. Small team. Global startup. Serious brains. Real responsibility. Fast growth. Mobile app / B2C SaaS.




We are past product-market fit and are now in fast-scaling mode.




If you want to ship fast, work hard, own systems end-to-end, and grow with the company - this is for you.




Founded by Jani Pravdič and Matic Jesenovec – entrepreneurs who've together built multiple businesses impacting hundreds of thousands of people and generating well over eight figures in revenue. We're now building something that we believe has the potential to grow much larger and have a greater impact than anything we’ve done before.




What’s in it for you

Working closely with the founders
Working in a high-performance team
It takes us hours instead of weeks to launch/implement something
We test things and we listen to the numbers, not who is the loudest in the room
Failure is part of the process; we accept it and learn from it
Opportunity to grow into a higher role
The chance to genuinely impact many thousands of lives globally
A front-row seat in an exciting, fast-growing start-up
Competitive salary
Performance bonuses




Our values

Strategic Boldness: We do what it takes to deliver faster, farther, and louder.
Agility > Perfection: We value action over polish, effectiveness over effort. Ship fast, refine later.
Scrappy & Curious: We test, learn, and adapt constantly. Resourceful beats resource-rich.
Ambition + Humility: We learn from the best, then push beyond. Inspiration fuels our innovation.
Long Game Thinking: We’re impatient with action, patient with results. Correct quickly, stay the course.




Next steps

We’re looking for someone to start as soon as possible. So if the role sounds exciting, please follow these steps:

Fill out the application form.
Our team carefully reviews every application, so the selection process may take some time.
The best candidates will receive an additional test assignment.
Those who perform best on this task will be invited to an extensive interview.




If you do not hear from us within 14 days of submitting your application, it means we have decided to continue the process with other candidates at this time.

Title: Business Intelligence Analyst
Description:
We’re looking for a data-driven and curious Business Intelligence Analyst to help turn complex data into actionable insights that support smarter business decisions. In this role, you’ll work closely with stakeholders across the business to identify trends, improve performance, optimize processes, and support strategic initiatives through analytics and reporting.

You’ll play a key role in helping teams leverage data effectively while contributing to the continuous improvement of our BI environment, reporting infrastructure, and data governance practices.




What You’ll Do

Transform business data into actionable insights and recommendations
Support teams in identifying opportunities for growth, optimization, and operational improvement
Create, maintain, and improve BI reporting solutions and dashboards
Develop clear and impactful data visualizations to communicate findings effectively
Analyze complex datasets from multiple sources to uncover patterns, trends, and potential risks
Perform behavioral analysis and support A/B testing initiatives
Build forecasts and provide analytical support for new projects and business initiatives
Help maintain and improve data governance standards and BI best practices
Support data warehouse access layers and collaborate closely with other data-focused teams
Contribute to the administration and maintenance of enterprise reporting tools, including MicroStrategy and Tableau
Support and monitor BI microservices and troubleshoot BI-related technical issues
Provide training and support to users to encourage self-service analytics adoption




What We’re Looking For

2+ years of experience in a Business Intelligence, Data Analyst, or similar analytical role
Strong analytical mindset with excellent attention to detail and accuracy
Solid SQL skills and understanding of relational databases
Good understanding of ETL processes and data workflows
Experience working with data visualization and reporting tools
Familiarity with MicroStrategy and Tableau administration and maintenance is considered a strong advantage
Ability to interpret complex data and present findings clearly to non-technical stakeholders
Strong written and verbal communication skills in English
Ability to work independently in a fast-paced environment
Experience in online entertainment, gaming, or other high-volume digital industries is considered an advantage




What We Offer

Paid annual leave (as established by law)
Birthday day off
3 additional wellness days per year
Access to the Salu Digital Healthcare Program
Monthly sports compensation (€55/month via Stebby)
Learning & Development budget in line with company policy
Team event budget

Title: Product Data Analyst
Description:
About Phorest

Would you like to create the future of a product used by thousands of businesses and millions of consumers? Phorest Salon Software powers over 11,000 hair and beauty salons in Ireland, UK, Germany, Australia, Finland, USA, Canada, UAE and Netherlands and the platform processes 7 million appointments a month for hair and beauty consumers. Phorest is the number 1 salon software brand for premium independent salons in those markets and our next goal is to become a platform upon which other companies can build applications for premium salons.

Our system touches every point of the salon experience. We have the in-salon software desktop application, Phorest Go (the native staff application), online bookings website for salons, and custom native apps per salon. In a typical month, Phorest processes 3 million appointments and we send over 3 million SMS and 4 million emails. We process 200k online bookings per month and over 150 custom built white label native apps.

The Opportunity

As a Product Data Analyst at Phorest, you’ll be embedded at the heart of our product development process, turning data into clear, actionable insights that shape how we build and improve our software. Working closely with Product Managers, Designers, and Engineers, you’ll help the team understand user behaviour, measure the impact of product changes, and identify opportunities to deliver more value to salons and their clients.

You’ll own product analytics end-to-end — from defining metrics and building dashboards to conducting deep-dive analyses and presenting findings to stakeholders. This is a role for someone who is genuinely curious about how products are used, comfortable working in a fast-moving agile environment, and confident translating complex data into decisions.

What You'll Do


Partner with Product Managers and teams to define success metrics, instrument features, and measure the impact of product changes and releases.
Build and maintain Looker dashboards and explores that surface product usage, engagement, retention, and funnel metrics to stakeholders across the business.
Conduct deep-dive analyses on user behaviour, feature adoption, and churn signals to surface actionable insights that inform product roadmap decisions.
Define and maintain a product metrics framework, ensuring consistency in how KPIs are measured and reported across teams.
Support A/B testing and experimentation, helping teams design experiments, interpret results, and make statistically sound decisions.
Collaborate with Data Engineering to ensure the right events and data points are captured, pipelines are reliable, and the data model supports product analytics needs.
Proactively monitor product metrics, flagging anomalies, trends, and opportunities to the relevant teams.
Contribute to data literacy across product teams, coaching team members on self-serve analytics, dashboard use, and metric interpretation.
Support ad hoc analytical requests from product and leadership, delivering timely and rigorous analysis.


Who You Are


3+ years of experience in data analytics, ideally with some of that time in a product analytics context or working directly alongside a product team.
Strong proficiency in SQL, with the ability to write complex queries across large datasets.
Hands-on experience with Looker (LookML, dashboards, explores) — a core requirement for this role.
Solid understanding of the product development lifecycle and how data fits into agile ways of working.
Strong data visualisation and communication skills — you can tell a clear story with data for both technical and non-technical audiences.
Analytical mindset with strong attention to detail and a rigorous approach to data accuracy.
Self-starter who can manage competing priorities and work independently in a fast-moving environment.


The following are preferred but not required:


Experience in the hair & beauty industry or a vertical SaaS business serving SMEs.
Experience with Python or R for more advanced analysis.


Benefits

🧘 Your wellbeing is important to us - we provide private healthcare, 2 Wellness Days, an employee assistance program and a free online GP service.

💰 As part of our Financial Wellbeing, we provide competitive Compensation, an Employee Share Purchase Scheme, Pension, Life Assurance, and Income Protection.

🚵🏿 We help you travel by providing a bike to work scheme as well as tax saver transport tickets.

🦸‍♀️ We support the women who work in Phorest by offering 2 weeks leave for Fertility Treatment, Pregnancy Loss and Menopause.

🍼 We care for your family and provide Enhanced Maternity and Paternity Benefits.

🌳 We grow our own timber! We provide a great learning environment and extensive development opportunities. We run development programs and provide access to many online resources including LinkedIn learning.

🏠 Moving house? Phorest employees get 3 moving days.

Want to learn more about Phorest? Check out nothingventured.rocks for our blog and Insights on building an evergreen company from the team here at Phorest.

Phorest is an equal opportunity employer. For this position, flexi-time and working from home is possible. We are also open to remote work. Get in touch to ask for more information or to chat about your future with Phorest!

Research shows that while men apply to jobs when they meet an average of 60% of the criteria, women and other marginalised folks tend to only apply when they check every box. So if you think you have what it takes, but don't necessarily meet every single point on the job description, please still get in touch. We'd love to have a chat and see if you could be a great fit.

Title: Data Analyst
Description:
We are looking to hire a data analyst to join our data team. You will take responsibility for managing our master data set, developing reports, and troubleshooting data issues. To do well in this role you need a very fine eye for detail, experience as a data analyst, and a deep understanding of the popular data analysis tools and databases. Data Analyst Responsibilities: Managing master data, including creation, updates, and deletion. Managing users and user roles. Provide quality assurance of imported data, working with quality assurance analysts if necessary. Commissioning and decommissioning of data sets. Processing confidential data and information according to guidelines. Helping develop reports and analyses. Managing and designing the reporting environment, including data sources, security, and metadata. Supporting the data warehouse in identifying and revising reporting requirements. Supporting initiatives for data integrity and normalization. Assessing tests and implementing new or upgraded software and assisting with strategic decisions on new systems. Generating reports from single or multiple systems. Troubleshooting the reporting database environment and reports. Evaluating changes and updates to source production systems. Training end-users on new reports and dashboards. Providing technical expertise in data storage structures, data mining, and data cleansing. Data Analyst Requirements: Bachelor s degree from an accredited university or college in computer science. Work experience as a data analyst or in a related field. Ability to work with stakeholders to assess potential risks. Ability to analyze existing tools and databases and provide software solution recommendations. Ability to translate business requirements into nontechnical, lay terms. High-level experience in methodologies and processes for managing large-scale databases. Demonstrated experience in handling large data sets and relational databases. Understanding of addressing and metadata standards. High-level written and verbal communication skills.

Title: Data Analyst
Description:
Data Analyst

Boston VA Research Institute, Inc. (BVARI)




We are a Boston-based epidemiology and biomedical research organization. We are actively engaged in several clinical trials, pharmacoepidemiology, chronic disease epidemiology, genetic epidemiology, biostatistical and genomics research. Our research team oversees study data-related activities and quality improvement initiatives to improve the way translational research is conducted and clinical care is delivered. The multi-disciplinary center consists of a highly talented group of biomedical researchers, clinicians, epidemiologists, biostatisticians, data analysts/programmers, project coordinators, and informatics team with academic affiliations to Harvard Medical School and Boston University. We are looking for candidates with a service-oriented mentality, high sense of ownership of assigned tasks and the ability to solve complex data-related problems.




Project Background:

This role is involved in managing data aspects of large nationwide randomized clinical trials as well as integrating electronic health record (EHR) data with the Million Veteran Program (MVP) data to build cohorts for clinical and genetic research, enabling robust investigation of complex health outcomes in Veterans.




General Statement of Duties:

The Data Analyst will work with the study teams to gather study data requirements, pull all necessary data from electronic health records (EHR), and perform data cleaning, and aggregation/synthesis as appropriate. The Data Analyst will a) generate recruitment lists to the enrollment sites of national clinical trials and perform quality assurance on the data generated, b) develop reports as needed for the Principal Investigator and other project staff as needed, c) perform validation of programs and analytical databases to fulfill specifications and ensure integrity of outcomes, d) turn in assignments in a timely manner, meeting appropriate timelines, e) establish and maintain communication with customers to keep them updated on status of their requests.




Essential Duties and Responsibilities:




Mainly responsible for constructing, cleaning, validating, modifying, and analyzing biomedical research data
Query and extract large-scale EHR and MVP data using SQL
Generate reports and visualizations using tools such as R and Python to effectively communicate findings
Collaborate with biostatisticians, clinicians, epidemiologists, data management team and data analysts/programmers to design, create and deliver analytical databases, reports and analyses
Perform validation of programs and analytical databases to fulfill specifications and ensure integrity of outcomes
Establish and maintain communication with customers to keep them updated on status of their requests
Provide statistical expertise towards research projects and conduct statistical analysis using SAS or R
Proactively identify data issues, troubleshoot problems, and implement solutions with minimal supervision
Establish and maintain communication with study team to keep them updated on status of requests
Turn in assignments in a timely manner, meeting appropriate timelines.




QUALIFICATIONS AND ENTRANCE REQUIREMENTS:

Master’s degree in public health domain, statistics, biostatistics or epidemiology, data science, or a related field with 2+ years of statistical programming experience; OR Bachelor’s degree in a related field with 5+ years of programming experience.
Strong programming and analytical skills, with demonstrated proficiency in SQL and at least one statistical programming language (R, Python, or SAS)
Hands-on experience with Microsoft SQL Server, including working with relational datasets, managing and structuring large observational datasets, and writing efficient complex SQL queries for data extraction, creating cohorts, data transformation, and analysis.
Experience in data visualization and reporting using R or Python.
Basic knowledge of study design and research methodology.
Capable of working independently in writing analytic plans, performing analyses and writing reports to facilitate research decision-making.
Demonstrated ability to think algorithmically and approach problems with a strong coding and problem-solving mindset.
Understanding of the healthcare domain, clinical, epidemiologic and/or genomic data, and clinical trials research is a plus.
Natural Language Processing experience is a plus.
Excellent attention to detail, logic, and organizational skills.
Strong written and verbal communication skills.




The employee must be a resident of Massachusetts upon the start date.




Please note we are unable to provide work authorization and/or visa sponsorship.




Work Environment

This job operates in a professional office environment. This role routinely uses standard office equipment such as computers, phones, photocopiers, filing cabinets and fax machines.




Physical Demands

The physical demands described in this job description are representative of those that must be met by an employee to successfully perform the essential functions of this job.




While performing the duties of this job, the employee frequently is required to stand; walk; use hands; and reach with hands and arms.




Selective Service

If you are male application born after December 31, 1959, you must certify that you have registered with the Selective Service System or are exempt from having to do so under the Selective Service Law.




This position requires the ability to occasionally lift office products and supplies, up to 20 pounds.




Position Type/Expected Hours of Work

This is a full-time position, and hours of work and days are Monday through Friday, 8:30 a.m. to 5 p.m.




Travel

No travel is required for this position. Optional travel to scientific meetings may occur from time to time.




Work Authorization/Security Clearance

An employee in this position must complete all appropriate background checks at the time of hire and periodic reappointment.




Other Duties

Please note this job description is not designed to cover or contain a comprehensive listing of activities, duties or responsibilities that are required of the employee for this job. Duties, responsibilities, and activities may change at any time with or without notice.




Equal Opportunity Employer

Boston VA Research Institute provides equal employment opportunities to all employees and applicants for employment and prohibits discrimination and harassment of any type without regard to race, color, religion, age, sex, national origin, disability status, genetics, protected veteran status, sexual orientation, gender identity or expression, or any other characteristic protected by federal, state, or local laws. This policy applies to all terms and conditions of employment, including recruiting, hiring, placement, promotion, termination, layoff, transfer, leaves of absences, compensation, and training.




Pay Range

Minimum $70,000.00, Midpoint $80,000, Maximum $90,000.00

Salary is based on related experience, expertise, and internal equity; generally, new hires can expect pay between the minimum and midpoint of the range.

Title: SQL Data Analyst
Description:
Department: Finance

Employment Type: Permanent

Location: Burlington, Massachusetts

Compensation: $100,000 - $110,000 / year

Description

Highwire, a Veriforce company, is the Contractor Success Platform for asset owners, general contractors, and facilities managers. The world’s most admired organizations use Highwire to ensure their contractors and subcontractors deliver great work, on budget and on schedule, by collaborating with them to dynamically mitigate safety, financial, quality, and sustainability risks during capital projects and facilities maintenance.

To support our growth, we’re looking for a technically minded, detail-obsessed Data Analyst to own the integrity of our internal data. This role is perfect for someone who’s a SQL expert and eager to work directly with complex datasets.

Your mission is to ensure the data that powers Highwire — from customer renewal rates and campaign performance to financial reporting — is accurate, complete, and decision-ready. You’ll take ownership of the end-to-end accuracy of key datasets, using SQL to investigate anomalies, validate transformations, and deliver reliable data to teams across Product, Engineering, Finance, and other business functions. Your work will enable the entire company to move quickly and make informed decisions based on trustworthy data.

What You'll Do


Write and improve SQL queries that power reporting and analysis. You’ll support business teams by extracting and transforming data, building reusable queries, and collaborating with engineering to enhance data models and pipelines.
Deliver clean, trusted data. You’ll ensure that the data used across Finance, Product, Sales, and Engineering is accurate and reliable, enabling fast and confident decision-making and reducing rework and second-guessing.
Identify and fix root-cause issues. You’ll proactively identify bugs, inconsistencies, or oddities in the data that powers our reports, and collaborate with Engineering and Product to determine the root cause of the issue.
Enable real-time reporting. Department heads will rely on you for recurring and ad-hoc reports that are both timely and accurate. You’ll build reports that scale as our teams grow.
Continuously improve data quality. You won’t wait for others to flag data issues. Instead, you’ll take initiative to audit, investigate, and improve datasets before problems reach stakeholders.


Who You Are


SQL Expert. You’re comfortable querying relational databases, navigating schemas, writing joins, and thinking in data logic.
Detail-oriented. You notice inconsistencies that others miss and take pride in spotting the one broken row in a dataset of thousands.
Owner’s mindset. You feel a strong sense of responsibility for the quality of your work and the integrity of the data others rely on. If it’s broken, you’ll fix it.
Adaptable. You’re comfortable working with imperfect or incomplete data, and you adjust quickly to changing product requirements or business needs.
Analytical. You excel at analyzing large datasets, identifying patterns and anomalies, and transforming complex or ambiguous information into actionable insights.
Proactive collaborator. You don’t wait for someone else to raise a flag; you pull in the right people to get the right answer, fast.


This is an ideal role for someone who is confident in writing SQL to explore, transform, and troubleshoot data in a relational database. You might come from a background in data analysis, analytics engineering, operational support, or even Excel/Sheets-heavy reporting work, but the key is that you’ve used SQL hands-on, and you’re excited to deepen that skill and partner closely with leaders across our organization to ensure our data is accurate, accessible, and trustworthy.

Attention to detail is a critical competency for success in this Data Analyst role. To help us identify candidates who read thoroughly, you’ll see a mystery question during the application process. The correct answer is: attention to detail.

What You’ll Get In Return

Our benefits go well beyond the basics. To support your work-life integration, we built a benefits program that takes care of the whole person, including physical, mental, financial, and emotional health.


Medical, Dental, & Vision Insurance. We offer health, dental, and vision insurance to all full-time employees. Best part? You’ll be eligible on day one.
401k. We offer a 401k and a 3% company contribution (even better than a match) to help you get ready to retire no matter how far off that seems.
Parental leave. We take care of new parents by offering paid time off for births, adoptions, and fostering.
Paid Time Off. Outstanding time off benefits.
Mentorship. Our office-based culture fosters meaningful learning, growth, and mentorship for our team.

Title: Data Analyst
Description:
About Us

RTK Tickets is a dynamic and rapidly expanding company at the intersection of entertainment, technology, and trading. From buzzer-beaters and sold-out stadiums to front-row concerts, we provide a comprehensive platform for buying, selling, and optimizing access to live events — and increasingly, other high-demand assets.




We’re a fast-moving, data-driven brokerage redefining how markets are priced, traded, and optimized across entertainment and emerging asset classes. What began as a two-person idea has evolved into a 20-person team built on passion, precision, and performance.




Now, we’re looking for a Data Analyst to help us build and scale the data infrastructure and predictive models that power how we buy and sell tickets. If you’re excited by real-time markets, complex datasets, and turning raw data into measurable outcomes, you could be a great fit for our team.




What You’ll Do

As a Data Analyst at RTK Tickets, you’ll own the systems and models that drive our pricing decisions. You’ll design and maintain data pipelines that ingest market data from dozens of sources, build predictive models that forecast demand, and work closely with our risk and operations teams to turn insights into actions.

You’ll have end-to-end ownership; from raw data ingestion to production-ready models. Your work will directly influence how we price, buy, and sell across thousands of live events.







Your Day-to-Day

Design, build, and maintain data pipelines that ingest and process transaction and listing data from external marketplaces
Develop and refine predictive models that forecast demand, identify pricing inefficiencies, and optimize offers
Implement data quality frameworks and monitoring systems to ensure fresh, accurate, and reliable data
Build and manage data warehouse pipelines and modeling (ETL/ELT) for large-scale datasets
Collaborate with the risk and operations teams to translate model outputs into actionable decisions
Partner with engineers to integrate pipelines with CI/CD processes and automate data workflows
Present your findings clearly to both technical and non-technical teammates







What You Bring

Required:

1-3 years of experience in data science, data engineering, or a closely related analytical role
Strong Proficiency in Python - including experience with data processing libraries
Solid SQL skills for data analysis, transformation, and pipeline work
Bachelor’s degree in Computer Science, Data Science, Applied Mathematics, Statistics, or a related field
Comfortable working in a fast-paced, startup-like environment where you take ownership and adapt quickly
Clear communication skills — able to explain complex ideas simply and confidently
Excitement about AI, automation, and cutting-edge technology

Nice to Have:

Exposure to machine learning model deployment or MLOps practices
Interest in live event markets, trading markets, sports analytics, or alternative asset pricing
Experience with LLM-based automation tools for data extraction or enrichment







Why You’ll Love It Here

At RTK Tickets, you’ll gain hands-on experience at the intersection of data, product, and live entertainment. Your pipelines and models will directly influence how we price and trade across thousands of live events every week.

If you want to grow your career at the intersection of data, markets, and live entertainment, we’d love to hear from you.







Interview Process

Our interview process includes several stages designed to assess both technical skills and team fit. Candidates should be prepared to meet with the hiring team for introductory conversations, followed by a Python-based take-home exercise focused on data ingestion, pipeline design, and predictive modeling - mirroring the kind of end-to-end work this role owns. Candidates will walk through their solution with the team before final interviews with the hiring manager and senior leadership.




Compensation & Benefits

Competitive salary with opportunity for performance-based bonuses
Health and Dental insurance
401(k) retirement plan with company matching
Tickets to select live events

  • Develop and refine predictive models that forecast demand, identify pricing inefficiencies, and optimize offers
  • Implement data quality frameworks and monitoring systems to ensure fresh, accurate, and reliable data
  • Build and manage data warehouse pipelines and modeling (ETL/ELT) for large-scale datasets
  • Collaborate with the risk and operations teams to translate model outputs into actionable decisions
  • Partner with engineers to integrate pipelines with CI/CD processes and automate data workflows
  • Present your findings clearly to both technical and non-technical teammates



What You Bring

Required:

  • 1-3 years of experience in data science, data engineering, or a closely related analytical role
  • Strong Proficiency in Python - including experience with data processing libraries
  • Solid SQL skills for data analysis, transformation, and pipeline work
  • Bachelor’s degree in Computer Science, Data Science, Applied Mathematics, Statistics, or a related field
  • Comfortable working in a fast-paced, startup-like environment where you take ownership and adapt quickly
  • Clear communication skills — able to explain complex ideas simply and confidently
  • Excitement about AI, automation, and cutting-edge technology

Nice to Have:

  • Exposure to machine learning model deployment or MLOps practices
  • Interest in live event markets, trading markets, sports analytics, or alternative asset pricing
  • Experience with LLM-based automation tools for data extraction or enrichment



Why You’ll Love It Here

At RTK Tickets, you’ll gain hands-on experience at the intersection of data, product, and live entertainment. Your pipelines and models will directly influence how we price and trade across thousands of live events every week.

If you want to grow your career at the intersection of data, markets, and live entertainment, we’d love to hear from you.



Interview Process

Our interview process includes several stages designed to assess both technical skills and team fit. Candidates should be prepared to meet with the hiring team for introductory conversations, followed by a Python-based take-home exercise focused on data ingestion, pipeline design, and predictive modeling - mirroring the kind of end-to-end work this role owns. Candidates will walk through their solution with the team before final interviews with the hiring manager and senior leadership.


Compensation & Benefits

  • Competitive salary with opportunity for performance-based bonuses
  • Health and Dental insurance
  • 401(k) retirement plan with company matching
  • Tickets to select live events

Boston Celtics Job Posting




 




Job Title: Business Intelligence & Operations Intern




Department: Business Intelligence & Operations




Supervisor: VP, Business Strategy and Operations

Summary:




 




The mission of the Boston Celtics internship program is to educate its participants in all aspects of operating a professional sports organization. Assist in the development of SQL scripts used to build Tableau server data sources and validate data accuracy

Field inbound fan questions related to tickets and troubleshoot technical issues


Support various fan engagement programs and solving in-person ticketing issues at the TD Garden Box Office during home games


Research and present best practices relating to business analytics and operations in the sports industry


Other collaborative duties as assigned by the Director of Business Intelligence













Qualifications: 













Strong critical thinking, a growth mindset, an deductive problem-solving


Detail-oriented and thoughtful approaches to ensure accuracy and understanding of projects


Interest in developing ideas and projects from the ground up and being a self-starter


Enthusiasm to understand data, database management, and reporting and analytics


Ability to communicate and work with multiple departments and senior management













Learning Opportunities:













How to build data visualizations in Tableau, including how to tell stories and how to use data to drive insights to key stakeholders


Writing effective and efficient SQL scripts used to pull, validate, and manipulate data


A macro-level understanding of the role data plays in an organization, including how data flows out of and into systems used for ticketing, CRM, and email marketing


The ins and outs of a CRM system that manages hundreds of thousands of customer records


Foundation training and regular use of Ticketmaster’s Archtics ticketing system, the most commonly used ticketing system in professional sports













Requirements:













Must be a junior or senior undergraduate student or graduate student


Academic Credit


Ability to work scheduled home games and events

Title: Data Analyst Intern
Description:
Ascensus is the leading independent technology and service platform powering savings plans across America, providing products and expertise that help nearly 16 million people save for a better today and tomorrow.

Are you a student looking to make extra money this summer while gaining valuable work experience? Ascensus is the leading independent technology and service platform powering savings plans across America, providing products and expertise that help nearly 16 million people save for a better today and tomorrow. Our internship program offers practical corporate work experience that could lead to full-time work after graduation. We are seeking students going into their senior year, graduating in the spring of 2027.

The shift is from 9 am to 5 pm, 40 hours per week, starting in June and ending in August. This is a hybrid position in Newton, MA that pays $20 per hour.

The Data Analyst Intern will support the Retirement Operations team by helping transform data into meaningful insights that inform business decisions. This internship is designed to provide hands‑on experience working with real‑world datasets, analytics tools, and business partners while building foundational skills in data analysis, reporting, and data storytelling. The intern will work closely with experienced analysts and stakeholders and receive mentorship throughout the program.

Key Responsibilities


Assist in collecting, cleaning, and validating data from internal and external sources to ensure data quality and accuracy
Support analysis of datasets to identify trends, patterns, and opportunities for operational or business improvement
Help build and maintain dashboards, reports, and visualizations used by leadership and cross‑functional teams
Partner with analysts and business stakeholders to understand data needs and help define metrics and KPIs
Contribute to the development and documentation of data models, data dictionaries, and standard reporting definitions
Perform ad hoc analyses to support business questions and ongoing initiatives
Assist with tracking performance metrics and supporting regular reporting cycles
Collaborate with data engineering, IT, and analytics teams to learn how data pipelines and infrastructure are managed
Follow established data governance, privacy, and security standards


Skills & Qualifications

Required:


Currently pursuing a Bachelor’s degree in Data Analytics, Statistics, Mathematics, Computer Science, Economics, or a related field (or recent graduate)
Strong interest in data analysis, analytics, or business intelligence
Exposure to SQL or relational databases through coursework, projects, or internships
Familiarity with Excel (basic to intermediate formulas, pivot tables, data analysis concepts)
Ability to analyze data, think critically, and solve problems
Strong attention to detail and willingness to learn
Ability to communicate findings clearly to both technical and non‑technical audiences


Preferred:


Exposure to data visualization tools such as Power BI, Tableau, or Alteryx
Coursework or project experience using Python or R for data analysis
Familiarity with basic data modeling or data warehousing concepts
Interest in financial services, retirement plans, or a highly metrics‑driven industry
Experience working on academic, personal, or team data projects


Core Competencies


Analytical and quantitative thinking
Curiosity and eagerness to learn
Data visualization and storytelling fundamentals
Collaboration and communication skills
Time management and ability to balance multiple priorities
Growth mindset and openness to feedback


Be aware of employment fraud. All email communications from Ascensus or its hiring managers originate from @ascensus.com or @futureplan.com email addresses. We will never ask you for payment or require you to purchase any equipment. If you are suspicious or unsure about validity of a job posting, we strongly encourage you to apply directly through our website.

Title: Data Analyst
Description:
Our client is seeking an experienced data analyst to support our Digital Transformation program under the Workday Data workstream.

This is engagement is focused on data analysis, cleansing, profiling, and definition work.

We are looking for someone with a blend of hands-on data skills, strong business acumen, and the ability to engage effectively with cross-functional stakeholders across IT, Data, and Business teams.

Location: Boston area - ability to get to the Boston office when needed.




Keys to this role:

Perform data analysis, cleansing, and mapping across source systems to support rationalization and data cleansing efforts
Conduct data quality analysis on transactional and master data to identify critical data improvement opportunities
Facilitate working sessions with business and technical stakeholders to inform data analysis work
Support content creation for data governance documentation and working group meetings
Propose and apply approved AI tools and methodologies to accelerate data conversion and analysis efforts

Must Haves:

Strong business analysis and data analysis capabilities
Demonstrated experience with data quality, cleansing, mapping, and governance processes
Advanced SQL skills
Proficiency with Google Big Query and Looker preferred
Comfortable working with AI tools (ChatGPT, Gemini, and/or Claude) to perform traditional data analysis responsibilities
Comfort operating in a collaborative, cross-functional environment with both technical and non-technical audiences

Strong Nice to Haves:

Prior experience with Workday Finance and HCM modules
Experience working with financial data; chart of accounts, trial balances, and p&l data preferred
